proverb_native: Les princes se servent des hommes comme le laboureur des abeilles
proverb_en: Princes use men as the husbandman uses bees.

proverb_native: Les princes tiennent toujours leurs comptes, ils ne perdent jamais rien
proverb_en: Princes keep good reckoning, they never lose anything.

proverb_native: L’esprit qu’on veut avoir gâte celui qu’on a
proverb_en: The wit one wants spoils what one has.

proverb_native: Les raisonnements bannissent la raison
proverb_en: Reasonings banish reason.

proverb_native: Les rois ont les mains longues
proverb_en: Kings have long hands.

proverb_native: Les tonneaux vides sont ceux qui font le plus de bruit
proverb_en: Empty casks make the most noise.

proverb_native: Leurs chiens ne chassent point ensemble
proverb_en: Their dogs don’t hunt in couples.

proverb_native: Le ventre emporte la tête
proverb_en: The belly overrules the head.

proverb_native: Lever à cinq, disner à neuf, souper à cinq, coucher à neuf, font vivre d’ans nonante-neuf
proverb_en: To rise at five, dine at nine, sup at five, go to bed at nine, makes a man live to ninety-nine.

proverb_native: Lever à six, manger à dix, souper à six, coucher à dix, font vivre l’homme dix fois dix
proverb_en: To rise at six, eat at ten, sup at six, go to bed at ten, makes a man live years ten times ten.

proverb_native: Le vilain ne sait ce qu’éperons valent
proverb_en: The churl knows not the worth of spurs.

proverb_native: Le vin donné aux ouvriers est le plus cher vendu
proverb_en: The wine given to your workmen is that for which you get the best paid.

proverb_native: Le vin ne porte point de chausses
proverb_en: Wine wears no breeches.

proverb_native: Le vrai n’est pas toujours vraisemblable
proverb_en: What is true is not always probable.

proverb_native: L’habit ne fait pas le moine
proverb_en: The gown does not make the monk.

proverb_native: L’homme est bien heureux qui a une belle femme auprès d’une abbaye
proverb_en: Happy is the man who has a handsome wife close to an abbey.

proverb_native: L’homme est de feu, la femme d’étoupe; le diable vient qui souffle
proverb_en: Man is fire, woman is tow, and the devil comes and blows.

proverb_native: L’homme n’a ni sens ni raison, qui jeune femme laisse au tison
proverb_en: The man has neither sense nor reason who leaves a young wife at home.

proverb_native: L’homme propose et Dieu dispose
proverb_en: Man proposes and God disposes.

proverb_native: L’hôte et le poisson en trois jours sont poison
proverb_en: A guest and a fish after three days are poison.

proverb_native: L’ignorance des hommes fait bouillir le pot aux prêtres
proverb_en: Men’s ignorance makes the pot boil for priests.

proverb_native: L’intention est réputée pour le fait
proverb_en: The will is taken for the deed.

proverb_native: L’occasion fait le larron
proverb_en: Opportunity makes the thief.

proverb_native: L’”oe]il du maître engraisse le cheval
proverb_en: The eye of the master fattens the steed.

proverb_native: Loin des yeux loin du c”oe]ur
proverb_en: Out of sight out of mind.

proverb_native: L’oiseau ne doit pas salir son nid
proverb_en: The bird ought not to soil its own nest.

proverb_native: Longue demeure fait changer ami
proverb_en: Long absence changes friends.

proverb_native: Longue langue, courte main
proverb_en: Long tongue, short hand.

proverb_native: Longues paroles font les jours courts
proverb_en: Long talk makes short days.

proverb_native: Maille à maille on fait le haubergeon
proverb_en: Link by link the coat of mail is made.

proverb_native: Maints sont bons parce qu’ils ne peuvent nuire
proverb_en: Many a one is good because he can do no mischief.

proverb_native: Maison faite et femme à faire
proverb_en: A house ready made and a wife to make.

proverb_native: Mal d’autrui n’est que songe
proverb_en: Another’s misfortune is only a dream.

proverb_native: Mal soupe qui tout dîne
proverb_en: He sups ill who eats up all at dinner.

proverb_native: Mal sur mal n’est pas santé
proverb_en: Misfortune upon misfortune is not wholesome.

proverb_native: Marchand d’oignons se connaît en ciboules
proverb_en: A dealer in onions is a good judge of scallions.

proverb_native: Marchand qui perd ne peut rire
proverb_en: The merchant that loses cannot laugh.

proverb_native: Mariage d’épervier: la femelle vaut mieux que le mâle
proverb_en: A hawk’s marriage: the hen is the better bird.

proverb_native: Marie ton fils quand tu voudras, ta fille quand tu pourras
proverb_en: Marry your son when you please, your daughter when you can.

proverb_native: Mari sourd et femme aveugle font toujours bon ménage
proverb_en: A deaf husband and a blind wife are always a happy couple.

proverb_native: Mauvais c”oe]ur et bon estomac
proverb_en: A bad heart and a good stomach.

proverb_native: Mauvaise herbe croît toujours
proverb_en: Ill weeds grow apace.

proverb_native: Mauvais ouvrier ne trouvera jamais bon outil
proverb_en: A bad workman never finds a good tool.

proverb_native: Méchant chien, court lien
proverb_en: A wicked dog must be tied short.

proverb_native: Méchant poulain peut devenir bon cheval
proverb_en: A ragged colt may make a good horse.

proverb_native: Mère piteuse fait sa fille rogneuse
proverb_en: A tender-hearted mother makes a scabby daughter.

proverb_native: Mets ton manteau comme vient le vent
proverb_en: Arrange your cloak as the wind blows.

proverb_native: Mettre la charrue devant les b”oe]ufs
proverb_en: To put the plough before the oxen.

proverb_native: Mieux nourri qu’instruit
proverb_en: Better fed than taught.

proverb_native: Mieux vaut assez que trop
proverb_en: Enough is better than too much.

proverb_native: Mieux vaut avoir ami en voye qu’or ou argent en corroye
proverb_en: Better to have a friend on the road than gold or silver in your purse.

proverb_native: Mieux vaut bon repas que bel habit
proverb_en: Better a good dinner than a fine coat.

proverb_native: Mieux vaut couard que trop hardi
proverb_en: Better be a coward than foolhardy.

proverb_native: Mieux vaut engin que force
proverb_en: Contrivance is better than force.

proverb_native: Mieux vaut être tête de chien que queue de lion
proverb_en: Better be the head of a dog than the tail of a lion.

proverb_native: Mieux vaut faire envie que pitié
proverb_en: Better be envied than pitied.

proverb_native: Mieux vaut glisser du pied que de la langue
proverb_en: Better a slip of the foot than of the tongue.

proverb_native: Mieux vaut marcher devant une poule que derrière un b”oe]uf
proverb_en: Better walk before a hen than behind an ox.

proverb_native: Mieux vaut perdre la laine que la brebis
proverb_en: Better lose the wool than the sheep.

proverb_native: Mieux vaut plein poing de bonne vie que ne faict sept muys de clergie
proverb_en: A handful of good life is better than seven bushels of learning.

proverb_native: Mieux vaut plier que rompre
proverb_en: Better bend than break.

proverb_native: Mieux vaut règle que rente
proverb_en: Thrift is better than an annuity.

proverb_native: Mieux vaut tard que jamais
proverb_en: Better late than never.

proverb_native: Mieux vaut terre gâtée que terre perdue
proverb_en: Better a ruined than a lost land.

proverb_native: Mieux vaut une once de fortune qu’une livre de sagesse
proverb_en: An ounce of luck is worth a pound of wisdom.

proverb_native: Mieux vaut un pied que deux échasses
proverb_en: One foot is better than two stilts.

proverb_native: Mieux vaut un tiens que deux tu l’auras
proverb_en: One “take this” is worth more than two “you shall have.”

proverb_native: Moineau en main vaut mieux que pigeon qui vole
proverb_en: A sparrow in the hand is better than a pigeon on the wing.

proverb_native: Moine qui demande pour Dieu demande pour deux
proverb_en: The monk that begs for God’s sake begs for two.

proverb_native: Moins vaut rage que courage
proverb_en: Rage avails less than courage.

proverb_native: Moitié figue, moitié raisin
proverb_en: Half figs, half raisins.

proverb_native: Montre-moi un menteur je te montrerai un larron
proverb_en: Show me a liar and I’ll show you a thief.

proverb_native: Montrer le soleil avec un flambeau
proverb_en: To show the sun with a torch.

proverb_native: Morceau avalé n’a plus de goût
proverb_en: There is no flavour in a swallowed morsel.

proverb_native: Morte la bête, mort le venin
proverb_en: The beast dead, the venom is dead.

proverb_native: Mot à mot on fait les gros livres
proverb_en: Word by word the big books are made.

proverb_native: Muraille blanche papier de fou
proverb_en: A white wall is the fool’s paper.

proverb_native: Nager entre deux eaux
proverb_en: To swim between two waters.

proverb_native: Nage toujours et ne t’y fie
proverb_en: Swim on and don’t trust.

proverb_native: N’a pas fait qui commence
proverb_en: He has not done who is beginning.

proverb_native: Nécessité est mère d’invention
proverb_en: Necessity is the mother of invention.

proverb_native: Nécessité n’a pas de loi
proverb_en: Necessity has no law.

proverb_native: Ne crachez pas dans le puits, vous pouvez en boire l’eau
proverb_en: Spit not in the well, you may have to drink its water.

proverb_native: Ne croire à Dieu que sur bons gages
proverb_en: Trust not to God but upon good security.

proverb_native: Ne fais pas un four de ton bonnet ni de ton ventre un jardinet
proverb_en: Don’t make an oven of your cap or a garden of your belly.

proverb_native: Ne mets ton doigt en anneau trop étroit
proverb_en: Don’t put your finger into too tight a ring.

proverb_native: Ne meurs cheval, herbe te vient
proverb_en: Horse, don’t die yet, grass is coming.

proverb_native: Ne prêtez point votre argent à un grand seigneur
proverb_en: Do not lend your money to a great man.

proverb_native: Ne reprens ce que n’entens
proverb_en: Don’t find fault with what you don’t understand.

proverb_native: Ne sont pas tous chasseurs qui sonnent du cor
proverb_en: All are not hunters who blow the horn.

proverb_native: Ne touchez point à l’argent d’autrui, car le plus honnête homme n’y ajouta jamais rien
proverb_en: Touch not another man’s money, for the most honest never added to it.

proverb_native: N’éveille pas le chat qui dort
proverb_en: Wake not a sleeping cat.

proverb_native: Noblesse oblige
proverb_en: Nobility imposes obligations.

proverb_native: Noire geline pond blanc ”oe]uf
proverb_en: A black hen lays a white egg.

proverb_native: Nous verrons, dit l’aveugle
proverb_en: We shall see, as the blind man said.

proverb_native: Nul feu sans fumée
proverb_en: No fire without smoke.

proverb_native: Nul n’aura bon marché s’il ne le demande
proverb_en: No one will get a bargain he does not ask for.

proverb_native: Nul n’est prophète dans son pays
proverb_en: No man is a prophet in his own country.

proverb_native: Nul n’est si large que celui qui n’a rien à donner
proverb_en: No one is so liberal as he who has nothing to give.

proverb_native: Nul vent ne fait pour lui qui n’a point de port destiné
proverb_en: No wind can do him good who steers for no port.

proverb_native: Oignez vilain il vous poindra, poignez vilain il vous oindra
proverb_en: Anoint a villain and he will prick you, prick a villain and he will anoint you.

proverb_native: Oiseau débonnaire de lui-même se fait
proverb_en: The gentle hawk mans herself.

proverb_native: On a beau mener le b”oe]uf à l’eau s’il n’a soif
proverb_en: It is in vain to lead the ox to the water if he is not thirsty.

proverb_native: On achète tout fors le jour et la nuit
proverb_en: Everything may be bought except day and night.

proverb_native: On a plus de mal à se damner qu’à se sauver
proverb_en: People take more pains to be damned than to be saved.

proverb_native: On apprend en faillant
proverb_en: One learns by failing.

proverb_native: On a souvent besoin de plus petit que soi
proverb_en: One often has need of a lesser than oneself.

proverb_native: On a tant crié Noël qu’à la fin il est venu
proverb_en: Christmas has been talked of so long that it has come at last.

proverb_native: On a toujours assez de force pour supporter le malheur de ses amis
proverb_en: One has always strength enough to bear the misfortunes of one’s friends.

proverb_native: On compte les défauts de ceux qu’on attend
proverb_en: People count up the faults of those who keep them waiting.

proverb_native: On connaît l’ami au besoin
proverb_en: A friend is known in time of need.

proverb_native: Oncques mâtin n’aima levrier
proverb_en: Mastiff never liked greyhound.

proverb_native: Oncques souhait n’emplit le sac
proverb_en: Wishes never filled the bag.

proverb_native: Oncques vieil singe ne fit belle moue
proverb_en: An old ape never made a pretty grimace.

proverb_native: On dit est un sot
proverb_en: ”They say” is a fool.

proverb_native: On fait dire aux cloches tout ce qu’on veut
proverb_en: People make the bells say what they please.

proverb_native: On fait le loup plus grand qu’il n’est
proverb_en: The wolf is not so big as people make him.

proverb_native: On ferait un bien gros livre de tous les peut-être qui se disent en un jour
proverb_en: It would be a very big book that contained all the maybes uttered in a day.

proverb_native: On frotte tant le fer qu’à la fin il s’échauffe
proverb_en: Iron may be rubbed so long that it gets heated.

proverb_native: On lie bien le sac avant qu’il soit plein
proverb_en: A sack is best tied before it is full.

proverb_native: On n’a jamais bon marché d’une mauvaise marchandise
proverb_en: Bad ware is never cheap.

proverb_native: On n’a jamais de marchandise que pour son argent
proverb_en: One never gets more than one’s money’s worth of anything.

proverb_native: On n’a jamais vu chèvre morte de faim
proverb_en: No one ever saw a goat dead of hunger.

proverb_native: On n’a rien pour rien
proverb_en: Nothing is had for nothing.

proverb_native: On ne connaît point le vin aux cercles
proverb_en: The wine is not known by the hoops.

proverb_native: On ne doit pas à gras pourceau le cul oindre
proverb_en: There’s no need to grease the fat pig’s rump.

proverb_native: On ne doit pas laisser bonne terre pour mauvais seigneur
proverb_en: Good land should not be quitted for a bad landlord.

proverb_native: On ne fait pas de rien grasse porée
proverb_en: Fat broth cannot be made of nothing.

proverb_native: On ne jette des pierres qu’à l’arbre chargé de fruits
proverb_en: It is only at the tree loaded with fruit that people throw stones.

proverb_native: On ne peut contenter tout le monde et son père
proverb_en: One cannot please everybody and one’s father.

proverb_native: On ne peut faire qu’en faisant
proverb_en: To do, one must be doing.

proverb_native: On ne peut pas empêcher le vent de venter
proverb_en: One can’t hinder the wind from blowing.

proverb_native: On ne peut pas être en même temps au four et au moulin
proverb_en: One cannot be at the oven and the mill at the same time.

proverb_native: On ne peut pas être et avoir été
proverb_en: One cannot be and have been.

proverb_native: On ne peut sonner les cloches et aller à la procession
proverb_en: One cannot ring the bells and walk in the procession.

proverb_native: On ne prend pas le lièvre au son du tambour
proverb_en: Hares are not caught by the sound of the drum.

proverb_native: On ne prête qu’aux riches
proverb_en: People lend only to the rich.

proverb_native: On ne sait pas pour qui on amasse
proverb_en: One knows not for whom he gathers.

proverb_native: On ne saurait faire boire un âne s’il n’a pas soif
proverb_en: You cannot make an ass drink if he is not thirsty.

proverb_native: On ne saurait faire d’une buse un épervier
proverb_en: You cannot make a hawk of a buzzard.

proverb_native: On ne saurait tirer de l’huile d’un mur
proverb_en: You cannot get oil out of a wall.

proverb_native: On n’est jamais crotté que par la boue
proverb_en: One is never soiled but by filth.

proverb_native: On n’est jamais si riche que quand on déménage
proverb_en: One is never so rich as when one removes (from one house to another).

proverb_native: On ne va jamais si loin que lorsqu’on ne sait pas où l’on va
proverb_en: One never goes so far as when one doesn’t know whither one is going.

proverb_native: On pêche bien en eau troublé
proverb_en: It is good fishing in troubled waters.

proverb_native: On peut payer l’or trop cher
proverb_en: One may buy gold too dear.

proverb_native: On prend plus de mouches avec du miel qu’avec du vinaigre
proverb_en: Flies are easier caught with honey than with vinegar.

proverb_native: On prend son bien où on le trouve
proverb_en: A man takes his own wherever he finds it.

proverb_native: On revient toujours à ses premières amours
proverb_en: One always returns to one’s first love.

proverb_native: On se heurte toujours où l’on a mal
proverb_en: One always knocks oneself in the sore place.

proverb_native: On se soûle bien de manger tartes
proverb_en: One may tire of eating tarts.

proverb_native: On touche toujours sur le cheval qui tire
proverb_en: The horse that draws most is most whipped.

proverb_native: On trouve remède à tout fors à la mort
proverb_en: There is a remedy for everything but death.

proverb_native: On va bien loin depuis qu’on est las
proverb_en: One may go a long way after one is tired.

proverb_native: On voit plus de vieux ivrognes que de vieux médecins
proverb_en: There are more old drunkards than old doctors.

proverb_native: Or est qui or vaut
proverb_en: That is gold which is worth gold.

proverb_native: Ote-toi de là que je m’y mette
proverb_en: Get out of that place and let me take it.

proverb_native: Où il est faible le fil se rompt
proverb_en: Where the thread is weakest it breaks.

proverb_native: Où il n’y a point de mal il ne faut point d’emplâtre
proverb_en: Where there is no sore there needs no plaister.

proverb_native: Où il n’y a rien le roi perd ses droits
proverb_en: Where there is nothing the king loses his rights.

proverb_native: Où la chèvre est attachée il faut qu’elle broute
proverb_en: Where the goat is tied she must browse.

proverb_native: Où la guêpe a passé le moucheron demeure
proverb_en: Where the wasp has passed the fly sticks fast.

proverb_native: Où la haie est plus basse on saute dessus
proverb_en: Where the hedge is lowest men jump over.

proverb_native: Où l’hôtesse est belle le vin est bon
proverb_en: Where the hostess is handsome the wine is good.

proverb_native: Oy, voy, et te tay, si veux vivre en paix
proverb_en: Hear, see, and say nothing if you would live in peace.

proverb_native: Pain dérobé réveille l’appétit
proverb_en: Stolen bread stirs the appetite.

proverb_native: Pain tant qu’il dure, vin à mesure
proverb_en: Eat bread at pleasure, drink wine by measure.

proverb_native: Parens sans amis, amis sans pouvoir, pouvoir sans vouloir, vouloir sans effet, effet sans profit, profit sans vertu, ne vaut un fétu
proverb_en: Kindred without friends, friends without power, power without will, will without effect, effect without profit, profit without virtue, is not worth a rush.

proverb_native: Paris n’a pas été fait en un jour
proverb_en: Paris was not built in a day.

proverb_native: Parole jetée va partout à la volée
proverb_en: A word once out flies everywhere.

proverb_native: Partage de Montgomery: tout d’un côté, rien de l’autre
proverb_en: A Montgomery division: all on one side, nothing on the other.

proverb_native: Par trop débattre la vérité se perd
proverb_en: In too much disputing truth is lost.

proverb_native: Par trop presser l’anguille on la perd
proverb_en: To squeeze an eel too hard is the way to lose it.

proverb_native: Pas à pas on va bien loin
proverb_en: Fair and softly goes far.

proverb_native: Passez-moi la rhubarbe je vous passerai le séné
proverb_en: Give me the rhubarb and you may take the senna.

proverb_native: Pauvreté est une espèce de ladrerie
proverb_en: Poverty is a sort of leprosy.

proverb_native: Pauvreté n’est pas vice
proverb_en: Poverty is not a vice.

proverb_native: Péché caché est à demi pardonné
proverb_en: A sin concealed is half pardoned (meaning when care is taken to conceal the scandal).

proverb_native: Pendant que les chiens s’entre-grondent le loup dévore la brebis
proverb_en: Whilst the dogs are growling at each other the wolf devours the sheep.

proverb_native: Pense moult, parle peu, écris moins
proverb_en: Think much, say little, write less.

proverb_native: Petit à petit l’oiseau fait son nid
proverb_en: Little by little the bird builds its nest.

proverb_native: Petit chaudron, grandes oreilles
proverb_en: Little pitchers have long ears.

proverb_native: Petit dîner longuement attendu, n’est pas donné, mais chèrement vendu
proverb_en: A little dinner, long expected and cold, is by no means given, but dearly sold.

proverb_native: Petite brebiette toujours semble jeunette
proverb_en: A little sheep always seems young.

proverb_native: Petite chose aide souvent
proverb_en: A little thing often helps.

proverb_native: Petite étincelle luit en ténèbres
proverb_en: A little spark shines in the dark.

proverb_native: Petite pluie abat grand vent
proverb_en: A little rain stills a great wind.

proverb_native: Petit homme abat grand chêne
proverb_en: A little man fells a great oak.

proverb_native: Peu de levain aigrit grand’pâte
proverb_en: A little leaven leavens a great mass.

proverb_native: Pierre qui roule n’amasse point de mousse
proverb_en: A rolling stone gathers no moss.

proverb_native: Plumer l’oie sans la faire crier
proverb_en: To pluck the goose without making it cry out.

proverb_native: Plus fait douceur que violence
proverb_en: Gentleness does more than violence.

proverb_native: Plus le singe s’élève plus il montre son cul pelé
proverb_en: The higher the ape climbs the more he shows his rump.

proverb_native: Plus on est de fous, plus on rit
proverb_en: The more fools the more laughter.

proverb_native: Plus on remue la merde et plus elle pue
proverb_en: The more you stir it the more it stinks.

proverb_native: Plus on se découvre, plus on a froid
proverb_en: The more a man exposes his nakedness the colder he is.

proverb_native: Plus on se hâte moins on avance
proverb_en: The more haste the worse speed.

proverb_native: Point d’argent, point de Suisse
proverb_en: No money no Swiss.

proverb_native: Point de nouvelles bonnes nouvelles
proverb_en: No news is good news.

proverb_native: Point de roses sans épines
proverb_en: No rose without a thorn.

proverb_native: Porte fermée, le diable s’en va
proverb_en: When the devil finds the door shut he goes away.

proverb_native: Porter de l’eau à la rivière
proverb_en: To carry water to the river.

proverb_native: Porter lanterne à midi
proverb_en: To carry a lantern in mid-day.

proverb_native: Possession vaut titre
proverb_en: Possession is as good as a title.

proverb_native: Pour bien connaître un homme il faut avoir mangé un boisseau de sel avec lui
proverb_en: To know a man well one must have eaten a bushel of salt with him.

proverb_native: Pour de l’argent les chiens dansent
proverb_en: Money makes dogs dance.

proverb_native: Pour faire un bon ménage, il faut que l’homme soit sourd et la femme aveugle
proverb_en: To make a happy couple, the husband must be deaf and the wife blind.

proverb_native: Pour l’amour du chevalier baise la dame l’écuyer
proverb_en: For sake of the knight the lady kisses the squire.

proverb_native: Pour néant demande conseil qui ne le veut croire
proverb_en: He asks advice in vain who will not follow it.

proverb_native: Pour un moine l’abbaye ne faut point
proverb_en: The abbey does not fail for want of one monk.

proverb_native: Pour un plaisir mille douleurs
proverb_en: For one pleasure a thousand pains.

proverb_native: Prendre l’occasion aux cheveux
proverb_en: To take opportunity by the forelock.

proverb_native: Prends le premier conseil d’une femme et non le second
proverb_en: Take a woman’s first advice and not her second.

proverb_native: Près de l’église et loin de Dieu
proverb_en: The nearer the church the farther from God.

proverb_native: Près du moustier à messe le dernier
proverb_en: The nearer the minster the later to mass.

proverb_native: Promettre et tenir sont deux
proverb_en: Promising and performing are two things.

proverb_native: Promettre plus de beurre que de pain
proverb_en: To promise more butter than bread.

proverb_native: Puisque le vin est tiré il faut le boire
proverb_en: Since the wine is drawn it must be drunk.

proverb_native: Quand Dieu envoie la farine le diable enlève le sac
proverb_en: When God sends flour the devil carries off the sack.

proverb_native: Quand il n’y a point de vent chacun sait naviguer
proverb_en: When there is no wind every man is a pilot.

proverb_native: Quand la cage est faite l’oiseau s’envole
proverb_en: When the cage is ready the bird is flown.

proverb_native: Quand la fille est mariée il arrive assez de gendres
proverb_en: After the daughter is married, then come sons-in-law in plenty.

proverb_native: Quand la porte est basse il faut se baisser
proverb_en: When the door is low one must stoop.

proverb_native: Quand l’arbre est tombé tout le monde court aux branches
proverb_en: When the tree is down everybody runs to the branches.

proverb_native: Quand l’aveugle porte la bannière, mal pour ceux qui marchent derrière
proverb_en: When the blind man carries the banner, woe to those who follow.

proverb_native: Quand le chien se noye chacun lui porte de l’eau
proverb_en: When the dog is drowning every one brings him water.

proverb_native: Quand le diable devient vieux il se fait ermite
proverb_en: When the devil grows old he turns hermit.

proverb_native: Quand le diable dit ses patenôtres il veut te tromper
proverb_en: When the devil says his paternosters he means to cheat you.

proverb_native: Quand le Français dort le diable le berce
proverb_en: When the Frenchman sleeps the devil rocks him.

proverb_native: Quand les biens viennent les corps faillent
proverb_en: When goods increase the body decreases.

proverb_native: Quand les paroles sont dites l’eau bénite est faite
proverb_en: When the words are said, the holy water is made.

proverb_native: Quand on a des filles on est toujours berger
proverb_en: He who has daughters is always a shepherd.

proverb_native: Quand on est bien il faut s’y tenir
proverb_en: When you are well off keep as you are.

proverb_native: Quand on est mort, c’est pour longtemps
proverb_en: When one is dead, it is for a long while.

proverb_native: Quand on n’a pas ce que l’on aime, il faut aimer ce que l’on a
proverb_en: When one has not what one likes, one must like what one has.

proverb_native: Quand on parle du loup, on en voit la queue
proverb_en: Talk of the wolf and you see his tail.

proverb_native: Quand tous péchés sont vieux l’avarice est encore jeune
proverb_en: When all other sins are old avarice is still young.

proverb_native: Quand vient la gloire s’en va la mémoire
proverb_en: When glory comes memory departs.

proverb_native: Que ta chemise ne sache ta guise
proverb_en: Let not your shirt know your way of thinking.

proverb_native: Qui a bonne tête ne manque pas de chapeaux
proverb_en: A good head does not want for hats.

proverb_native: Qui a bu boira
proverb_en: He who has drunk will drink.

proverb_native: Qui a compagnon a maître
proverb_en: He who has a companion has a master.

proverb_native: Qui a de l’argent a des pirouettes
proverb_en: He who has money has capers.

proverb_native: Qui a froid souffle le feu
proverb_en: Let him who is cold blow the fire.

proverb_native: Qui a honte de manger a honte de vivre
proverb_en: He that is ashamed to eat is ashamed to live.

proverb_native: Qui aime Bertrand aime son chien
proverb_en: Love Bertrand love his dog.

proverb_native: Qui aime bien châtie bien
proverb_en: Who loves well chastises well.

proverb_native: Qui aime bien tard oublie
proverb_en: Who loves well is slow to forget.

proverb_native: Qui a la bourse pleine prêche au pauvre
proverb_en: He who has his purse full preaches to the poor man.

proverb_native: Qui a tête de cire ne doit pas s’approcher du feu
proverb_en: He that hath a head of wax must not approach the fire.

proverb_native: Qui attend les souliers d’un mort risque d’aller pieds nus
proverb_en: He who waits for a dead man’s shoes is in danger of going barefoot.

proverb_native: Qui cache peut trouver
proverb_en: He that hides can find.

proverb_native: Qui casse les verres les paye
proverb_en: He pays for the glasses who breaks them.

proverb_native: Qui cesse d’être ami ne l’a jamais été
proverb_en: He never was a friend who has ceased to be one.

proverb_native: Qui chapon mange chapon lui vient
proverb_en: Who eats capon, capon comes to him.

proverb_native: Qui choisit, prend le pire
proverb_en: He who chooses takes the worst.

proverb_native: Qui commence et ne parfait, sa peine perd
proverb_en: He who begins and does not finish loses his labour.

proverb_native: Qui compte sans son hôte, compte deux fois
proverb_en: He that reckons without his host must reckon again.

proverb_native: Qui court deux lièvres, n’en prendra aucun
proverb_en: He that hunts two hares will catch neither.

proverb_native: Qui crache contre le ciel, il lui tombe sur la tête
proverb_en: Who spits against heaven, it falls on his head.

proverb_native: Qui craint de souffrir, souffre de crainte
proverb_en: He who fears to suffer, suffers from fear.

proverb_native: Qui cuir voit tailler, courroye en demande
proverb_en: He who sees leather cut asks for a thong.

proverb_native: Qui dit averti, dit muni
proverb_en: Forewarned, forearmed.

proverb_native: Qui donner peut, il a maint bon voisin
proverb_en: He who can give has many a good neighbour.

proverb_native: Qui doute ne se trompe point
proverb_en: Who doubts errs not.

proverb_native: Qui écoute aux portes, entend plus qu’il ne désire
proverb_en: He who listens at doors hears more than he desires.

proverb_native: Qui en dit du mal, veut l’acheter
proverb_en: He who dispraises a thing, wants to buy it.

proverb_native: Qui épargne, gagne
proverb_en: Saving is getting.

proverb_native: Qui épargne le vice, fait tort à la vertu
proverb_en: He who spares vice wrongs virtue.

proverb_native: Qui est avec les loups, il lui faut hurler
proverb_en: He who kennels with wolves must howl.

proverb_native: Qui est bien, qu’il s’y tienne
proverb_en: Whose is well let him keep so.

proverb_native: Qui est sur la mer, il ne fait pas des vents ce qu’il veut
proverb_en: He who is at sea does not direct the winds.

proverb_native: Qui femme a, noise a
proverb_en: He that hath a wife is sure of strife.

proverb_native: Qui femme croit et âne mène, son corps ne sera jamais sans peine
proverb_en: He who trusts a woman and leads an ass will never be free from plague.

proverb_native: Qui gagne, joue bien
proverb_en: He plays well that wins.

proverb_native: Qui juge entre deux amis, perdra l’un ou l’autre
proverb_en: He who judges between two friends loses one of them.

proverb_native: Qui mal cherche, mal trouve
proverb_en: Harm watch harm catch.

proverb_native: Qui mange de l’oye du roi, chiera une plume quarante ans après
proverb_en: He who eats of the king’s goose will void a feather forty years after.

proverb_native: Qui mange la vache du roi maigre, la paie grasse
proverb_en: He who eats the king’s cow lean, pays for it fat.

proverb_native: Qui menace, a peur
proverb_en: He who threatens is afraid.

proverb_native: Qui monte la mule, la ferre
proverb_en: He who rides the mule shoes her.

proverb_native: Qui mouche trop son nez, en tire du sang
proverb_en: Who blows his nose too hard makes it bleed.

proverb_native: Qui naît le dimanche, jamais ne meurt de peste
proverb_en: A Sunday’s child never dies of the plague.

proverb_native: Qui n’amorce pas son haim, pèche en vain
proverb_en: He who does not bait his hook fishes in vain.

proverb_native: Qui n’a, ne peut
proverb_en: Who has not, cannot.

proverb_native: Qui n’a pas argent en bourse, ait miel en bouche
proverb_en: He that has not money in his purse should have honey in his mouth.

proverb_native: Qui naquit chat, court après les souris
proverb_en: Who is born of a cat will run after mice.

proverb_native: Qui n’a qu’un ”oe]il, bien le garde
proverb_en: A man who has but one eye must take good care of it.

proverb_native: Qui n’a rien, ne craint rien
proverb_en: He who has nothing fears nothing.

proverb_native: Qui n’a santé, n’a rien
proverb_en: He who has not health has nothing.

proverb_native: Qui ne châtie culot, ne châtie culasse
proverb_en: He that corrects not youth controls not age.

proverb_native: Qui ne dit mot, consent
proverb_en: Silence gives consent.

proverb_native: Qui ne fait pas quand il peut, il ne fait pas quand il veut
proverb_en: He that will not when he may, when he will shall have nay.

proverb_native: Qui ne fait rien, fait mal
proverb_en: He who does nothing does ill.

proverb_native: Qui ne gagne, perd
proverb_en: He who does not gain loses.

proverb_native: Qui ne hasarde rien, n’a rien
proverb_en: Nothing venture, nothing have.

proverb_native: Qui n’entend qu’une cloche, n’entend qu’un son
proverb_en: Who hears but one bell hears but one sound.

proverb_native: Qui ne parle, n’erre
proverb_en: He who holds his tongue does not commit himself.

proverb_native: Qui ne peut faire son salut par c”oe]ur, ne le fera par livre
proverb_en: Who cannot work out his salvation by heart will not do it by book.

proverb_native: Qui ne peut mordre, ne doit pas montrer les dents
proverb_en: Don’t show your teeth if you can’t bite.

proverb_native: Qui ne regarde pas en avant, se trouve en arrière
proverb_en: He who looks not before finds himself behind.

proverb_native: Qui ne retire de sa vache que la queue, ne perd pas tout
proverb_en: He who recovers but the tail of his cow does not lose all.

proverb_native: Qui ne sait bien parler de son métier, il ne le sait pas
proverb_en: He who cannot speak well of his trade does not understand it.

proverb_native: Qui ne s’aventure, n’a ni cheval ni mule; et qui trop s’aventure, perd cheval et mule
proverb_en: Who does not venture gets neither horse nor mule, and who ventures too much loses horse and mule.

proverb_native: Qui ne se lasse pas, lasse l’adversité
proverb_en: He who does not tire, tires adversity.

proverb_native: Qui ne sort que de jour, n’a que faire de lanterne
proverb_en: He who goes abroad by day has no need of a lantern.

proverb_native: Qui ne souffre pas seul, ne souffre pas tant
proverb_en: Company in distress makes trouble less.

proverb_native: Qui ne veut parler, ne veut gagner
proverb_en: Spare to speak and spare to speed.

proverb_native: Qui n’y va, n’y chet
proverb_en: He that ventures not fails not.

proverb_native: Qui parle, sème; qui écoute, recueille
proverb_en: Who speaks, sows; who listens, reaps.

proverb_native: Qui partout va, partout prend
proverb_en: He who goes everywhere gains everywhere.

proverb_native: Qui passe un jour d’hiver, il passe un de ses ennemis mortels
proverb_en: He who passes a winter’s day passes one of his mortal enemies.

proverb_native: Qui paye, a bien le droit de donner son avis
proverb_en: He who pays is fairly entitled to speak his mind.

proverb_native: Qui paye bien, est bien servi
proverb_en: He who pays well is well served.

proverb_native: Qui paye tôt, emprunte quand il veut
proverb_en: Who pays soon borrows when he will.

proverb_native: Qui perd, pèche
proverb_en: He who loses sins.

proverb_native: Qui peut lécher, peut mordre
proverb_en: He who can lick can bite.

proverb_native: Qui plus qu’il n’a vaillant dépend, il fait la corde à quoi se pend
proverb_en: He that spends more than he is worth spins a rope for his own neck.

proverb_native: Qui plus sait, plus se tait
proverb_en: Who knows most says least.

proverb_native: Qui porte un fardeau, en portera bientôt cent
proverb_en: He who carries one burden will soon carry a hundred.

proverb_native: Qui pourrait vivre sans espoir?
proverb_en: Who could live without hope?

proverb_native: Qui prend femme, prend maître
proverb_en: He who takes a wife takes a master.

proverb_native: Qui prend une femme pour sa dot, à la liberté tourne le dos
proverb_en: Who wives for a dower, resigns his own power.

proverb_native: Qui prête à l’ami, perd au double
proverb_en: Who lends to a friend loses doubly.

proverb_native: Qui prouve trop, ne prouve rien
proverb_en: Who proves too much proves nothing.

proverb_native: Qui quitte sa place, la perd
proverb_en: He who quits his place loses it.

proverb_native: Qui refuse, muse
proverb_en: Who refuses, muses.

proverb_native: Qui répond, paye
proverb_en: Who answers for another pays.

proverb_native: Qui reste dans la vallée ne passera jamais la montagne
proverb_en: He that stays in the valley will not get over the hill.

proverb_native: Qui rien ne porte, rien ne lui chet
proverb_en: He who carries nothing loses nothing.

proverb_native: Qui s’acquitte, s’enrichit
proverb_en: He who gets out of debt enriches himself.

proverb_native: Qui s’arrête à chaque pierre, n’arrive jamais
proverb_en: He who stops at every stone never gets to his journey’s end.

proverb_native: Qui s’attend à l’écuelle d’autrui, dîne souvent par c”oe]ur
proverb_en: He who waits for another man’s trencher often dines in imagination (or with Duke Humphrey).

proverb_native: Qui saurait les aventures, ne serait jamais pauvre
proverb_en: Could a man foresee events he would never be poor.

proverb_native: Qui se couche avec des chiens, se lève avec des puces
proverb_en: He who lies down with dogs gets up with fleas.

proverb_native: Qui se détourne, évite le danger
proverb_en: He who turns aside avoids danger.

proverb_native: Qui se fâche, a tort
proverb_en: He who loses his temper is in the wrong.

proverb_native: Qui se fait brebis, le loup le mange
proverb_en: Make yourself a sheep and the wolf will eat you.

proverb_native: Qui se marie à la hâte, se repent à loisir
proverb_en: Marry in haste and repent at leisure.

proverb_native: Qui se marie par amours, a bonnes nuits et mauvais jours
proverb_en: He who marries for love has good nights and bad days.

proverb_native: Qui sème des chardons, recueille des épines
proverb_en: He who sows thistles reaps thorns.

proverb_native: Qui sème épines, n’aille déchaux
proverb_en: Who sows thorns should not go barefoot.

proverb_native: Qui sème, recueille
proverb_en: Who sows reaps.

proverb_native: Qui se remue, Dieu l’adjue
proverb_en: God helps him who helps himself.

proverb_native: Qui se ressemble s’assemble
proverb_en: Like will to like.

proverb_native: Qui sert commun, nul ne le paye, et s’il défaut, chacun l’abbaye
proverb_en: Who serves the mass is thanked by none, but cursed if aught be left undone.

proverb_native: Qui se sent galeux, se gratte
proverb_en: Let him that itches scratch himself.

proverb_native: Qui se sent morveux, se mouche
proverb_en: Let him who feels he has a dirty nose wipe it.

proverb_native: Qui se tient à Paris, ne sera jamais pape
proverb_en: He who never budges from Paris will never be pope.

proverb_native: Qui s’excuse, s’accuse
proverb_en: He who excuses himself accuses himself.

proverb_native: Qui s’y frotte, s’y pique
proverb_en: No jesting with edged tools.

proverb_native: Qui terre a, guerre a
proverb_en: Whoso hath land hath war.

proverb_native: Qui tient la poële par la queue, il la tourne là où il veut
proverb_en: He who holds the handle of the frying-pan turns it as he pleases.

proverb_native: Qui tient le fil, tient le peloton
proverb_en: He who holds the thread holds the ball.

proverb_native: Qui tôt donne, deux fois donne
proverb_en: He gives twice who gives in a trice.

proverb_native: Qui tourmente les autres, ne dort pas bien
proverb_en: He who torments others does not sleep well.

proverb_native: Qui trébuche et ne tombe pas, avance son chemin
proverb_en: He that stumbles and falls not, mends his pace.

proverb_native: Qui trop change, empire
proverb_en: Who often changes, damages.

proverb_native: Qui trop embrasse, mal étreint
proverb_en: He who grasps too much holds not firmly.

proverb_native: Qui trop se hâte en cheminant, en beau chemin se fourvoye souvent
proverb_en: He that is too much in haste, may stumble on a good road.

proverb_native: Qui un punit, cent menace
proverb_en: Who punishes one threatens a hundred.

proverb_native: Qui va chercher de la laine, revient tondu
proverb_en: He who goes to collect wool may come back shorn.

proverb_native: Qui va et retourne, fait bon voyage
proverb_en: Who goes and returns makes a good journey.

proverb_native: Qui va, il lèche; qui repose, il sèche
proverb_en: He who bestirs himself sucks up, he who lies still dries up.

proverb_native: Qui veut apprendre à prier, aille souvent sur la mer
proverb_en: If a man would learn to pray let him go often to sea.

proverb_native: Qui veut être riche en un an, au bout de six mois est pendu
proverb_en: He who wants to be rich in a year is hanged at six months’ end.

proverb_native: Qui veut faire une porte d’or, il y met tous les jours un clou
proverb_en: He who wishes to make a golden door drives a nail into it every day.

proverb_native: Qui veut noyer son chien, l’accuse de rage
proverb_en: A man who wants to drown his dog says he is mad.

proverb_native: Qui veut plaire à tout le monde, doit se lever de bonne heure
proverb_en: He had need rise early who would please everybody.

proverb_native: Qui veut prendre un oiseau, qu’il ne l’effarouche
proverb_en: To scare a bird is not the way to catch it.

proverb_native: Qui veut tenir nette maison, il n’y faut prêtre ni pigeon
proverb_en: He that would keep his house clean must not let priest or pigeon into it.

proverb_native: Qui veut vivre à Rome, ne doit pas se quereller avec le pape
proverb_en: He who wishes to live at Rome must not quarrel with the pope.

proverb_native: Qui veut voyager loin, ménage sa monture
proverb_en: He who wants to travel far takes care of his beast.

proverb_native: Qui vient, est beau; qui apporte, est encore plus beau
proverb_en: Fair is he that comes, but fairer he that brings.

proverb_native: Qui vit à compte, vit à honte
proverb_en: Who lives on the score has shame evermore.

proverb_native: Qui vit longtemps, sait ce qu’est douleur
proverb_en: He who lives long knows what pain is.

proverb_native: Qui vivra, verra
proverb_en: Who lives will see.

proverb_native: Qui voit une épingle et ne la prend, vient un temps qu’il s’en repent
proverb_en: See a pin and let it lie, you’ll want a pin before you die.

proverb_native: Quoique fol tarde, jour ne tarde
proverb_en: Though the fool waits, the day does not.

proverb_native: Raison contre le fort est un trepiteux port
proverb_en: Reason not with the great, ’tis a perilous gate.

proverb_native: Renard qui dort la matinée, n’a pas la langue emplumée
proverb_en: The fox that sleeps in the morning has not his tongue feathered.

proverb_native: Rendre à quelqu’un la monnaie de sa pièce
proverb_en: To give change out for his coin.

proverb_native: Rendre pois pour fève
proverb_en: To give a pea for a bean.

proverb_native: Ressembler aux bahutiers, qui font plus de bruit que de besogne
proverb_en: Like box-makers, more noise than work.

proverb_native: Rien n’a qui assez n’a
proverb_en: He has nothing who has not enough.

proverb_native: Rien n’arrive pour rien
proverb_en: Nothing happens for nothing.

proverb_native: Rien ne pèse tant qu’un secret
proverb_en: Nothing is so burthensome as a secret.

proverb_native: Rien ne ressemble plus à un honnête homme qu’un fripon
proverb_en: Nothing is more like an honest man than a rogue.

proverb_native: Rien ne se donne si libéralement que les conseils
proverb_en: Nothing is so liberally given as advice.

proverb_native: Rien n’est bon comme le fruit défendu
proverb_en: Nothing so good as forbidden fruit.

proverb_native: Rien ne vaut poulain s’il ne rompt son lien
proverb_en: A colt is good for nothing if it does not break its halter.

proverb_native: Rien ne vieillit plus vite qu’un bienfait
proverb_en: Nothing grows old sooner than a kindness.

proverb_native: Rira bien qui rira le dernier
proverb_en: He’ll laugh well that laughs longest.

proverb_native: Rome n’a pas été faite en un jour
proverb_en: Rome was not built in a day.

proverb_native: Saint ne peut, si Dieu ne veut
proverb_en: Saint cannot if God will not.

proverb_native: Saint qui ne guérit de rien, n’a guère de pélerins
proverb_en: The saint who works no cures has few pilgrims to his shrine.

proverb_native: Sans pain, sans vin, amour n’est rien
proverb_en: Without bread and wine even love will pine.

proverb_native: Sauter de la poële sur la braise
proverb_en: To jump out of the frying pan into the fire.

proverb_native: Secret de deux, secret de Dieu; secret de trois, secret de tous
proverb_en: The secret of two is God’s secret, the secret of three is everybody’s secret.

proverb_native: Se faire d’évêque meunier
proverb_en: From bishop to turn miller.

proverb_native: Se faire marchand de poissons la veille de Pâques
proverb_en: To turn fishmonger on Easter-eve.

proverb_native: Se jeter dans l’eau de peur de la pluie
proverb_en: To jump into the water for fear of the rain.

proverb_native: Selon le bras la saignée
proverb_en: According to the arm be the bleeding.

proverb_native: Selon le saint l’encens
proverb_en: Like saint like incense.

proverb_native: Selon le vent la voile
proverb_en: As the wind so the sail.

proverb_native: Sers comme serf, ou fuy comme cerf
proverb_en: Serve as a serf or fly like a deer.

proverb_native: Service de grands n’est pas héritage
proverb_en: Service is no inheritance.

proverb_native: Si ce n’était le si et mais, nous serions tous riches à jamais
proverb_en: Were it not for “if” and “but,” we should all be rich for ever.

proverb_native: Si c’était un loup, il vous sauterait au cou
proverb_en: Were it a wolf it would spring at your throat.

proverb_native: Si enfer n’est plein, jamais n’y aura d’avocat sauvé
proverb_en: Unless hell is full no lawyer will ever be saved.

proverb_native: Signer pour les deux parties
proverb_en: To sign for both parties.

proverb_native: Si jeunesse savait! si vieillesse pouvait!
proverb_en: If youth knew! if age could!

proverb_native: Si le ciel tombait il y aurait bien des alouettes prises
proverb_en: If the sky were to fall we should catch plenty of larks.

proverb_native: Si le diable sortait de l’enfer pour combattre, il se présenterait aussitôt un Français pour accepter le défi
proverb_en: Were the devil to come from hell to fight, there would forthwith be a Frenchman to accept the challenge.

proverb_native: S’il est vrai, il peut être
proverb_en: ’Tis possible if true.

proverb_native: S’il fait beau, prends ton manteau; s’il pleut, prends-le si tu veux
proverb_en: If the weather is fine, put on your cloak; if it rains, do as you please.

proverb_native: S’il ne tient qu’à jurer, la vache est à nous
proverb_en: If it only depends on swearing, the cow is ours.

proverb_native: Si nous payons la musique, nous voulons aussi danser
proverb_en: If we pay for the music we will join in the dance.

proverb_native: Si souhaits fussent vrais, pastoureaux rois seraient
proverb_en: If wishes were true, shepherds would be kings.

proverb_native: Si tu as la tête de beurre, ne te fais pas boulanger
proverb_en: If your head is made of butter, don’t be a baker.

proverb_native: Si tu ne le peux dire, si le monstre au doigt
proverb_en: If you cannot say it, point to it with your finger.

proverb_native: Si vous lui donnez un pied, il vous en prendra quatre
proverb_en: Give him a foot and he’ll take four.

proverb_native: Six choses au monde n’ont mestier: prestre hardy, coüard chevalier, juge convoiteux, puant barbier, mère piteuse, rogneux boulengier
proverb_en: Six things have no business in the world: a fighting priest, a coward knight, a covetous judge, a stinking barber, a soft-hearted mother, and an itchy baker.

proverb_native: Sois vraiment ce que tu veux qu’on te croie
proverb_en: Be truly what thou wouldst be thought to be.

proverb_native: Soleil qui luisarne au matin, femme qui parle latin, enfant nourri de vin, ne viennent point à bonne fin
proverb_en: A glaring sunny morning, a woman that talks Latin, and a child reared on wine, never come to a good end.

proverb_native: Son cheval a la tête trop grosse, il ne peut sortir de l’écurie
proverb_en: His horse’s head is too big, it cannot get out of the stable.

proverb_native: Songes sont mensonges
proverb_en: Dreams are lies.

proverb_native: Soubs ombre d’asne entre chien en moulin
proverb_en: The dog gets into the mill under cover of the ass.

proverb_native: Souffler le chaud et le froid
proverb_en: To blow hot and cold.

proverb_native: Soupçon est d’amitié poison
proverb_en: Suspicion is the poison of friendship.

proverb_native: Souris qui n’a qu’un trou est bientôt prise
proverb_en: The mouse that has but one hole is soon caught.

proverb_native: Souvent les railleurs sont raillés
proverb_en: The biter is often bit.

proverb_native: Suivez la rivière et vous gagnerez la mer
proverb_en: Follow the river and you will reach the sea.

proverb_native: Surement va qui n’a rien
proverb_en: He goes safely who has nothing.

proverb_native: Sur un ”oe]uf pond la poule un ”oe]uf
proverb_en: Upon an egg the hen lays an egg.

proverb_native: Tant doit-on le chien blandir qu’on ait la voie passée
proverb_en: One must talk soothingly to the dog until one has passed him.

proverb_native: Tant va la cruche à l’eau qu’à la fin elle se brise
proverb_en: The pitcher goes often to the well and gets broken at last.

proverb_native: Tant vaut l’homme, tant vaut sa terre
proverb_en: As the man is worth his land is worth.

proverb_native: Tard donner, c’est refuser
proverb_en: To give tardily is to refuse.

proverb_native: Tel a beaux yeux qui n’y voit goutte
proverb_en: Some have fine eyes and can’t see a jot.

proverb_native: Tel a du pain qui n’a plus de dents
proverb_en: Some have bread who have no teeth left.

proverb_native: Tel croit se chauffer qui se brûle
proverb_en: Some who mean only to warm, burn themselves.

proverb_native: Tel cuide avoir fait qui commence
proverb_en: Some think they have done when they are only beginning.

proverb_native: Tel cuide venger sa honte qui l’accroît
proverb_en: Some thinking to avenge their shame increase it.

proverb_native: Tel en pâtit qui n’en peut mais
proverb_en: Many a one suffers for what he can’t help.

proverb_native: Tel maître, tel valet
proverb_en: Like master, like man.

proverb_native: Tel menace, qui a peur
proverb_en: A man may threaten yet be afraid.

proverb_native: Tel menace, qui est battu
proverb_en: The threatener sometimes gets a beating.

proverb_native: Tel qui rit vendredi, dimanche pleurera
proverb_en: He that laughs on Friday may cry on Sunday.

proverb_native: Tel vend, qui ne livre pas
proverb_en: Some sell and don’t deliver.

proverb_native: Temps, vent, femme, et fortune, changent comme la lune
proverb_en: Weather, wind, women, and fortune change like the moon.

proverb_native: Tendresse maternelle toujours se renouvelle
proverb_en: Mother’s love is ever in its spring.

proverb_native: Tenir le loup par les oreilles
proverb_en: To hold the wolf by the ears.

proverb_native: Tête de fou ne blanchit jamais
proverb_en: A fool’s head never whitens.

proverb_native: Tirer le diable par la queue, ne mène loin jeunes ni vieux
proverb_en: Pulling the devil by the tail does not lead far young or old.

proverb_native: Tirer les marrons du feu avec la patte du chat
proverb_en: To take the chesnuts out of the fire with the cat’s paw.

proverb_native: Tôt gagné, tôt gaspillé
proverb_en: Soon gained soon squandered.

proverb_native: Toujours amoureux, jamais marié
proverb_en: Always in love, never married.

proverb_native: Toujours ne frappe-t-on pas ce à quoy l’on vise
proverb_en: One does not always hit what one aims at.

proverb_native: Toujours pêche qui en prend un
proverb_en: He fishes on who catches one.

proverb_native: Toujours truye songe bran
proverb_en: A sow is always dreaming of bran.

proverb_native: Tout bec crochu de proye est soustenu
proverb_en: Every hooked beak is maintained by prey.

proverb_native: Tout bois n’est pas bon à faire flèche
proverb_en: Not every sort of wood is fit to make an arrow.

proverb_native: Tout ce qui branle ne tombe pas
proverb_en: Every thing does not fall that totters.

proverb_native: Tout ce qui reluit n’est pas or
proverb_en: All is not gold that glitters.

proverb_native: Tout chemin mène à Rome
proverb_en: Every road leads to Rome.

proverb_native: Toute chair n’est pas venaison
proverb_en: All flesh is not venison.

proverb_native: Toute chose qui est bonne à prendre est bonne à rendre
proverb_en: What is worth receiving is worth returning.

proverb_native: Toute comparaison est odieuse
proverb_en: Comparisons are odious.

proverb_native: Toute eau éteint feu
proverb_en: Any water puts out fire.

proverb_native: Toutes les clefs ne pendent pas à une ceinture
proverb_en: All the keys don’t hang at one girdle.

proverb_native: Toutes têtes ne sont pas coffres à raison
proverb_en: All heads are not sense-boxes.

proverb_native: Toutes vérités ne sont pas bonnes à dire
proverb_en: All truths are not good to be uttered.

proverb_native: Tout état, et rien au plat
proverb_en: All state, and nothing on the plate.

proverb_native: Tout fait ventre, pourvu qu’il entre
proverb_en: A bellyful is a bellyful.

proverb_native: Tout le monde est sage après l’événement
proverb_en: Everybody is wise after the thing has happened.

proverb_native: Tout paraît jaune à qui a la jaunisse
proverb_en: To the jaundiced all things seem yellow.

proverb_native: Tout passe, tout casse, tout lasse
proverb_en: Everything passes, everything breaks, everything wearies.

proverb_native: Tout va à qui n’a pas besoin
proverb_en: Everything goes to him who does not want it.

proverb_native: Tout vient à point à qui sait attendre
proverb_en: Everything in time comes to him, who knows how to wait.

proverb_native: Tout y va par compère et commère
proverb_en: Everything goes by favour and cousinship.

proverb_native: Tricherie revient à son maître
proverb_en: Trickery comes back to its master.

proverb_native: Triste est la maison où le coq se tait et la poule chante
proverb_en: It is a sorry house in which the cock is silent and the hen crows.

proverb_native: Trois déménagements valent un incendie
proverb_en: Three removals are as bad as a fire.

proverb_native: Trois frères, trois châteaux
proverb_en: Three brothers, three castles.

proverb_native: Trop achète le miel qui le lèche sur les épines
proverb_en: He pays dear for honey who licks it off thorns.

proverb_native: Trop de zèle gâte tout
proverb_en: Too much zeal spoils all.

proverb_native: Trop gratter cuit, trop parler nuit
proverb_en: Too much scratching smarts, too much talking harms.

proverb_native: Trop tard crie l’oiseau quand il est pris
proverb_en: It is too late for the bird to scream when it is caught.

proverb_native: Trop tranchant ne coupe pas, trop pointu ne perce pas
proverb_en: Too keen an edge does not cut, too fine a point does not pierce.

proverb_native: Truie aime mieux bran que roses
proverb_en: A sow prefers bran to roses.

proverb_native: Tuer la poule pour avoir l’”oe]uf
proverb_en: To kill the hen by way of getting the egg.

proverb_native: Tuer un mercier pour un peigne
proverb_en: To kill a mercer for a comb.

proverb_native: Un ami de table et de vin, tenir ne faut pour bon voisin
proverb_en: A friend to my table and wine, is no good neighbour.

proverb_native: Un âne ne trébuche pas deux fois sur la même pierre
proverb_en: An ass does not stumble twice over the same stone.

proverb_native: Un apothicaire ne doit être longtemps cocu
proverb_en: An apothecary ought not to be long a cuckold.

proverb_native: Un aveugle mène l’autre en la fosse
proverb_en: One blind man leads another into the ditch.

proverb_native: Un barbier en rase un autre
proverb_en: One barber shaves another.

proverb_native: Un bienfait reproché tint toujours lieu d’offense
proverb_en: Upbraiding makes a benefit an injury.

proverb_native: Un boiteux ne veut aller avec un plus boiteux que lui
proverb_en: A lame man won’t walk with one who is lamer.

proverb_native: Un bon avis vaut un ”oe]il dans la main
proverb_en: A good advice is as good as an eye in the hand.

proverb_native: Un bon bailleur en fait bailler deux
proverb_en: A good gaper makes two gapers.

proverb_native: Un bon renard ne mange pas les poules de son voisin
proverb_en: A good fox does not eat his neighbour’s fowls.

proverb_native: Un bon repas doit commencer par la faim
proverb_en: A good repast ought to begin with hunger.

proverb_native: Un brochet fait plus qu’une lettre de recommandation
proverb_en: A jackfish does more than a letter of recommendation.

proverb_native: Un cerveau ne vaut guère sans langue
proverb_en: A brain is worth little without a tongue.

proverb_native: Un chien regarde bien un évêque
proverb_en: A dog may look at a bishop.

proverb_native: Un clou chasse l’autre
proverb_en: One nail drives out another.

proverb_native: Un coup de langue est pire qu’un coup de lance
proverb_en: The tongue wounds more than a lance.

proverb_native: Un courtisan doit être sans humeur et sans honneur
proverb_en: A courtier should be without feeling and without honour.

proverb_native: Une chandelle à Saint Michel et une à son diable
proverb_en: One candle for St. Michael, and another for his devil.

proverb_native: Une conscience pure est un bon oreiller
proverb_en: A clear conscience is a good pillow.

proverb_native: Une faute niée est deux fois commise
proverb_en: A fault denied is twice committed.

proverb_native: Une femme ne cèle que ce qu’elle ne sait pas
proverb_en: A woman conceals only what she does not know.

proverb_native: Une fleur ne fait pas une guirlande
proverb_en: One flower does not make a garland.

proverb_native: Une fois n’est pas coutume
proverb_en: Once is no custom.

proverb_native: Une hirondelle ne fait pas le printemps
proverb_en: One swallow does not make a spring.

proverb_native: Une once de faveur vaut mieux qu’une livre de justice
proverb_en: An ounce of favour goes further, or is worth more, than a pound of justice.

proverb_native: Une poule aveugle peut quelquefois trouver son grain
proverb_en: A blind hen can sometimes find her corn.

proverb_native: Une science requiert tout son homme
proverb_en: An art requires a whole man.

proverb_native: Une tromperie en attire une autre
proverb_en: One deceit brings on another.

proverb_native: Un fou avise bien un sage
proverb_en: A wise man may learn of a fool.

proverb_native: Un fou fait toujours commencement
proverb_en: A fool is always beginning.

proverb_native: Un homme averti en vaut deux
proverb_en: A man warned is as good as two.

proverb_native: Un homme bien monté est toujours orgueilleux
proverb_en: A man well mounted is always proud.

proverb_native: Un homme de paille vaut une femme d’or
proverb_en: A man of straw is worth a woman of gold.

proverb_native: Un homme mort n’a ni parents ni amis
proverb_en: A dead man has neither relations nor friends.

proverb_native: Un homme, nul homme
proverb_en: One man, no man.

proverb_native: Un homme qui se noie s’attache à un brin d’herbe
proverb_en: A drowning man clings to a blade of grass.

proverb_native: Un homme riche n’est jamais laid pour une fille
proverb_en: A rich man is never ugly in the eyes of a girl.

proverb_native: Un jour en vaut deux pour qui fait chaque chose en son lieu
proverb_en: One day is as good as two for him who does everything in its place.

proverb_native: Un malheur ne vient jamais seul
proverb_en: Misfortunes never come single.

proverb_native: Un marteau d’argent rompt une porte de fer
proverb_en: A silver hammer breaks an iron door.

proverb_native: Un mauvais accommodement vaut mieux qu’un bon procès
proverb_en: A bad compromise is better than a good lawsuit.

proverb_native: Un moineau dans la main vaut mieux qu’une grue qui vole
proverb_en: A sparrow in the hand is better than a crane on the wing.

proverb_native: Un noble prince ou roy n’a jamais pille ne croix
proverb_en: A noble prince or king never has a coin to bless himself.

proverb_native: Un petit homme projette parfois une grande ombre
proverb_en: A little man sometimes casts a long shadow.

proverb_native: Un peu d’absence fait grand bien
proverb_en: A little absence does much good.

proverb_native: Un peu d’aide fait grand bien
proverb_en: A little help does a great deal.

proverb_native: Un peu de fiel gâte beaucoup de miel
proverb_en: A little gall spoils a great deal of honey.

proverb_native: Un seigneur de paille mange un vassal d’acier
proverb_en: A lord of straw devours a vassal of steel.

proverb_native: Un seul homme ne peut suffire à tout
proverb_en: No living man all things can.

proverb_native: Un sot trouve toujours un plus sot qui l’admire
proverb_en: One fool always finds a greater fool to admire him.

proverb_native: Un tiens vaut mieux que deux tu l’auras
proverb_en: One take-this is better than two thou-shalt-haves.

proverb_native: Un ver se recoquille quand on marche dessus
proverb_en: Tread on a worm and it will turn.

proverb_native: Un vieux four est plus aisé à chauffer qu’un neuf
proverb_en: An old oven is easier to heat than a new one.

proverb_native: Vache de loin a lait assez
proverb_en: A cow from afar gives plenty of milk.

proverb_native: Vache ne sait ce que vaut sa queue jusqu’à ce qu’elle l’ait perdue
proverb_en: A cow does not know what her tail is worth until she has lost it.

proverb_native: Valet devant, maître derrière, en pont, en planche, en rivière
proverb_en: At a bridge, a plank, a river, the servant foremost, the master behind.

proverb_native: Vent au visage rend un homme sage
proverb_en: Adversity makes a man wise.

proverb_native: Ventre affamé n’a point d’oreilles
proverb_en: A hungry belly has no ears.

proverb_native: Ventre plein conseille bien
proverb_en: A full belly counsels well.

proverb_native: Veux-tu meilleur pain que de froment?
proverb_en: Do you want better bread than wheaten?

proverb_native: Viande d’ami est bientôt prête
proverb_en: A friend’s meat is soon ready.

proverb_native: Vides chambres font femmes folles
proverb_en: Empty rooms make giddy housewives.

proverb_native: Vie de pourceau, courte et bonne
proverb_en: A pig’s life, short and sweet.

proverb_native: Vieilles amours et vieux tisons s’allument en toutes saisons
proverb_en: Old love and old brands kindle at all seasons.

proverb_native: Vieux amis et comptes nouveaux
proverb_en: Old friends and new reckonings.

proverb_native: Vieux b”oe]uf fait sillon droit
proverb_en: An old ox makes a straight furrow.

proverb_native: Vieux chien n’aboie pas en vain
proverb_en: An old dog does not bark for nothing.

proverb_native: Vilain affamé, demi enragé
proverb_en: A hungry clown is half mad.

proverb_native: Vilain enrichi ne connaît parent ni ami
proverb_en: A clown enriched knows neither relation nor friend.

proverb_native: Vilain ne sçait qu’esperon vaut
proverb_en: A churl knows not the worth of spurs

proverb_native: Ville qui parlemente est moitié rendue
proverb_en: The town that parleys is half surrendered.

proverb_native: Vin versé n’est pas avalé
proverb_en: Wine poured out is not wine swallowed.

proverb_native: Vivre au jour la journée
proverb_en: To live from hand to mouth.

proverb_native: Voix du peuple, voix de Dieu
proverb_en: The people’s voice, God’s voice.

proverb_native: Voler un voleur n’est pas voler
proverb_en: To rob a robber is not robbing.

proverb_native: Vouloir, c’est pouvoir
proverb_en: Will is power.

proverb_native: Vraie noblesse nul ne blesse
proverb_en: True nobility is invulnerable.

proverb_native: Abbiamo pur fiorini che troveremo cugini
proverb_en: Let us have florins and we shall find cousins.

proverb_native: Abbi piuttosto il piccolo per amico, che il grande per nemico
proverb_en: Rather have a little one for your friend, than a great one for your enemy.

proverb_native: Abbondanza genera fastidio
proverb_en: Plenty makes daintiness.

proverb_native: A bisogni si conoscon gli amici
proverb_en: Friends are known in time of need.

proverb_native: A buona derrata pensaci su
proverb_en: At a good bargain pause and ponder.

proverb_native: A buon cavaliere non manca lancia
proverb_en: A good cavalier never lacks a lance.

proverb_native: A buon cavallo non manca sella
proverb_en: A good horse never lacks a saddle.

proverb_native: A buon cavallo non occorre dirgli trotta
proverb_en: No need to say “trot” to a good horse.

proverb_native: A buon intenditor poche parole
proverb_en: A word to the wise is enough.

proverb_native: A buon vino non bisogna frasca
proverb_en: Good wine needs no bush.

proverb_native: A cader va chi troppo in alto sale
proverb_en: He who climbs too high is near a fall.

proverb_native: A can che fugge, dagli, dagli
proverb_en: When a dog runs away, hit him! hit him!

proverb_native: A can che lecchi cenere, non gli fidar farina
proverb_en: The dog that licks ashes is not to be trusted with flour.

proverb_native: A cane scottato l’acqua fredda pare calda
proverb_en: A scalded dog thinks cold water hot.

proverb_native: A cattiva vacca Dio dà corte corna
proverb_en: God gives a curst cow short horns.

proverb_native: A cattivo cane corto legame
proverb_en: A mischievous dog must be tied short.

proverb_native: A causa perduta, parole assai
proverb_en: Plenty of words when the cause is lost.

proverb_native: A caval donato, non guardar in bocca
proverb_en: Look not a gift horse in the mouth.

proverb_native: A caval donato, non si mira il pelo
proverb_en: Never heed the colour of a gift horse.

proverb_native: A cavalli tristi o buoni, sempre porta i tuoi sproni
proverb_en: Be the horse good or bad always wear your spurs.

proverb_native: A caval magro vanno le mosche
proverb_en: Flies flock to the lean horse.

proverb_native: A cavar di casa un morto, ci voglion quattro vivi
proverb_en: It takes four living men to carry one dead man out of a house.

proverb_native: Accasca in un punto quel che non accasca in cento anni
proverb_en: That happens in a moment which may not happen in a hundred years.

proverb_native: Accenna al savio e lascia far a lui
proverb_en: Give the wise man a hint and leave him to act.

proverb_native: Accordatevi, dice Arlotto, ed io farò piovere
proverb_en: Agree between yourselves (as to the time), quoth Arlotto, and I will make it rain.

proverb_native: Accosta più la camicia che il giubbone
proverb_en: The shirt is nearer than the doublet.

proverb_native: Accostati a’ buoni e sarai uno di essi
proverb_en: Associate with the good and you will be one of them.

proverb_native: A chi compra non bastan cent’occhi, e à chi vende ne basta un solo
proverb_en: For the buyer a hundred eyes are too few, for the seller one is enough.

proverb_native: A chi dici il tuo secreto, doni la tua libertà
proverb_en: To whom you tell your secret you surrender your freedom.

proverb_native: A chi fa male, mai mancano scuse
proverb_en: The wrong-doer never lacks excuses.

proverb_native: A chi fugge, ogni cosa dà impaccio
proverb_en: The fugitive finds everything impede him.

proverb_native: A chi ha testa, non manca cappello
proverb_en: He who has a head won’t want for a hat.

proverb_native: A chi la riesce bene, è tenuto per savio
proverb_en: He who succeeds is reputed wise.

proverb_native: A chi non si lascia consigliare, non si può aiutare
proverb_en: There is no helping him who will not be advised.

proverb_native: A chi parla poco, gli basta la metà del cervello
proverb_en: Half a brain is enough for him who says little.

proverb_native: A chi piace il bere, parla sempre di vino
proverb_en: He who likes drinking is always talking of wine.

proverb_native: A chi ti dà un porco, tu gli puoi ben dar una carbonata
proverb_en: To him who gives you a pig you may well give a rasher.

proverb_native: A chi ti può torre ciò che hai, dagli ciò che ti chiede
proverb_en: To him who can take what thou hast, give what he asks.

proverb_native: A chi troppo ride gli duole il cuore
proverb_en: He who laughs overmuch may have an aching heart.

proverb_native: A chi veglia tutto si rivela
proverb_en: To him who watches, everything reveals itself.

proverb_native: A chi vuole, non è cosa difficile
proverb_en: Nothing is difficult to a willing mind.

proverb_native: A chi vuole, non mancano modi
proverb_en: Where there’s a will there’s a way.

proverb_native: A chi vuol fare, non manca che fare
proverb_en: To him who is determined it remains only to act.

proverb_native: A colomba pasciuta la vescia par amara
proverb_en: Vetches seem bitter to the full-cropped pigeon.

proverb_native: A conti vecchi contese nuove
proverb_en: Old reckonings, new disputes.

proverb_native: Acqua che corre non porta veleno
proverb_en: Running water carries no poison.

proverb_native: Acqua cheta vermini mena
proverb_en: Still water breeds vermin.

proverb_native: Acqua, fumo, e mala femmina, cacciano la gente di casa
proverb_en: Water, smoke, and a vicious woman, drive men out of the house.

proverb_native: Acqua lontana non spegne fuoco vicino
proverb_en: Water afar does not quench a fire at hand.

proverb_native: Acqua passata non macina più
proverb_en: Water past will not turn the mill.

proverb_native: Acqua torbida non fa specchio
proverb_en: Muddy water won’t do for a mirror.

proverb_native: Acqua torbida non lava
proverb_en: Dirty water does not wash clean.

proverb_native: Acquista buona fama e mettiti à dormire
proverb_en: Get a good name and go to sleep.

proverb_native: A cuor vile forza non giova
proverb_en: Strength avails not a coward.

proverb_native: Adagio a’ mal passi
proverb_en: Go softly at bad bits of road.

proverb_native: Ad albero caduto accétta, accétta
proverb_en: To the fallen tree, hatchets! hatchets!

proverb_native: Ad arbor che cade ognun grida dàgli, dàgli
proverb_en: When a tree is falling, every one cries, down with it.

proverb_native: Ad arca aperta il giusto pecca
proverb_en: At an open chest the righteous sins.

proverb_native: A dono nuovo non convien grazia vecchia
proverb_en: Old thanks are not for new gifts.

proverb_native: Ad occhio infermo nuoce la luce
proverb_en: The light is painful to sore eyes.

proverb_native: Ad ogni santo la sua torcia
proverb_en: To every saint his torch.

proverb_native: Ad ogni santo vien la sua festa
proverb_en: Every saint has his festival.

proverb_native: Ad ogni uccello suo nido par bello
proverb_en: Every bird thinks its own nest beautiful.

proverb_native: Ad ogni volpe piace il pollaio
proverb_en: Every fox likes a henroost.

proverb_native: Ad ognuno par più grave la croce sua
proverb_en: Every one thinks his own cross the heaviest.

proverb_native: Ad ora ad ora vola tutto il tempo
proverb_en: Hour by hour time departs.

proverb_native: Ad un cieco mal può mostrarsi il cammino
proverb_en: It is not easy to show the way to a blind man.

proverb_native: Ad un colpo non cade à terra l’albero
proverb_en: The tree is not felled at one blow.

proverb_native: Ad un popolo pazzo, un prete spiritato
proverb_en: A mad parish, a mad priest.

proverb_native: Ad un uomo dabbene avanza la metà del cervello; ad un tristo non basta neanche tutto
proverb_en: For an honest man half his wits are enough; the whole is too little for a knave.

proverb_native: A fiume famoso non andar a pesca
proverb_en: Don’t go a-fishing to a famous stream.

proverb_native: A gatta che lecca spiedo non fidar arrosto
proverb_en: A cat that licks the spit is not to be trusted with roast meat.

proverb_native: A giovane cuor tutto è giuoco
proverb_en: To a young heart everything is sport.

proverb_native: A giovano soldato vecchio cavallo
proverb_en: An old horse for a young soldier.

proverb_native: Agli uomini ogni peccato mortale è veniale, alle donne ogni veniale è mortale
proverb_en: In men every mortal sin is venial, in women every venial sin is mortal.

proverb_native: A goccia à goccia s’incava la pietra
proverb_en: Drop by drop wears away the stone.

proverb_native: A gran ruscello passate l’ultimo
proverb_en: At a great river be the last to pass.

proverb_native: A gran salita gran discesa
proverb_en: The higher the rise the greater the fall.

proverb_native: A grassa cucina povertà è vicina
proverb_en: A fat kitchen is next door to poverty.

proverb_native: Ai mali estremi, estremi rimedi
proverb_en: For extreme ills extreme remedies.

proverb_native: A la barba de’ pazzi il barbier impara a radere
proverb_en: On a fool’s beard the barber learns to shave.

proverb_native: Albero spesso trapiantato mai di frutti è caricato
proverb_en: A tree often transplanted is never loaded with fruit.

proverb_native: Al bugiardo non si crede la verità
proverb_en: The liar is not believed when he speaks the truth.

proverb_native: Al cattivo cane tosto vien la coda
proverb_en: A cur’s tail grows fast.

proverb_native: Al confessore, medico, e avvocato, non tenere il ver celato
proverb_en: Hide not the truth from your confessor, your doctor, or your lawyer.

proverb_native: Al fin del giuoco si vede chi guadagna
proverb_en: At the end of the game we see who wins.

proverb_native: Al ladro fa paura anche un sorcio
proverb_en: The thief is frightened even by a mouse.

proverb_native: Al molino ed alla sposa, sempre manca qualche cosa
proverb_en: A mill and a wife are always in want of something.

proverb_native: Alla fama si va per varie strade
proverb_en: Various are the roads to fame.

proverb_native: All’amico mondagli il fico, all’inimico il persico
proverb_en: Peel a fig for your friend, a peach for your enemy.

proverb_native: Alla pace si può sacrificar tutto
proverb_en: Anything for a quiet life.

proverb_native: Alla pignatta che bolle non s’accostano le mosche
proverb_en: Flies don’t light on a boiling pot.

proverb_native: Alla porta chiusa il diavolo volge le spalle
proverb_en: The devil turns away from a closed door.

proverb_native: Alle belle donno le più volte toccano i brutti uomini
proverb_en: Handsome women generally fall to the lot of ugly men.

proverb_native: Alle volte più vale la feccia che il vino
proverb_en: Sometimes the lees are better than the wine.

proverb_native: Alle volte si dà un uovo per un bue
proverb_en: Sometimes an egg is given for an ox.

proverb_native: All’impossibile nessuno è tenuto
proverb_en: No one is bound to do impossibilities.

proverb_native: All’onor chi manca d’un momento, non lo ripara in anni cento
proverb_en: A hundred years cannot repair a moment’s loss of honour.

proverb_native: All’opera si conosce il maestro
proverb_en: The workman is known by his work.

proverb_native: All’orsa paiono belli i suoi orsatti
proverb_en: The she-bear thinks her cubs pretty.

proverb_native: Al lume di lucerna ogni rustica par bella
proverb_en: By lamplight every country wench seems handsome.

proverb_native: Al nimico che fugge il ponte d’oro
proverb_en: For the flying enemy a golden bridge.

proverb_native: Al più potente ceda il più prudente
proverb_en: The most prudent yields to the strongest.

proverb_native: Al più triste porco vien la meglior pera
proverb_en: The worst pig gets the best pear.

proverb_native: Al primo colpo non casca l’albero
proverb_en: The tree does not fall at the first stroke.

proverb_native: Altra cosa è il dire, altra il fare
proverb_en: Saying is one thing, doing another.

proverb_native: Altri tempi altri costumi
proverb_en: Different times different manners.

proverb_native: A Lucca ti vidi, a Pisa ti conobbi
proverb_en: I saw you at Lucca, I knew you at Pisa.

proverb_native: A lunga corda tira chi la morte altrui desidera
proverb_en: He hauls at a long rope who expects another’s death.

proverb_native: Al villano, se gli porgi il dito, ei prende la mano
proverb_en: Give a clown your finger he’ll grasp your fist.

proverb_native: Ama l’amico tuo col vizio suo
proverb_en: Love your friend with his faults.

proverb_native: A mal passo l’onore
proverb_en: At a dangerous passage yield precedence.

proverb_native: Amami, poco, ma continua
proverb_en: Love me little and love me long.

proverb_native: Amante non sia che coraggio non ha
proverb_en: Let him not be a lover who has not courage.

proverb_native: Amato non sarai, se à te solo penserai
proverb_en: You will not be loved if you think of yourself alone.

proverb_native: Ambasciator non porta pena
proverb_en: An ambassador beareth no blame.

proverb_native: Amicizia de’grandi vicinanza di leoni
proverb_en: The friendship of the great is fraternity with lions.

proverb_native: Amicizia riconciliata piaga mal saldata
proverb_en: Reconciled friendship is a wound ill salved.

proverb_native: Amico da sternuti, il più che se ne cava è un Dio ti aiuta
proverb_en: He’s a friend at sneezing-time,—the most that can be got from him is a “God bless you.”

proverb_native: Amico d’ognuno, amico di nessuno
proverb_en: Everybody’s friend, nobody’s friend.

proverb_native: Amico, e guardati
proverb_en: A friend, and look to thyself.

proverb_native: A molti puzza l’ambra
proverb_en: Many stop their noses at ambergris.

proverb_native: Amor dà per mercede gelosia e rotta fede
proverb_en: Love’s merchandise is jealousy and broken faith.

proverb_native: Amor è cieco ma vede da lontano
proverb_en: Love is blind but sees afar.

proverb_native: Amor è il vero prezzo con cui si compra amore
proverb_en: Love is the true price at which love is bought.

proverb_native: Amor e signoria non voglion compagnia
proverb_en: Love and lordship like not fellowship.

proverb_native: Amor non conosce travaglio
proverb_en: Love knows not labour.

proverb_native: Amor regge il suo regno senza spada
proverb_en: Love rules his kingdom without a sword.

proverb_native: Amor regge senza legge
proverb_en: Love rules without law.

proverb_native: Amor, tosse, e fumo malamente si nascondono
proverb_en: Love, a cough, and smoke, are hard to hide.

proverb_native: Amor vero non diventa mai canuto
proverb_en: True love never grows old.

proverb_native: Amor vuol fede, e fede vuol fermezza
proverb_en: Love demands faith, and faith firmness.

proverb_native: A nave rotta ogni vento è contrario
proverb_en: To a crazy ship every wind is contrary.

proverb_native: Anche delle pecore contate ne mangia il lupo
proverb_en: Even counted sheep are eaten by the wolf.

proverb_native: Anche delle volpi si pigliano
proverb_en: Even foxes are caught.

proverb_native: Anche i boschi hanno l’orecchie
proverb_en: Even woods have ears.

proverb_native: Anche il diritto ha bisogno d’aiuto
proverb_en: Even the just has need of help.

proverb_native: Anche il mar, che è si grande, si pacifica
proverb_en: Even the sea, great as it is, grows calm.

proverb_native: Anche il pazzo talvolta dice qualche parola da savio
proverb_en: Even the fool says a wise word sometimes.

proverb_native: Anche il sole passa sopra il fango, e non s’imbratta
proverb_en: The sun passes over filth and is not defiled.

proverb_native: Anche la mosca ha la sua collera
proverb_en: Even a fly has its anger.

proverb_native: Anche la rana morderebbe se avesse denti
proverb_en: Even a frog would bite if it had teeth.

proverb_native: Anch’io so menar le oche a bere quando piove
proverb_en: I too can lead the geese to water when it rains.

proverb_native: Anco gli apostoli ebbero un Giuda
proverb_en: Even among the apostles there was a Judas.

proverb_native: Anco il cane col dimenar la coda si guadagna le spese
proverb_en: Even the dog gets bread by wagging his tail.

proverb_native: Anco il cavallo si stanca, sebben ha quattro piedi
proverb_en: Even a horse, though he has four feet, stumbles.
