proverb_native: La paura guarda la vigna
proverb_en: Fear guards the vineyard.

proverb_native: La pelle d’asino è usa al bastone
proverb_en: The ass’s hide is used to the stick.

proverb_native: La più lunga strada è la più prossima a casa
proverb_en: The longest way round is the shortest way home.

proverb_native: La porta di dietro è quella che ruba la casa
proverb_en: The back door is the one that robs the house.

proverb_native: La prima scodella piace ad ognuno
proverb_en: The first dish pleases every one.

proverb_native: La Quaresima che par così lunga, alla tavola d’altri poco dura
proverb_en: Lent, which seems so long, is short at other men’s tables.

proverb_native: L’aquila non fa guerra ai ranocchi
proverb_en: The eagle does not war against frogs.

proverb_native: La rana non morde perchè non può
proverb_en: The frog does not bite because it cannot.

proverb_native: L’armi de’ poltroni non tagliano, nè forano
proverb_en: Cowards’ weapons neither cut nor pierce.

proverb_native: L’armi portan pace
proverb_en: Arms carry peace.

proverb_native: La roba non è di chi la fa, ma di chi la gode
proverb_en: Wealth is not his who makes it, but his who enjoys it.

proverb_native: Lascia la burla quando più piace
proverb_en: Drop the jest when it is most amusing.

proverb_native: La scusa non richiesta presuppone errore
proverb_en: An unasked for excuse infers transgression.

proverb_native: L’asino non conosce la coda, se non quando non l’ha più
proverb_en: The ass does not know the worth of his tail till he has lost it.

proverb_native: La superbia andò a cavallo, e tornò a piedi
proverb_en: Pride went out on horseback, and returned on foot.

proverb_native: La veste bianca non fa molinaro
proverb_en: The white coat does not make the miller.

proverb_native: La vita de’ medici, l’anima de’ preti, e la roba de’ legisti sono in gran pericolo
proverb_en: The lives of doctors, the souls of priests, and the property of lawyers, are in great danger.

proverb_native: La volontà è tutto
proverb_en: The will is everything.

proverb_native: La volpe consiglia le altre a tagliarsi la coda, per aver lasciata la propria al laccio
proverb_en: The fox advised the others to cut off their tails, because he had left his own in the trap.

proverb_native: La volpe dice che l’uva è agresta
proverb_en: The fox said the grapes were sour.

proverb_native: Le amicizie son a buon conto quando si comprano a sberrettate
proverb_en: Friendships are cheap when they can he bought by doffing the hat.

proverb_native: Le bestemmie fanno come le processioni: ritornano donde partirono
proverb_en: Curses are like processions: they return to whence they set out.

proverb_native: Le buone derrate vuotano la borsa
proverb_en: Good bargains empty the purse.

proverb_native: Le cattive nuove sono le prime
proverb_en: Bad news is the first to come.

proverb_native: Le cose non sono come sono, ma come si vedono
proverb_en: Things are not as they are, but as they are regarded.

proverb_native: Le disgrazie non vengon mai sole
proverb_en: Misfortunes never come single.

proverb_native: Le donne dicono sempre il vero, ma non lo dicono tutto intero
proverb_en: Women always speak the truth, but not the whole truth.

proverb_native: Le donne sanno un punto più del diavolo
proverb_en: Women know a point more than the devil.

proverb_native: Le donne si fanno rosse per non arrossire
proverb_en: Women rouge that they may not blush.

proverb_native: Le feste sono belle a casa d’altri
proverb_en: ’Tis good feasting in other men’s houses.

proverb_native: Legami mani e piei, e gettami tra’ miei
proverb_en: Tie me hand and foot and throw me among my own people.

proverb_native: Le insalate pazze, le fanno i savj
proverb_en: When wise men play mad pranks they do it with a vengeance.

proverb_native: L’elefante non sente il morso della pulce
proverb_en: The elephant does not feel a flea-bite.

proverb_native: Le leggi sono fatte pei tristi
proverb_en: Laws were made for rogues.

proverb_native: Le lucciole non sono lanterne
proverb_en: Glowworms are not lanterns.

proverb_native: Le minacce son arme del minacciato
proverb_en: Threats are arms for the threatened.

proverb_native: Le parole non pascono i gatti
proverb_en: Words won’t feed cats.

proverb_native: Le parole son femmine, e i fatti son maschi
proverb_en: Words are female, deeds are male.

proverb_native: Le piccole spese son quelle che vuotano la borsa
proverb_en: It is the petty expenses that empty the purse.

proverb_native: Le rose cascano, e le spine rimangono
proverb_en: The roses fall, and the thorns remain.

proverb_native: Le secchie si mettono a combattere col pozzo, e ne portano la testa rotta
proverb_en: The buckets take to fighting with the well, and get their heads broken.

proverb_native: Le siepi non hanno occhi, ma orecchie
proverb_en: Hedges have no eyes, but they have ears.

proverb_native: Le vesti degli avvocati sono foderate dell’ostinazion dei litiganti
proverb_en: Lawyers’ robes are lined with the obstinacy of suitors.

proverb_native: L’hai tolta bella? Tuo danno
proverb_en: You have married a beauty? So much the worse for you.

proverb_native: L’indugiare è pericoloso
proverb_en: Delays are dangerous.

proverb_native: L’infermo ha libertà di dire il tutto
proverb_en: The sick man is free to say all.

proverb_native: L’Inglese italianizzato, un diavolo incarnato
proverb_en: The Italianised Englishman is a devil incarnate.

proverb_native: L’occhio del padrone ingrassa il cavallo
proverb_en: The eye of the master fattens the horse.

proverb_native: Loda il mar, e tienti alla terra
proverb_en: Praise the sea and keep on land.

proverb_native: Lontano dagli occhi, lontano dal cuore
proverb_en: Far from the eyes, far from the heart.

proverb_native: L’oro non compra tutto
proverb_en: Gold does not buy everything.

proverb_native: Lo sparagno è il primo guadagno
proverb_en: Savings are the first gain.

proverb_native: L’ospite, ed il pesce dopo tre dì rincresce
proverb_en: A guest and a fish stink in three days.

proverb_native: L’ultimo vestito ce lo fanno senza tasche
proverb_en: Our last garment is made without pockets.

proverb_native: Lunga lingua, corta mano
proverb_en: Long tongue, short hand.

proverb_native: Lupo non mangia lupo
proverb_en: Wolves don’t eat wolves.

proverb_native: Mai si fa cosa ben in fretta, che il fuggir la peste e i rumori, e pigliar pulci
proverb_en: Nothing is ever well done in a hurry, except flying from the plague or from quarrels, and catching fleas.

proverb_native: Mai si serra una porta, che non si apra un’altra
proverb_en: One door never shuts but another opens.

proverb_native: Male in vacche, e peggio in buoi
proverb_en: Ill in kine and worse in beeves.

proverb_native: Mangiando viene l’appetito
proverb_en: Appetite comes in eating.

proverb_native: Mangia tanto una rozza quanto un buon cavallo
proverb_en: A jade eats as much as a good horse.

proverb_native: Matta è quella pecora che si confessa al lupo
proverb_en: ’Tis a silly sheep that confesses to the wolf.

proverb_native: Medico, cura te stesso
proverb_en: Physician, heal thyself.

proverb_native: Meglio è aver il marito senza amore che con gelosia
proverb_en: It is better to have a husband without love than jealous.

proverb_native: Meglio è poco che niente
proverb_en: Better aught than nought.

proverb_native: Meglio tardi che mai
proverb_en: Better late than never.

proverb_native: Meglio un prossimo vicino che un lontano cugino
proverb_en: Better a near neighbour than a distant cousin.

proverb_native: Mentre l’erba cresce il cavallo muore di fame
proverb_en: Whilst the grass grows the steed starves.

proverb_native: Mettersi prima il giuppone che la camicia
proverb_en: To put on one’s doublet before one’s shirt.

proverb_native: Mille verisimili non fanno un vero
proverb_en: A thousand probabilities do not make one truth.

proverb_native: Misura tre volte, e taglia una
proverb_en: Measure three times and cut once.

proverb_native: Molti si fan scrupolo di sputare in chiesa, e poi inbrattano l’altare
proverb_en: Many scruple to spit in church, and afterwards defile the altar.

proverb_native: Molti son bravi quando l’inimico fugge
proverb_en: Many are brave when the enemy flies.

proverb_native: Molti voglion l’albero che fingon di rifiutar il frutto
proverb_en: Many desire the tree who pretend to refuse the fruit.

proverb_native: Molto fumo, e poco arrosto
proverb_en: Much smoke, and little roast.

proverb_native: Monaco vagabondo non disse mai lode del suo monastero
proverb_en: A vagabond monk never spoke well of his convent.

proverb_native: Morso di pecora non passa mai la pelle
proverb_en: A sheep’s bite is never more than skin deep.

proverb_native: Morta la bestia, morto il veleno
proverb_en: The beast once dead, the venom is dead.

proverb_native: Morto io, morto ognun, ed il porco
proverb_en: When I’m dead, everybody’s dead, and the pig too.

proverb_native: Muove la coda il cane, non per te, ma per il pane
proverb_en: The dog wags his tail, not for you, but for bread.

proverb_native: Muraglia bianca, carta di matto
proverb_en: A white wall is the fool’s paper.

proverb_native: N’ammazza più la gola che la spada
proverb_en: Gluttony kills more than the sword.

proverb_native: Natale non viene che una volta l’anno
proverb_en: Christmas comes but once a year.

proverb_native: Necessità non ha legge
proverb_en: Necessity has no law.

proverb_native: Nè donna, nè tela a lume di candela
proverb_en: Neither women nor linen by candlelight.

proverb_native: Nei piccioli sacchi sono le migliori spezie
proverb_en: The best spices are in small bags.

proverb_native: Nella coda sta il veleno
proverb_en: The venom is in the tail.

proverb_native: Nella guerra d’amor vince chi fugge
proverb_en: In the war of love who flies conquers.

proverb_native: Nella prosperità non fumano gli altari
proverb_en: In prosperity no altars smoke.

proverb_native: Nella veste più fina fa maggior danno la tarmina
proverb_en: The moth does most mischief to the finest garment.

proverb_native: Nella zuffa il debole è forte
proverb_en: In the fray the weak are strong.

proverb_native: Nessun buon avvocato piatisce mai
proverb_en: No good lawyer ever goes to law himself.

proverb_native: Nessun buon medico piglia mai medicine
proverb_en: No good doctor ever takes physic.

proverb_native: Nessun diventò mai povero per far limosina
proverb_en: No one ever became poor through giving alms.

proverb_native: Nessuno si pentì mai d’aver taciuto
proverb_en: No one ever repented of having held his tongue.

proverb_native: Nessun sente da che parte preme la scarpa, se non chi se la calza
proverb_en: No one perceives where the shoe pinches but he who wears it.

proverb_native: Niuna maraviglia dura più che tre giorni
proverb_en: No wonder lasts more than three days.

proverb_native: Non basta di saper rubare, bisogna anche saper nascondere
proverb_en: It is not enough to know how to steal, one must know also how to conceal.

proverb_native: Non bisogna fasciarsi il capo prima di romperselo
proverb_en: There is no need to bind up one’s head before it is broken.

proverb_native: Non bisogna ripescare tutte le secchie che cascano
proverb_en: It is not necessary to fish up every bucket that falls into the well.

proverb_native: Non c’è amor senza gelosia
proverb_en: There is no love without jealousy.

proverb_native: Non c’è il peggior frutto di quello che non matura mai
proverb_en: There is no worse fruit than that which never ripens.

proverb_native: Non ci è fumo senza fuoco
proverb_en: There’s no smoke without fire.

proverb_native: Non ci è il più cattivo sordo di quel che non vuol udire
proverb_en: None so deaf as he that will not hear.

proverb_native: Non ci è la peggior burla che la vera
proverb_en: There is no worse joke than a true one.

proverb_native: Non credere al santo se non fa miracoli
proverb_en: Don’t believe in the saint unless he works miracles.

proverb_native: Non dar del pane al cane ogni volta che dimena la coda
proverb_en: Do not give the dog bread every time he wags his tail.

proverb_native: Non darebbe il coltello al diavolo per scannarsi
proverb_en: He would not give the devil a knife to cut his throat.

proverb_native: Non dir mal dell’anno finchè passato non sia
proverb_en: Speak not ill of the year until it is past.

proverb_native: Non è bello quel che è bello, ma quel che piace
proverb_en: Handsome is not what is handsome, but what pleases.

proverb_native: Non è buon murator chi rifiuta pietra alcuna
proverb_en: He is not a good mason who refuses any stone.

proverb_native: Non è in alcun luogo chi è per tutto
proverb_en: He is in no place who is everywhere.

proverb_native: Non entra a messa la campana, e pur ognuno ci chiama
proverb_en: The bell does not go to mass, and yet calls every one to it.

proverb_native: Non è onore all’aquila il vincer la colomba
proverb_en: It is no honour for an eagle to vanquish a dove.

proverb_native: Non è scappato chi si strascina la catena dietro
proverb_en: He is not free who drags his chain after him.

proverb_native: Non è tempo da giuocar a scacchi quando la casa brucia
proverb_en: It is no time to play chess when the house is on fire.

proverb_native: Non è tutto butiro che fa la vacca
proverb_en: All is not butter that comes from the cow.

proverb_native: Non è uomo chi non sa dir di nò
proverb_en: He is not a man who cannot say no.

proverb_native: Non si fa ber l’asino quando non ha sete
proverb_en: There’s no making the ass drink when he is not thirsty.

proverb_native: Non fu mai cacciator gatto che miagola
proverb_en: Never was a mewing cat a good mouser.

proverb_native: Non fu mai così bella scarpa che non diventasse brutta ciabatta
proverb_en: There never was a shoe however handsome that did not become an ugly slipper.

proverb_native: Non fu mai sacco sì pieno che non v’entrasse ancor un grano
proverb_en: A sack was never so full but it could hold another grain.

proverb_native: Non fu mai vista capra morta di fame
proverb_en: No one ever saw a goat dead of hunger.

proverb_native: Non giova a dire, per tal via non passerò, nè di tal acqua beverò
proverb_en: There is no use in saying, I will not go such a way, nor drink of such a water.

proverb_native: Non giudicar la nave stando in terra
proverb_en: Do not judge of the ship from the land.

proverb_native: Non gridar i pesci fritti prima d’esser presi
proverb_en: Don’t cry fried fish before they are caught.

proverb_native: Non istanno bene due galli in un cortile
proverb_en: Two cocks in one yard do not agree.

proverb_native: Non ischerzar coll’orso, se non vuoi esser morso
proverb_en: Don’t play with the bear if you don’t want to be bit.

proverb_native: Non lasciar chiodo che non si ributti
proverb_en: Leave no nail unclenched.

proverb_native: Non mi dir oliva prima che mi vedi colta
proverb_en: Call me not olive before you see me gathered.

proverb_native: Non mi punse mai scorpione che io non mi medicassi col suo olio
proverb_en: A scorpion never stung me but I cured myself with its grease.

proverb_native: Non mordere se non sai se è pietra o pane
proverb_en: Don’t bite till you know whether it is bread or a stone.

proverb_native: Non mostrar mai nè il fondo della tua borsa, nè del tuo animo
proverb_en: Never let the bottom of your purse or of your mind be seen.

proverb_native: Non ogni fiore fa buon odore
proverb_en: It is not every flower that smells sweet.

proverb_native: Non ogni giorno è festa
proverb_en: Every day is not a holiday.

proverb_native: Non ogni parola vuol risposta
proverb_en: Not every word requires an answer.

proverb_native: Non pensa il cor tutto quel che dice la bocca
proverb_en: The heart does not think all the mouth says.

proverb_native: Non pianse mai uno che non ridesse un altro
proverb_en: One never wept but another laughed.

proverb_native: Non puoi mal fare a nave rotta
proverb_en: You cannot damage a wrecked ship.

proverb_native: Non può uscir del sacco se non quel che ci è
proverb_en: Nothing can come out of a sack but what is in it.

proverb_native: Non resta mai carne in beccheria per trista ch’ella sia
proverb_en: No meat ever remains in the shambles however bad it may be.

proverb_native: Non ricordare la croce al diavolo
proverb_en: Don’t mention the cross to the devil.

proverb_native: Non ricordar il capestro in casa dell’impiccato
proverb_en: Never speak of a rope in the house of one who was hanged.

proverb_native: Non ride sempre la moglie del ladro
proverb_en: The thief’s wife does not always laugh.

proverb_native: Non scortica la lingua il parlar dolce
proverb_en: Smooth words do not flay the tongue.

proverb_native: Non si crede al santo se non fa miracoli
proverb_en: The saint has no believers unless he works miracles.

proverb_native: Non si deve far male per trarne bene
proverb_en: Never do evil that good may come of it.

proverb_native: Non si dice mai tanto una cosa che non sia qualche cosa
proverb_en: A thing is never much talked of but there is some truth in it.

proverb_native: Non si fa mantello per un’acqua sola
proverb_en: A cloak is not made for a single shower of rain.

proverb_native: Non si offende mai cane gettandogli le ossa
proverb_en: A dog is never offended at being pelted with bones.

proverb_native: Non si può ad un tempo bere e fischiare
proverb_en: One cannot drink and whistle at the same time.

proverb_native: Non si può cavar sangue dalla rapa
proverb_en: You cannot draw blood from a turnip.

proverb_native: Non si può entrare in Paradiso a dispetto de’santi
proverb_en: One can’t enter Paradise in spite of the saints.

proverb_native: Non si può far andar un molino a vento co’ mantici
proverb_en: There’s no turning a windmill with a pair of bellows.

proverb_native: Non si serra mai una porta che non se n’apra un’altra
proverb_en: One door never shuts but another opens.

proverb_native: Non si va in Paradiso in carrozza
proverb_en: There’s no getting to heaven in a coach.

proverb_native: Non si vorria esser solo in Paradiso
proverb_en: One would not be alone in Paradise.

proverb_native: Non son tutti santi quelli che vanno in chiesa
proverb_en: All are not saints who go to church.

proverb_native: Non svegliare il can che dorme
proverb_en: Wake not a sleeping dog.

proverb_native: Non ti metter in dito anello troppo stretto
proverb_en: Don’t put too tight a ring on your finger.

proverb_native: Non tuona mai che non piova
proverb_en: It never thunders but it rains.

proverb_native: Non tutte le pecore sono per il lupo
proverb_en: All the sheep are not for the wolf.

proverb_native: Non tutti dormono quelli che hanno serrati gli occhi
proverb_en: Not all are asleep who have their eyes shut.

proverb_native: Non v’è barba al mondo così ben rasa, che un altro barbier non ci trovi da radere
proverb_en: There is no beard so well shaven but another barber will find something more to shave from it.

proverb_native: Non vender la pelle dell’orso prima di pigliarlo
proverb_en: Don’t sell the bearskin before you have caught the bear.

proverb_native: Non v’è peggior burla della vera
proverb_en: There’s no worse joke than a true one.

proverb_native: Non v’è peggior ladro d’un cattivo libro
proverb_en: There is no worse thief than a bad book.

proverb_native: Non v’è rosa senza spina
proverb_en: No rose without a thorn.

proverb_native: Non v’è sì tristo cane che non meni la coda
proverb_en: There is no dog, be he ever so wicked, but wags his tail.

proverb_native: Non vi è abbastanza se niente avanza
proverb_en: There is never enough where nought is left.

proverb_native: Non vien dì che non venga sera
proverb_en: No day but has its evening.

proverb_native: Novella trista arriva presto
proverb_en: Ill news comes apace.

proverb_native: Nulla nuova, buona nuova
proverb_en: No news is good news.

proverb_native: Nuova rete non piglia uccello vecchio
proverb_en: Old birds are not caught with new nets.

proverb_native: O bene o male, tutti dobbiamo vivere
proverb_en: Good or bad we must all live.

proverb_native: Occhio che non vede, cuor che non duole
proverb_en: What the eye sees not the heart rues not.

proverb_native: Odi l’altra parte, e credi poco
proverb_en: Hear the other side, and believe little.

proverb_native: Odio ricominciato è peggio che prima
proverb_en: Hatred renewed is worse than at first.

proverb_native: Odi, vedi, e taci, se vuoi viver in pace
proverb_en: Hear, see, and say nothing, if you would live in peace.

proverb_native: Offerir molto è spezie di negare
proverb_en: Extravagant offers are a kind of denial.

proverb_native: Ogni acqua estingue il fuoco
proverb_en: Any water will put out fire.

proverb_native: Ogni acqua va al mare
proverb_en: All water runs to the sea.

proverb_native: Ogni bottega ha la sua malizia
proverb_en: Every shop has its trick.

proverb_native: Ogni cane è leone a casa sua
proverb_en: Every dog is a lion at home.

proverb_native: Ogni cosa è d’ogni anno
proverb_en: Everything is of every year.

proverb_native: Ogni cosa ha cagione
proverb_en: There is a cause for all things.

proverb_native: Ogni cosa serve a qualche cosa
proverb_en: Everything is good for something.

proverb_native: Ogni cosa si sopporta eccetto il buon tempo
proverb_en: Everything may be borne except good fortune.

proverb_native: Ogni cosa vuol principio
proverb_en: Everything must have a beginning.

proverb_native: Ogni dieci anni un uomo ha bisogno dell’altro
proverb_en: Every ten years one man has need of another.

proverb_native: Ogni dì non è festa
proverb_en: Every day is not a holiday.

proverb_native: Ogni disuguaglianza amore agguaglia
proverb_en: Love levels all inequalities.

proverb_native: Ogni dì vien sera
proverb_en: Every day has its night.

proverb_native: Ogni lucciola non è fuoco
proverb_en: Every glowworm is not a fire.

proverb_native: Ogni medaglia ha il suo rovescio
proverb_en: Every medal has its reverse.

proverb_native: Ogni monte ha la sua valle
proverb_en: Every hill has its valley.

proverb_native: Ogni nave fa acqua: quale a mezzo, quale a proda, e quale in sentina
proverb_en: All ships leak: some amidships, some in the bows, some in the hold.

proverb_native: Ogni pazzo è savio quando tace
proverb_en: Every fool is wise when he holds his tongue.

proverb_native: Ogni pazzo vuol dar consiglio
proverb_en: Every fool wants to give advice.

proverb_native: Ogni pignattaro loda la sua pignatta, e più quella che tiene rotta
proverb_en: Every potter praises his pot, and most of all the one that is cracked.

proverb_native: Ogni promessa è debito
proverb_en: Every promise is a debt.

proverb_native: Ogni rosa ha la sua spina
proverb_en: Every rose has its thorn.

proverb_native: Ogni scusa è buona pur che vaglia
proverb_en: Any excuse is good if it hold good.

proverb_native: Ogni tua guisa non sappia la tua camicia
proverb_en: Let not your shirt know all your thoughts.

proverb_native: Ogni uomo ha buona moglie e cattiva arte
proverb_en: Every man has a good wife and a bad trade.

proverb_native: Ogni vento non scuote il noce
proverb_en: Every wind does not shake down the nut.

proverb_native: Ogni vero non è buono a dire
proverb_en: Every truth is not good to be told.

proverb_native: Ogni vite vuole il suo palo
proverb_en: Every vine must have its stake.

proverb_native: Ogni volpe abbia cura della sua coda
proverb_en: Let every fox take care of his own tail.

proverb_native: Ognun biasima il suo mestiere
proverb_en: Every one finds fault with his own trade.

proverb_native: Ognun crede di aver più cervello che non ha, e meno quattrini
proverb_en: Every one gives himself credit for more brains than he has, and less money.

proverb_native: Ognuno all’arte sua, e il bue all’aratro
proverb_en: Every one to his own calling, and the ox to the plough.

proverb_native: Ognuno ama la giustizia a casa altrui: a nessun piace a casa sua
proverb_en: Every one likes justice in another’s house, none in his own.

proverb_native: Ognuno loda il proprio santo
proverb_en: Every one praises his own saint.

proverb_native: Ognuno sa dove la scarpa lo stringe
proverb_en: Every one knows where his shoe pinches him.

proverb_native: Ognuno si crede senza vizio, perchè non ha quelli degli altri
proverb_en: Every one thinks himself without sin because he has not those of others.

proverb_native: Ognun per sè, e Dio per tutti
proverb_en: Every one for himself and God for us all.

proverb_native: Ognun sa navigar per il buon tempo
proverb_en: Every one can navigate in fine weather.

proverb_native: Ognun si pari le mosche con la sua coda
proverb_en: Let every one keep off the flies with his own tail.

proverb_native: Ognun tira l’acqua al suo molino
proverb_en: Every one draws the water to his own mill.

proverb_native: Ognun va col suo sacco al molino
proverb_en: Every one goes with his own sack to the mill.

proverb_native: Ombra di signore, cappel di matto
proverb_en: The shadow of a lord is a cap for a fool.

proverb_native: Onor di bocca assai giova, e poco costa
proverb_en: Lip courtesy pleases much and costs little.

proverb_native: Oro è che oro vale
proverb_en: That is gold which is worth gold.

proverb_native: Oro non è tutto quel che risplende
proverb_en: All is not gold that glitters.

proverb_native: O sassi o pani, bisogna aver qualcosa in man pei cani
proverb_en: Stones or bread, one must have something in hand for the dogs.

proverb_native: Pagar uno della sua moneta
proverb_en: To pay one in his own coin.

proverb_native: Parente, o non parente, mal per quel che non ha niente
proverb_en: Kin or no kin, woe to him who has nothing.

proverb_native: Partoriscono i monti, e nasce un topo
proverb_en: The mountains are in labour, and bring forth a mouse.

proverb_native: Passa la festa, ed il matto resta
proverb_en: The feast passes and the fool remains.

proverb_native: Passato il fiume, è scordato il santo
proverb_en: The river passed the saint forgotten.

proverb_native: Passato il pericolo, gabbato il santo
proverb_en: The danger past, the saint cheated.

proverb_native: Passo a passo si va a Roma
proverb_en: Step by step one goes to Rome.

proverb_native: Patto chiaro, amico caro
proverb_en: A clear bargain, a dear friend.

proverb_native: Pazienza! disse il lupo all’asino
proverb_en: Patience! said the wolf to the ass.

proverb_native: Pazzo chi perde il volo per lo sbalzo
proverb_en: He is a fool who loses the flight for the leap.

proverb_native: Pazzo è chi non sa da che parte vien il vento
proverb_en: He is a fool who does not know from what quarter the wind blows.

proverb_native: Pazzo è colui che di quattro cose si vanta: di buon vino, di buon cavallo, di bella moglie, di danari assai
proverb_en: He is a fool who boasts of four things: that he has good wine, a good horse, a handsome wife, and plenty of money.

proverb_native: Pazzo è quel prete che biasima le sue reliquie
proverb_en: That priest is a fool who decries his relics.

proverb_native: Peccato celato, mezzo perdonato
proverb_en: A sin concealed is half forgiven.

proverb_native: Peccato confessato è mezzo perdonato
proverb_en: A sin confessed is half forgiven.

proverb_native: Pecora che bela perde il boccone
proverb_en: The sheep that bleats loses a mouthful.

proverb_native: Pecora mansueta da ogni agnello è tettata
proverb_en: A mild sheep is sucked by every lamb.

proverb_native: Peggio è la paura della guerra, che la guerra stessa
proverb_en: The fear of war is worse than war itself.

proverb_native: Pegno che mangia niuno lo pigli
proverb_en: No one should take in an eating pawn (or pledge).

proverb_native: Pela la gazza, e non la far strillare
proverb_en: Pluck the magpie, and don’t make her scream.

proverb_native: Pensa molto, parla poco, e scrivi meno
proverb_en: Think much, speak little, and write less.

proverb_native: Pensano gl’innamorati cho gli altri siano ciechi
proverb_en: Lovers think others are blind.

proverb_native: Per amistà conservare, muri bisogna piantare
proverb_en: To preserve friendship one must build walls.

proverb_native: Perchè vada il carro, bisogna unger le ruote
proverb_en: To make the cart go you must grease the wheels.

proverb_native: Perde le lagrime chi piange avanti al giudice
proverb_en: He wastes his tears who weeps before the judge.

proverb_native: Per dir gran mercè, la mia gatta morì
proverb_en: Thank you, pretty pussy, was the death of my cat.

proverb_native: Per diventar ricco in questo mondo, non ci vuol altro che voltar le spalle a Dio
proverb_en: To become rich in this world, it needs only to turn one’s back on God.

proverb_native: Per picciola cagione pigliasi il lupo il montone
proverb_en: On very small pretext the wolf seizes the sheep.

proverb_native: Per più strade si va a Roma
proverb_en: There are many roads to Rome.

proverb_native: Per saper troppo, perdè la sua coda la volpe
proverb_en: Through being too knowing the fox lost his tail.

proverb_native: Per tutto sono de’ tristi e de’ buoni
proverb_en: There are good and bad everywhere.

proverb_native: Per un monaco non si lascia di far l’abbate
proverb_en: The election of the abbot is not stopped for want of a monk.

proverb_native: Per un orecchio entra, per l’altro esce
proverb_en: In at one ear, out at the other.

proverb_native: Piaga antiveduta assai men duole
proverb_en: A wound foreseen pains the less.

proverb_native: Piaga per allentar d’arco non sana
proverb_en: Unbending the bow does not cure the wound.

proverb_native: Pian, barbiere, che l’acqua scotta
proverb_en: Softly, barber, the water scalds.

proverb_native: Piano, che non si levi la polvere
proverb_en: Softly, don’t raise a dust.

proverb_native: Piccola favilla accende gran fuoco
proverb_en: A little spark kindles a great fire.

proverb_native: Piccola pietra rovescia gran carro
proverb_en: A little stone overturns a great cart.

proverb_native: Piccola pioggia fa cessar gran vento
proverb_en: Small rain lays a great wind.

proverb_native: Piega l’albero quando è giovane
proverb_en: Bend the tree while it is young.

proverb_native: Pietra mossa non fa muschio
proverb_en: A rolling stone gathers no moss.

proverb_native: Pigliamo prima l’orso, e poi vendiamo la pelle
proverb_en: Let us first catch the bear and then sell its skin.

proverb_native: Pigliar due colombi a una fava
proverb_en: To catch two pigeons with one bean.

proverb_native: Pigliar la lepre col carro
proverb_en: To catch a hare with a cart.

proverb_native: Pignatta rotta non cade mai da uncino
proverb_en: A cracked pot never fell off the hook.

proverb_native: Più che il martello dura l’incudine
proverb_en: The anvil lasts longer than the hammer.

proverb_native: Più lungo d’un dì senza pane
proverb_en: Longer than a day without bread.

proverb_native: Più mi tocca la camicia che la gonnella
proverb_en: Near is my petticoat, but nearer is my smock.

proverb_native: Più ombra che frutto fanno gli arberi grandi
proverb_en: Large trees give more shade than fruit.

proverb_native: Più pazzi che quei da Zago, che davan del letame al campanile perchè crescesse
proverb_en: Greater fools than they of Zago, who dunged the steeple to make it grow.

proverb_native: Più sa il matto in casa sua che, il savio in casa d’altri
proverb_en: The fool knows more in his own house than the sage in other men’s.

proverb_native: Più son i minacciati che gli uccisi
proverb_en: There are more threatened than slain.

proverb_native: Più spegne una buona parola, che un secchio d’acqua
proverb_en: One good word quenches more heat than a bucket of water.

proverb_native: Più sventurato che i cani in chiesa
proverb_en: More unlucky than dogs in church.

proverb_native: Più tosto si arriva un bugiardo, che uno zoppo
proverb_en: A liar is sooner caught than a cripple.

proverb_native: Piuttosto cappello in mano, che mano alla borsa
proverb_en: Rather hat in hand than hand in purse.

proverb_native: Piuttosto un asino che porti, che un cavallo che butti in terra
proverb_en: Rather an ass that carries than a horse that throws.

proverb_native: Più vale guadagnar in loto, che perder in oro
proverb_en: Better gain in mud than lose in gold.

proverb_native: Più vale il fumo di casa mia, che il fuoco dell’altrui
proverb_en: The smoke of my own house is better than another man’s fire.

proverb_native: Più vede un occhio del padrone che quattro del servitore
proverb_en: One eye of the master sees more than four eyes of his servants.

proverb_native: Placato il cane, il rubar è facile
proverb_en: It is easy robbing when the dog is quieted.

proverb_native: Poco fiele fa amaro molto miele
proverb_en: A little gall makes a great deal of honey bitter.

proverb_native: Poichè la casa brucia, io mi scalderò
proverb_en: Since the house is on fire I will warm myself at the blaze.

proverb_native: Porco pigro non mangia pere mature
proverb_en: The lazy pig does not eat ripe pears.

proverb_native: Povero come un topo di chiesa
proverb_en: As poor as a church mouse.

proverb_native: Povertà non ha parenti
proverb_en: Poverty has no kin.

proverb_native: Prega il villano, il mercato è disfatto
proverb_en: Entreat the churl and the bargain is broken off.

proverb_native: Preso il partito, cassato l’affanno
proverb_en: Once resolved, the trouble is over.

proverb_native: Presto e bene non si conviene
proverb_en: Quick and well don’t agree.

proverb_native: Preti, frati, monache e polli non si trovan mai satolli
proverb_en: Priests, friars, nuns, and chickens never have enough.

proverb_native: Prometter più carri che buoi
proverb_en: To promise more carts than oxen.

proverb_native: Protestare e dare del capo nel muro, lo può fare ognuno
proverb_en: To protest and knock one’s head against the wall is what everybody can do.

proverb_native: Provocar il cavallo a correr per il piano
proverb_en: To spur a horse on level ground.

proverb_native: Qual è l’amante, tal è l’amata
proverb_en: As is the lover so is the beloved.

proverb_native: Qual figlia vuoi, tal moglie piglia
proverb_en: As you would have a daughter so choose a wife.

proverb_native: Quando Dio non vuole, i santi non possono
proverb_en: When God will not the saints cannot.

proverb_native: Quando è poco pan in tavola, mettine assai nella scodella
proverb_en: When there is little bread at table put plenty on your plate.

proverb_native: Quando i furbi vanno in processione, il diavolo porta la croce
proverb_en: When rogues go in procession the devil carries the cross.

proverb_native: Quando il leone è morto, le lepri gli saltano addosso
proverb_en: When the lion is dead the hares jump upon his carcase.

proverb_native: Quando il sole ti splende, non ti dèi curar della luna
proverb_en: When the sun shines on thee, thou needest not care for the moon.

proverb_native: Quando il tuo diavol nacque, il mio andava a scuola
proverb_en: When your devil was born, mine was going to school.

proverb_native: Quando i molinari fanno romore, tu lega i sacchi
proverb_en: When the millers are making an uproar, do you tie up your sacks.

proverb_native: Quando la cosa va bene, è buono dar consiglio
proverb_en: It is easy to give advice when all goes well.

proverb_native: Quando la donna regna, il diavolo governa
proverb_en: When woman reigns the devil governs.

proverb_native: Quando la gatta non è in paese, i topi ballano
proverb_en: When the cat’s away the rats dance.

proverb_native: Quando la gatta non v’è, i sorci ballano
proverb_en: When the cat’s away the mice dance.

proverb_native: Quando la pera è matura, convien ch’ella caggia
proverb_en: When the pear is ripe it must fall.

proverb_native: Quando la volpe predica, guardatevi, galline
proverb_en: When the fox preaches, take care of yourselves, hens.

proverb_native: Quando l’incendio è nel vicinato, porta l’acqua a casa tua
proverb_en: When there is a fire in the neighbourhood carry water to your own house.

proverb_native: Quando non c’è, perde la chiesa
proverb_en: When there is nothing the church loses.

proverb_native: Quando puoi aver del bene, pigliane
proverb_en: Never refuse a good offer.

proverb_native: Quando tuona, il ladro divien uomo dabbene
proverb_en: When it thunders, the thief becomes honest.

proverb_native: Quando tutti ti dicono briaco, va a dormire
proverb_en: When everybody says you are drunk, go to sleep.

proverb_native: Quando tu vedi il lupo, non ne cercar le pedate
proverb_en: When you see the wolf, do not look for his track.

proverb_native: Quando viene la fortuna, apri le porte
proverb_en: When fortune comes, open your doors.

proverb_native: Quanto più la volpe è maladetta, tanto maggior preda fa
proverb_en: The more the fox is cursed, the more prey he catches.

proverb_native: Quanto più si frega la schiena al gatto, più leva la coda
proverb_en: The more you stroke the cat’s back the more she sets up her tail.

proverb_native: Quattrini e amicizia rompon le braccia alla giustizia
proverb_en: Money and friendship break the arms of justice.

proverb_native: Quattrino risparmiato, due volte guadagnato
proverb_en: A farthing saved is twice earned.

proverb_native: Quel che è fatto non si può disfare
proverb_en: What’s done can’t be undone.

proverb_native: Quel che fa il pazzo all’ultimo, lo fa il savio alla prima
proverb_en: A wise man does at first what a fool must do at last.

proverb_native: Quel che non ammazza, ingrassa
proverb_en: What does not poison, fattens.

proverb_native: Quel che non è stato, può essere
proverb_en: What has not been, may be.

proverb_native: Quel che non puoi aver, biasima
proverb_en: What you can’t have, abuse.

proverb_native: Quel che pare burla, ben sovente è vero
proverb_en: Many a true word is spoken in jest.

proverb_native: Quel che ripara il freddo, ripara il caldo
proverb_en: What keeps out the cold keeps out the heat.

proverb_native: Quello che costa poco, si stima meno
proverb_en: What costs little is little esteemed.

proverb_native: Quello è dolce a ricordare, che fu duro a sopportare
proverb_en: That is pleasant to remember which was hard to endure.

proverb_native: Ragazzi savj e vecchi matti non furon mai buoni a nulla
proverb_en: Wise lads and old fools were never good for anything.

proverb_native: Ragghio d’asino non arriva al cielo
proverb_en: The braying of an ass does not reach heaven.

proverb_native: Rete nuova non piglia uccello vecchio
proverb_en: A new net won’t catch an old bird.

proverb_native: Ride bene chi ride l’ultimo
proverb_en: He laughs well who laughs last.

proverb_native: Ritornan molti dalla guerra che non sanno raccontar la battaglia
proverb_en: Many return from the war who cannot give an account of the battle.

proverb_native: Romper la casa per vender il calcinaccio
proverb_en: To pull down the house for the sake of the mortar.

proverb_native: Rompe una pietra una goccia d’acqua
proverb_en: A drop of water breaks a stone.

proverb_native: Rotta la testa, si mette la celata
proverb_en: When his head is broken he puts on his helmet.

proverb_native: Rubar il porco, e darne i piedi per l’amor di Dio
proverb_en: To steal the pig, and give away the pettitoes for God’s sake.

proverb_native: Sacco pieno rizza l’orecchio
proverb_en: A full sack pricks up its ear.

proverb_native: Sacco rotto non tien miglio, il pover uom non va a consiglio
proverb_en: A ragged sack holds no grain, a poor man is not taken into counsel.

proverb_native: Sacco vuoto non sta ritto
proverb_en: An empty sack won’t stand upright.

proverb_native: Sa dove il diavolo tien la coda
proverb_en: He knows where the devil has his tail.

proverb_native: Sa meglio i fatti suoi un matto, che un savio quei degli altri
proverb_en: A fool knows his own business better than a wise man knows that of others.

proverb_native: San Francesco prima si faceva la barba per sè, poi la faceva a’ suoi frati
proverb_en: St. Francis shaved himself first, and then he shaved his brethren.

proverb_native: Sanità senza quattrini è mezza malattia
proverb_en: Health without money is a half-malady.

proverb_native: S’annegherebbe in un cucchiar d’acqua
proverb_en: He would drown in a spoonful of water.

proverb_native: Sanno più un savio ed un matto, che un savio solo
proverb_en: A wise man and a fool together, know more than a wise man alone.

proverb_native: Sa più il papa e un contadino che il papa solo
proverb_en: The pope and a peasant know more than the pope alone.

proverb_native: Savie all’impensata, alla pensata pazze son le donne
proverb_en: Women are wise impromptu, fools on reflection.

proverb_native: Savio è colui che impara a spese altrui
proverb_en: He is wise who learns at another’s cost.

proverb_native: Schiaffo minacciato non è mai ben dato
proverb_en: A threatened buffet is never well given.

proverb_native: Sciocco è chi pensa che un altro non pensi
proverb_en: He is a fool who thinks that another does not think.

proverb_native: Scoprire un altare per ricoprirne un altro
proverb_en: To strip one altar to cover another.

proverb_native: Scorticar il cane scorticato
proverb_en: To flay the flayed dog.

proverb_native: Sdegno cresce amore
proverb_en: Anger increases love.

proverb_native: Sdegno d’amante poco dura
proverb_en: A lover’s anger is short-lived.

proverb_native: Se ben ho perso l’anello, ho pur anche le dita
proverb_en: If I have lost the ring I still have the fingers.

proverb_native: Segreto confidato non è più segreto
proverb_en: A secret imparted is no longer a secret.

proverb_native: Se il giovane sapesse, se il vecchio potesse, e’ non c’è cosa che non si facesse
proverb_en: If the young man knew, if the old man could, there is nothing but would be done.

proverb_native: Se io andassi al mare, lo troverei secco
proverb_en: If I went to sea I should find it dry.

proverb_native: Se la moglie pecca, non è il marito innocente
proverb_en: If the wife sins the husband is not innocent.

proverb_native: Se la superbia fosse arte, quanti dottori avremmo
proverb_en: If pride were an art, how many doctors we should have.

proverb_native: Se ’l sol mi splende, non curo la luna
proverb_en: If the sun shines on me I care not for the moon.

proverb_native: Sempre ha torto il più debole
proverb_en: The weakest goes to the wall.

proverb_native: Sempre ne va il meglio
proverb_en: The best always goes first.

proverb_native: Se non puoi mordere, non mostrar mai i denti
proverb_en: If you can’t bite, don’t show your teeth.

proverb_native: Senza debiti, senza pensieri
proverb_en: Without debt without care.

proverb_native: Se piovesser maccheroni, che bel tempo pei ghiottoni!
proverb_en: If it rained maccaroni, what a fine time for gluttons!

proverb_native: Servizio de’ grandi non è eredità
proverb_en: Service is not inheritance.

proverb_native: Se sorcio sei, non seguitar rane
proverb_en: If you are a mouse don’t follow frogs.

proverb_native: Se tacesse la gallina, non si saprebbe che ha fatto l’uovo
proverb_en: If the hen had not cackled we should not know she had laid an egg.

proverb_native: Se ti lasci metter in spalla il vitello, quindi a poco ti metteranno la vacca
proverb_en: If you let them put the calf on your shoulders, it will not be long before they clap on the cow.

proverb_native: Si arriva più presto un bugiardo che un zoppo
proverb_en: A liar is sooner caught than a cripple.

proverb_native: Si dice è mentitore
proverb_en: They say, is a liar.

proverb_native: Si dice sempre il lupo più grande che non è
proverb_en: The wolf is always said to be bigger than he is.

proverb_native: Simili con simili vanno
proverb_en: Like will to like.

proverb_native: S’io dormo, dormo a me; s’io lavoro, non so a che
proverb_en: If I sleep, I sleep for myself; if I work, I know not for whom.

proverb_native: Si può pagar l’oro troppo caro
proverb_en: One may buy gold too dear.

proverb_native: Si romperebbe il collo in un filo di paglia
proverb_en: He would break his neck against a straw.

proverb_native: Si trovano molti asini che non portano mai sacco
proverb_en: There are more asses than carry sacks.

proverb_native: Si trovano più ladri che forche
proverb_en: There are more thieves than gibbets.

proverb_native: Si vive bene all’ombra del campanile
proverb_en: It is good living under the shadow of the belfry.

proverb_native: Sorte, e dormi
proverb_en: Have luck, and sleep.

proverb_native: Sotto la bianca cenere sta la brace ardente
proverb_en: Under white ashes there is glowing coal.

proverb_native: Sotto l’istesso fuoco si purifica l’oro, e si consuma la paglia
proverb_en: The same fire purifies gold and consumes straw.

proverb_native: Spegner il fuoco con la stoppa
proverb_en: To put out the fire with tow.

proverb_native: Spesso chi crede fuggir il fumo, cade nel fuoco
proverb_en: They who shun the smoke often fall into the fire.

proverb_native: Spesso chi troppo fa, poco fa
proverb_en: Who does too much often does little.

proverb_native: Spesso d’un gran male nasce un gran bene
proverb_en: Out of a great evil often comes a great good.

proverb_native: Spesso i doni sono danni
proverb_en: Gifts are often losses.

proverb_native: Spogliar Pietro per vestir Paolo
proverb_en: To strip Peter to clothe Paul.

proverb_native: Sproni proprii e cavalli d’altri fanno corte le miglia
proverb_en: One’s own spurs and another’s horse make the miles short.

proverb_native: Suocera e nuora, tempesta e gragnuola
proverb_en: Mother-in-law and daughter-in-law, storm and hail.

proverb_native: Suon di campana non caccia cornacchia
proverb_en: The sound of the bell does not drive away rooks.

proverb_native: Superbo è quel cavallo che non si vuol portar la biada
proverb_en: It’s a very proud horse that will not carry his oats.

proverb_native: Taglia la coda al cane, e’ riman cane
proverb_en: Cut off the dog’s tail, he remains a dog.

proverb_native: Tal canta che allegro non è
proverb_en: Some sing who are not merry.

proverb_native: Tal ha belli occhi che niente vi vede
proverb_en: One may have good eyes and see nothing.

proverb_native: Tal ha paura che minacciar osa
proverb_en: Many a one threatens and yet is afraid.

proverb_native: Tal lascia l’arrosto, che poi ne brama il fumo
proverb_en: Many a one leaves the roast who afterwards longs for the smoke of it.

proverb_native: Tal padrone, tal servitore
proverb_en: Like master like man.

proverb_native: Tal si burla che si confessa
proverb_en: Some who jest tell tales of themselves.

proverb_native: Tal sprezza la superbia con una maggior superbia
proverb_en: There are some who despise pride with a greater pride.

proverb_native: T’annoia il tuo vicino? Prestagli uno zecchino
proverb_en: Does your neighbour bore you? Lend him a sequin.

proverb_native: Tante teste, tanti cervelli
proverb_en: So many heads, so many brains.

proverb_native: Tanti paesi, tante usanze
proverb_en: So many countries, so many customs.

proverb_native: Tanto buono che non val niente
proverb_en: So good that he is good for nothing.

proverb_native: Tanto è morir di male quanto d’amore
proverb_en: It is all one whether you die of sickness or of love.

proverb_native: Tanto va la secchia al pozzo che vi lascia il manico
proverb_en: The bucket goes so often to the well that it leaves its handle there.

proverb_native: Tanto vale il mio nò, quanto il tuo sì
proverb_en: My No is as good as your Yes.

proverb_native: Tardi furon savj i Troiani
proverb_en: The Trojans were wise too late.

proverb_native: Tardi si vien con l’acqua quando la casa è arsa
proverb_en: It is too late to come with water when the house is burnt down.

proverb_native: Tosto si trova il bastone per dare al cane
proverb_en: A stick is soon found to beat a dog.

proverb_native: Tra asino e asino, non corron se non calci
proverb_en: Nothing passes between asses but kicks.

proverb_native: Tra corsale e corsale, non si guadagna se non barili vuoti
proverb_en: Corsairs against corsairs, there is nothing to win but empty barrels.

proverb_native: Tra due poltroni, il vantaggio è di chi prima conosce l’altro
proverb_en: Between two cowards, he has the advantage who first detects the other.

proverb_native: Traduttori, traditori
proverb_en: Translators, traitors.

proverb_native: Tra la briglia e lo sprone consiste la ragione
proverb_en: Reason lies between bridle and spur.

proverb_native: Tra ’l cuoco e il canovaio non è mai nimicizia
proverb_en: There is never enmity between the cook and the butler.

proverb_native: Trar la cavezza dietro all’asino
proverb_en: To throw the halter after the ass.

proverb_native: Tre cose cacciano l’uomo di casa: fumo, goccia, e femmina arrabbiata
proverb_en: Three things drive a man out of doors: smoke, dropping water, and a shrew.

proverb_native: Tre donne e un papero fanno un mercato
proverb_en: Three women and a goose make a market.

proverb_native: Tre fratelli, tre castelli
proverb_en: Three brothers, three castles.

proverb_native: Tre lo sanno, tutti lo sanno
proverb_en: Three know it, all know it.

proverb_native: Trista è quella casa ove le galline cantano, e ’l gallo tace
proverb_en: It is a sorry house where the hens crow and the cock is silent.

proverb_native: Tristo è quel barbiere che ha un sol pettine
proverb_en: He is a sorry barber who has but one comb.

proverb_native: Trotto d’asino poco dura
proverb_en: An ass’s trot does not last long.

proverb_native: Tua camicia non sappia il secreto
proverb_en: Let not your shirt know your secret.

proverb_native: Tutte le chiavi non pendono ad una cintura
proverb_en: All the keys do not hang at one girdle.

proverb_native: Tutte le dita non son pari
proverb_en: All the fingers are not alike.

proverb_native: Tutte le parole non voglion risposta
proverb_en: Not all words require an answer.

proverb_native: Tutte le strade conducono a Roma
proverb_en: All roads lead to Rome.

proverb_native: Tutte le volpi alla fine si riveggono in pellicceria
proverb_en: At last the foxes all meet at the furrier’s.

proverb_native: Tutti i gusti son gusti
proverb_en: All tastes are tastes.

proverb_native: Tutti i santi non fanno miracoli
proverb_en: All saints do not work miracles.

proverb_native: Tutti son bravi quando l’inimico fugge
proverb_en: All are brave when the enemy flies.

proverb_native: Tutto è bene che riesce bene
proverb_en: All’s well that ends well.

proverb_native: Tutto il cervello non è in una testa
proverb_en: All the brains are not in one head.

proverb_native: Tutto quello che crolla non cade
proverb_en: Not all that shakes falls.

proverb_native: Tutto s’accommoda eccetto l’osso del collo
proverb_en: Everything may be repaired except the neckbone.

proverb_native: Tutto sapere è niente sapere
proverb_en: To know everything is to know nothing.

proverb_native: Una aiuta a maritare l’altra
proverb_en: One daughter helps to marry the other.

proverb_native: Una bugia ne tira dieci
proverb_en: One lie draws ten after it.

proverb_native: Una campana fa a un comune
proverb_en: One bell serves a parish.

proverb_native: Una mano lava l’altra, e tutt’e due lavano il viso
proverb_en: One hand washes the other, and both wash the face.

proverb_native: Una parola tira l’altra
proverb_en: One word brings on another.

proverb_native: Una pecora rognosa ne guasta un branco
proverb_en: One scabby sheep spoils a flock.

proverb_native: Una pulce non leva il sonno
proverb_en: One flea does not hinder sleep.

proverb_native: Una rondine non fa l’estate
proverb_en: One swallow does not make a summer.

proverb_native: Una sella non s’adatta ad un dosso solo
proverb_en: A saddle fits more backs than one.

proverb_native: Una spina non fa siepe
proverb_en: One briar does not make a hedge.

proverb_native: Un avvertito ne val due
proverb_en: A man warned is as good as two.

proverb_native: Un buon boccone, e cento guai
proverb_en: One good morsel and a hundred vexations.

proverb_native: Un canestro d’uva non fa vendemmia
proverb_en: One basket of grapes does not make a vintage.

proverb_native: Un chiodo caccia l’altro
proverb_en: One nail drives out another.

proverb_native: Un coltello aguzza l’altro
proverb_en: One knife whets another.

proverb_native: Un coltello fa tener l’altro nella guaina
proverb_en: One knife keeps another in its sheath.

proverb_native: Un demonio non fa l’inferno
proverb_en: One devil does not make hell.

proverb_native: Un diavol conosce l’altro
proverb_en: One devil knows another.

proverb_native: Un diavol scaccia l’altro
proverb_en: One devil drives out another.

proverb_native: Un fior non fa ghirlanda
proverb_en: One flower does not make a garland.

proverb_native: Un male ed un frate rare volte soli
proverb_en: A misfortune and a friar seldom go alone.

proverb_native: Un matto sa più domandare che sette savj rispondere
proverb_en: A fool can ask more questions than seven wise men can answer.

proverb_native: Un nemico è troppo, e cento amici non bastano
proverb_en: One enemy is too much, and a hundred friends are not enough.

proverb_native: Uno leva la lepre, un altro la piglia
proverb_en: One starts the hare, another catches it.

proverb_native: Un pajo d’orecchie seccherebbero cento lingue
proverb_en: One pair of ears would exhaust a hundred tongues.

proverb_native: Un pazzo getta una pietra nel pozzo, e vi voglion cento savii a cavarnela
proverb_en: A fool throws a stone into a well, and it requires a hundred wise men to get it out again.

proverb_native: Un peccato confessato è mezzo perdonato
proverb_en: A sin confessed is half forgiven.

proverb_native: Un poco di vero fa creder tutta la bugia
proverb_en: A little truth makes the whole lie pass.

proverb_native: Un sorcio mette paura ad un ladro
proverb_en: A mouse will scare a thief.

proverb_native: Unto alle ruote
proverb_en: Grease to the wheels.

proverb_native: Un uomo di paglia vuole una donna d’oro
proverb_en: A man of straw needs a woman of gold.

proverb_native: Uomo amante, uomo zelante
proverb_en: A loving man, a jealous man.

proverb_native: Uomo ammogliato, uccello in gabbia
proverb_en: A married man is a caged bird.

proverb_native: Uomo lento non ha mai tempo
proverb_en: A slothful man never has time.

proverb_native: Uomo morto non fa guerra
proverb_en: A dead man does not make war.

proverb_native: Uomo ozioso è il capezzale del diavolo
proverb_en: An idle man is the devil’s bolster.

proverb_native: Uomo solitario, o bestia o angiolo
proverb_en: A solitary man is either a brute or an angel.

proverb_native: Uscito è dal fango, ed è cascato nel rio
proverb_en: He got out of the mud and fell into the river.

proverb_native: Va al mare, se ben vuoi pescare
proverb_en: Go to the sea if you would fish well.

proverb_native: Val più un asino vivo che un dottore morto
proverb_en: A living ass is better than a dead doctor.

proverb_native: Val più un’oncia di discrezione che una libra di sapere
proverb_en: An ounce of discretion is better than a pound of knowledge.

proverb_native: Vaso che va spesso al fonte, ci lascia il manico o la fronte
proverb_en: The pitcher that goes often to the fountain leaves there either its handle or its spout.

proverb_native: Vaso vuoto suona meglio
proverb_en: Empty vessels make most noise.

proverb_native: Vedi Napoli e poi muori
proverb_en: See Naples and then die.

proverb_native: Vedon più quattr’occhi che due
proverb_en: Four eyes see more than two.

proverb_native: Vender il miele a chi ha le api
proverb_en: To sell the honey to one who has the bees.

proverb_native: Vender la pelle dell’orso innanzi che sia preso
proverb_en: To sell the skin of the bear before it is caught.

proverb_native: Vender l’uccello in su la frasca
proverb_en: To sell the bird in the bush.

proverb_native: Vendetta di cent’anni ha ancora i lattaiuoli
proverb_en: Revenge a hundred years old has still its milk-teeth.

proverb_native: Ventre digiuno non ode nessuno
proverb_en: A hungry belly has no ears.

proverb_native: Ventura aver poco senno basta
proverb_en: To have luck needs little wit.

proverb_native: Vicino alla chiesa, lontan da Dio
proverb_en: Near the church far from God.

proverb_native: Vien la fortuna a chi la procura
proverb_en: Fortune comes to him who strives for it.

proverb_native: Vino dentro, senno fuora
proverb_en: When the wine is in the wit is out.

proverb_native: Virtù di silenzio è gran scienza
proverb_en: The virtue of silence is a great piece of knowledge.

proverb_native: Vivendo s’impara
proverb_en: Live and learn.

proverb_native: Vive più il minacciato che l’impiccato
proverb_en: A threatened man lives longer than one that is hanged.

proverb_native: Vivi, e lascia vivere
proverb_en: Live and let live.

proverb_native: Voce di popolo, voce di Dio
proverb_en: The people’s voice, God’s voice.

proverb_native: Voce d’uno, voce di niuno
proverb_en: One voice, no voice.

proverb_native: Voler lasciar andare dodici danari al soldo
proverb_en: To be content to let twelve pennies pass for a shilling.

proverb_native: Freier Mann, freies Gut. Free man, free goods. (So: Free ships, free goods.—
proverb_en: American.

proverb_native: Ich brauche keine Hummeln in meinem Bienenkorbe. I want no drones in my bee-hive. (
proverb_en: So Shakspeare

proverb_en: Hamburg

proverb_native: Jeder weiß es am Besten, wo ihn der Schuh drückt. Everybody knows best where his own shoe pinches. (
proverb_en: Also Scotch.

proverb_native: Man spart am Zapfen und läßt’s am Spundloch auslaufen. To save at the spiggot, and let it run out at the bung-hole. (
proverb_en: Also Scotch.

proverb_native: Man spricht, an vielerlei Leuten ist Mangel auf Erden: an Pfaffen, sonst dürfte einer nicht sechs bis sieben Pründen; an Adelichen, sonst wollte nicht jeder Bauer ein Junker sein; an Huren, sonst würden das Handwerk Eheweiber und Nonnen nicht treiben; an Juden, sonst würden Christen nicht wuchern. Folks say there is a lack of four sorts of people on earth: of priests, else one would not have six or seven benefices; of gentlemen, else every boor would not want to be a squire; of whores, else married women and nuns would not carry on the trade; of Jews, else Christians would not practise usury. (
proverb_en: Trench quotes this as the longest Proverb known.

proverb_en: Motto of the Margrave of Brandenburg.

proverb_en: knows

proverb_native: Abad avariento por un bodigo pierde ciento
proverb_en: A coveteous abbot for one offering loses a hundred.

proverb_native: Abad de Carçuela, comistes la olla, pedis la caçuela
proverb_en: Abbot of Carçuela, you eat up the pot and ask for the pipkin.

proverb_native: Abajanse los adarves y alzanse los muladares
proverb_en: Walls sink and dunghills rise.

proverb_native: A barba de necio aprenden todos á rapar
proverb_en: On a fool’s beard all learn to shave.

proverb_native: Abiendo escalera por do bajar, buscais soga para os colgar
proverb_en: With a staircase before you, you look for a rope to go down by.

proverb_native: Abrenuncio Satanas, mala capa llevarás
proverb_en: Renounce the devil, and thou shalt wear a shabby cloak.

proverb_native: A buen compañon buena compañia
proverb_en: For a good companion good company.

proverb_native: A buey haron poco le presta el aguijon
proverb_en: A lazy ox is little the letter for the goad.

proverb_native: A buey viejo no le cates abrigo
proverb_en: No need to seek shelter for an old ox.

proverb_native: A cabo de cien años todos seremos calvos
proverb_en: A hundred years hence we shall all be bald.

proverb_native: A cada malo su dia malo
proverb_en: To every evil doer his evil day.

proverb_native: A cada necio agrada su porrada
proverb_en: Every fool is pleased with his bauble.

proverb_native: A calças cortas abugeta largas
proverb_en: Short hose must have long points.

proverb_native: A carne de lobo, diente de perro
proverb_en: To wolf’s flesh dog’s tooth.

proverb_native: A casa de tu hermano no irás cada serano
proverb_en: Go not every evening to your brother’s house.

proverb_native: A casa de tu tia, mas no cada dia
proverb_en: Go to your aunt’s house, but not every day.

proverb_native: A cavallo comedor cabedro corto
proverb_en: A short halter for a greedy horse.

proverb_native: A cavallo dado no le miren el diente
proverb_en: Look not a gift horse in the mouth.

proverb_native: A celada de bellacos, mejor es el hombre por los pies que por los manos
proverb_en: At an ambuscade of villains a man does better with his feet than his hands.

proverb_native: Achaques al viernes por no le ayunar
proverb_en: Friday pretexts for not fasting (meaning pleas of indisposition for not eating fish).

proverb_native: A chico pajarillo, chico nidillo
proverb_en: Little bird, little nest.

proverb_native: A clérigo hecho de fraile, no le fies tu comadre
proverb_en: Trust not your gossip to a priest who has been a friar.

proverb_native: A cuentas viejas, barajas nuevas
proverb_en: Old reckonings make new disputes.

proverb_native: Acuestate sin cena, y amanecerás sin deuda
proverb_en: Go to bed supperless and you will wake without debt.

proverb_native: A Dios rogando y con el mazo dando
proverb_en: Praying to God and hitting with the hammer.

proverb_native: A Dios te doy, libreta, bevida, y por hilar
proverb_en: God take you, pound (of flax), drunk out and not yet spun.

proverb_native: A dos pardales en una espiga nunca hay liga
proverb_en: Two sparrows on one ear of corn never agree.

proverb_native: Adó pensas que hay tocinos, no hay estacas
proverb_en: Where you think there is bacon, there are not even hooks for it.

proverb_native: Adó sacan y non pon, presto llegan al hondon
proverb_en: Always taking out and never putting in, soon reaches the bottom.

proverb_native: Adonde vas, mal? Adonde mas hay
proverb_en: Whither goest thou, misfortune? To where there is more.

proverb_native: Adonde yrá el buey, que no are?
proverb_en: Whither shall the ox go where he will not have to plough?

proverb_native: Adó vas, duelo? Adó suelo
proverb_en: Whither goest thou, sorrow? Whither I am used to go.

proverb_native: A fuer de Aragon, buen servicio, mal galardon
proverb_en: According to the custom of Aragon, good service, bad guerdon.

proverb_native: Agora que tengo oveja y borrego, todos me dizen: En hora buena estais, Pedro
proverb_en: Now that I have an ewe and a lamb, every one says to me: Good morrow, Peter.

proverb_native: A gran salto, gran quebranto
proverb_en: A great leap gives a great shake.

proverb_native: Agua passada no muele molino
proverb_en: Water past will not turn the mill.

proverb_native: A hija casada salen nos yernos
proverb_en: When our daughter is married sons-in-law are plenty.

proverb_native: Ahorrar para la vejez, ganar un maravedi y bever tres
proverb_en: To save for old age, earning a maravedi and drinking three.

proverb_native: A idos de mi casa, y que quereis con mi muger? no hay que responder
proverb_en: There is no answer for Get out of my house, and What have you to do with my wife?

proverb_native: Alábate, cesto, que venderte quiero
proverb_en: Praise yourself, basket, for I want to sell you.

proverb_native: A la boda de Don Garcia lleva pan en la capilla
proverb_en: Carry bread in your hood to Don Garcia’s wedding.

proverb_native: A la burla, dejarla quando mas agrada
proverb_en: Leave the jest at its best.

proverb_native: A la cabeça, la comida la endereça
proverb_en: Eating sets the head to rights.

proverb_native: Al agradecido, mas de lo pedido
proverb_en: To the grateful man give more than he asks.

proverb_native: A la longa el galgo á la liebre mata
proverb_en: In the long run the greyhound kills the hare.

proverb_native: A la luna el lobo al asno espulga
proverb_en: The wolf picks the ass’s fleas by moonlight.

proverb_native: A la muger y á la picaza, lo que dirias en la plaza
proverb_en: To a woman and a magpie tell your secrets in the marketplace.

proverb_native: A la primera azadonada quiere sacar agua
proverb_en: He expects to find water at the first stroke of the spade.

proverb_native: A las barbas con dineros honra hacen los caballeros
proverb_en: To beards with money cavaliers pay respect.

proverb_native: A las burlas así ve á ellas que no se salgan de veras
proverb_en: Jest so that it may not turn to earnest.

proverb_native: Al asno muerto, la cebada al rabo
proverb_en: The ass dead, the barley at his tail.

proverb_native: A las malas lenguas, tixera
proverb_en: For evil tongues, scissors.

proverb_native: A las veces do cazar pensamos, cazados quedamos
proverb_en: When we think to catch we are sometimes caught.

proverb_native: A las veces lleva el hombre á su casa con que llore
proverb_en: A man may hap to bring home with him what makes him weep.

proverb_native: A la vulpeja dormida, no le cae nada en la boca
proverb_en: Nothing falls into the mouth of a sleeping fox.

proverb_native: Al buen pagador, no le duelen prendas
proverb_en: A good paymaster needs no security.

proverb_native: Alcança, quien no cansa
proverb_en: He who does not tire, achieves.

proverb_native: Al desdichado poco le vale ser esforzado
proverb_en: It little avails the unfortunate to be brave.

proverb_native: Alegrias, antruejo, que mañana serás ceniza
proverb_en: Be merry, Shrovetide, for to-morrow thou wilt be ashes.

proverb_native: Al enemigo, si vuelve la espalda, la puente de plata
proverb_en: Make a bridge of silver for the flying enemy.

proverb_native: Al enhornar se tuerce el pan
proverb_en: It is in putting it into the oven that the loaf is made crooked.

proverb_native: Al envidioso afilásele el gesto y crécele el ojo
proverb_en: The envious man’s face grows sharp and his eyes big.

proverb_native: Al fin se canta la Gloria
proverb_en: At the end the Gloria is chanted.

proverb_native: Al gato por ser ladron, no le eches de tu mansion
proverb_en: Don’t send away your cat for being a thief.

proverb_native: Algo va de Pedro á Pedro
proverb_en: There is some distance between Peter and Peter.

proverb_native: Algun dia mande tanto Pedro como su amo
proverb_en: Some day Peter will command as much as his master.

proverb_native: Al gusto dañado lo dulce le es amargo
proverb_en: To a depraved taste sweet is bitter.

proverb_native: Al hacer temblar, y al comer sudar
proverb_en: To shiver at work, and sweat at meals.

proverb_native: Al hijo de tu vezina quítale el mico, y cásale con tu hija
proverb_en: Wipe the nose of your neighbour’s son, and marry him to your daughter.

proverb_native: Al hombre bueno, no le busques abolengo
proverb_en: Ask not after a good man’s pedigree.

proverb_native: Al hombre osado, la fortuna le da la mano
proverb_en: To the bold man fortune gives her hand.

proverb_native: Al hombre venturero, la hija le nace primero
proverb_en: The lucky man has a daughter for his first-born.

proverb_native: Allá va la lengua, do duele la muela
proverb_en: The tongue goes where the tooth aches.

proverb_native: Allá van leyes, do quieren reyes
proverb_en: Laws go the way kings direct.

proverb_native: Al lavar saldrá la mancilla
proverb_en: The spot will come out in the washing.

proverb_native: Allá vayas, mal, adó te pongan buen cabeçal
proverb_en: Away with thee, sickness, to where they make a good pillow for thee.

proverb_native: Allegador de la ceniza y deramador de la harina
proverb_en: He gathers up ashes and scatters flour.

proverb_native: Allégate á los buenos, y serás uno dellos
proverb_en: Associate with the good, and you will be one of them.

proverb_native: Al llamado de quien le piensa viene el buey á la melena
proverb_en: The ox comes to the yoke at the call of his feeder.

proverb_native: Al loco y al toro, darles corro
proverb_en: Make way for a madman and a bull.

proverb_native: Al mas ruin puerco la mejor bellota
proverb_en: The worst pig gets the best acorn.

proverb_native: Al médico, confesor, y letrado, no le hayas engañado
proverb_en: Deceive not thy physician, confessor, or lawyer.

proverb_native: Al mozo mal mandado, ponle la mesa, y embiale al recado
proverb_en: If you have a loitering servant, set his dinner before him and send him on an errand.

proverb_native: A lo que puedes solo, no esperes á otro
proverb_en: Expect not at another’s hand what you can do by your own.

proverb_native: A los bobos se les aperece la Madre de Dios
proverb_en: The Mother of God appears to fools.

proverb_native: A los osados, ayuda la fortuna
proverb_en: Fortune aids the bold.

proverb_native: Al perro flaco, todo es pulgas
proverb_en: The lean dog is all fleas.

proverb_native: Al puerco gordo, untarle el rabo
proverb_en: To grease the fat pig’s tail.

proverb_native: Al que cueze y amasa no le hurtar hogaça
proverb_en: Do not steal a loaf from him that kneads and bakes.

proverb_native: Al que dá el capon, dale la pierna y el alon
proverb_en: To him who gives you a capon you may spare a leg and a wing.

proverb_native: Al que mal hace, nunca le falta achaque
proverb_en: The wrong doer is never without a pretext.

proverb_native: Al que tiene muger hermosa, ó castillo en frontera, ó viña en carrera, nunca le falta guerra
proverb_en: He who has a handsome wife, a castle on the frontier, or a vineyard on the roadside, is never without war.

proverb_native: Al que yerra, perdónale una vez, mas no despues
proverb_en: Him who errs, forgive once, but never twice.

proverb_native: Alquimia provada, tener renta y no gastar nada
proverb_en: It is approved alchemy to have an income and spend nothing.

proverb_native: Al raton que no tiene mas que un agujero, presto le cogen
proverb_en: The rat that has but one hole is soon caught.

proverb_native: Al toro y al aire, darles calle
proverb_en: Don’t stop the way of a bull or of a current of air.

proverb_native: Al villano dadle el pie, y tomarse ha la mano
proverb_en: Give a clown your foot, and he’ll take your hand.

proverb_native: Al yerno y al cochino, una vez el camino
proverb_en: To a son-in-law and a hog you need show the way but once.

proverb_native: Ama á quien no te ama, responde á quien no te llama, correrás carrera vana
proverb_en: Love one that does not love you, answer one that does not call you, and you will run a fruitless race.

proverb_native: A maa veziña da agulla sin liña
proverb_en: The bad neighbour gives a needle without thread.

proverb_native: A madrina, que eso yo me lo sabia
proverb_en: Hush, brideswoman, I knew all that before.

proverb_native: A mal ñudo mal cuño
proverb_en: To a hard knot a hard wedge.

proverb_native: A maravedi de pleyto, real de papel
proverb_en: A lawsuit for a maravedi consumes a real’s worth of paper.

proverb_native: A Mari Ardida nunca le falta mal dia, á Mari Monton Dios se lo da, y Dios se le pon
proverb_en: Mary Busybody never wants a bad day, and Hilary Drone has God to give and bring to her.

proverb_native: Amar y saber, no puede ser
proverb_en: To love and be wise is impossible.

proverb_native: Ama, soys ama mientras el niño mama, y despues no nada
proverb_en: Nurse, you are mistress whilst the child sucks, and after that nothing.

proverb_native: A mengua de carne, buenos son pollos con tocino
proverb_en: When you can’t get meat, chickens and bacon are good.

proverb_native: A mengua de pan, buenas son tortas
proverb_en: When you can’t get bread, oat-cakes are not amiss.

proverb_native: Amigo del buen tiempo mudará con el viento
proverb_en: A fair-weather friend changes with the wind.

proverb_native: Amigo de pleitos, poco dinero; amigo de médicos, poca salud; amigo de frailes, poca honra
proverb_en: Fond of lawsuits, little wealth; fond of doctors, little health; fond of friars, little honour.

proverb_native: Amigo do todos y de ninguno todo es uno
proverb_en: Everybody’s friend and nobody’s friend is all one.

proverb_native: Amigo quebrado, soldado, mas nunca sano
proverb_en: Friendship broken may be soldered, but never made whole.

proverb_native: Amigo reconciliado, enemigo doblado
proverb_en: A reconciled friend is a double enemy.

proverb_native: Amigos y mulas fallescen á as duras
proverb_en: Friends and mules fail in hard trials.

proverb_native: Amistad de yerno, sol de invierno
proverb_en: A son-in-law’s friendship is a winter’s sun.

proverb_native: Amor de niño, agua en cesto
proverb_en: A boy’s love is water in a sieve.

proverb_native: Amor de padre, que todo lo otro es aire
proverb_en: A father’s love, for all other is air.

proverb_native: Amores nuevos olvidan viejos
proverb_en: New loves drive out the old.

proverb_native: Amores, dolores y dineros no pueden estar secretos
proverb_en: Love, grief, and money cannot be kept secret.

proverb_native: Amor fa molt, argent fa tot
proverb_en: Love does much, money does all.

proverb_native: Amor loco, yo por vos, y vos por otro
proverb_en: Mad love—I for you, and you for another.

proverb_native: A moro muerto gran lanzada
proverb_en: A great lance-thrust to a dead Moor.

proverb_native: A mucho hablar, mucho errar
proverb_en: Much talking, much erring.

proverb_native: A muertos y á idos no hay mas amigos
proverb_en: The dead and the absent have no friends.

proverb_native: A muger mala poco le aprovecha guarda
proverb_en: There is little use in watching a bad woman.

proverb_native: A mula vieja cabezadas nuevas
proverb_en: New trappings to an old mule.

proverb_native: Andando gana la hazeña, que no estándose queda
proverb_en: The mill gains by going, and not by standing still.

proverb_native: Andando y hablando, marido, á la horca
proverb_en: Talk as you go, husband, to the gallows.

proverb_native: Andar á caza con huron muerto
proverb_en: To go rabbit catching with a dead ferret.

proverb_native: Andeme yo caliente, y riase la gente
proverb_en: Let me go warm, and folks may laugh.

proverb_native: Animo vence en guerra, que no arma buena
proverb_en: It is courage that vanquishes in war, and not good weapons.

proverb_native: Ante la puerta del rezador, nunca eches tu trigo al sol
proverb_en: Never spread your corn to dry before the door of a saintly man.

proverb_native: Antes de casar, ten casas en que morar, y tierras en que labrar, y viñas en que podar
proverb_en: Before you marry, have a house to live in, fields to till, and vines to cut.

proverb_native: Antes de la hora gran denuedo; venidos al punto, venidos al miedo
proverb_en: Before the time great courage; when at the point, great fear.

proverb_native: Antes de mil años todos seremos calvos
proverb_en: In less than a thousand years we shall all be bald.

proverb_native: Antes di que digan
proverb_en: Say before they say.

proverb_native: Antes moral que almendro
proverb_en: Rather mulberry than almond.

proverb_native: Antes que cases, mira que haces, que no es ñudo que deshaces
proverb_en: Before you marry, beware, for it is a knot difficult to untie.

proverb_native: Antes toman al mentiroso que al cojo
proverb_en: The liar is sooner caught than the cripple.

proverb_native: A olla que hierve, ninguna mosca se atreve
proverb_en: No flies light on a boiling pot.

proverb_native: A otro perro con ese hueso
proverb_en: Throw that bone to another dog.

proverb_native: A padre guardador, hijo gastador
proverb_en: After a thrifty father, a prodigal son.

proverb_native: A palabras locas orejas sordas
proverb_en: To mad words deaf ears.

proverb_native: A pan duro diente agudo
proverb_en: A sharp tooth for hard bread.

proverb_native: A pobreza no hay vergüenza
proverb_en: Poverty is no sin.

proverb_native: A poca barba, poca vergüenza
proverb_en: Little beard, little modesty.

proverb_native: Aprendiz de Portugal, no sabe cozer y quiere cortar
proverb_en: A Portuguese apprentice who can’t sew, yet would be cutting out.

proverb_native: A presurosa demanda espaciosa respuesta
proverb_en: To a hasty demand a leisure reply.

proverb_native: A puerta cerrada, el diablo se vuelve
proverb_en: When the devil finds the door shut, he goes away.

proverb_native: A puerta de cazador nunca gran muladar
proverb_en: There is never a great dunghill at a sportsman’s door.

proverb_native: A puñadas entran las buenas hadas
proverb_en: Good luck makes its way in by elbowing.

proverb_native: Aquel es tu amigo que te quita de ruido
proverb_en: He is your friend who gets you out of a fray.

proverb_native: Aquella ave es mala, que en su nido caga
proverb_en: It is an ill bird that fouls its own nest.

proverb_native: Aquella es bien casada, que no tiene suegra ni cuñada
proverb_en: She is well married who has neither mother-in-law nor sister-in-law.

proverb_native: Aquellos son ricos que tienen amigos
proverb_en: They are rich who have friends.

proverb_native: Aquel pierde venta que no tiene que venda
proverb_en: He loses his market who has nothing to sell.

proverb_native: A quien dan, no escoge
proverb_en: Beggars must not be choosers.

proverb_native: A quien dices tu puridad, á ese das tu libertad
proverb_en: You surrender your freedom where you deposit your secret.

proverb_native: A quien Dios quiere bien, la perra le pare lechones
proverb_en: Whom God loves, his bitch litters pigs.

proverb_native: A quien hace casa, ó se casa, la bolsa le quede rasa
proverb_en: He who builds a house, or marries, is left with a lank purse.

proverb_native: A quien madruga, Dios le ayuda
proverb_en: God helps the early riser.

proverb_native: A quien miedo le han, lo suyo le dan
proverb_en: He who is feared gets more than his own.

proverb_native: A quien no le basta espada y corazon, no le bastarán corazas y lanzon
proverb_en: For whom sword and courage are not enough, corslet and lance will not be enough.

proverb_native: A quien no le sobra pan, no crie can
proverb_en: Who has no bread to spare should not keep a dog.

proverb_native: A quien no mata puerco, no le dan morcilla
proverb_en: They who don’t kill pigs must not expect black-puddings.

proverb_native: A quien no tiene nada, nada le espanta
proverb_en: Who has nothing, fears nothing.

proverb_native: A quien tiene buena muger, ningun mal le puede venir, que no sea de sufrir
proverb_en: He who has a good wife can bear any evil.

proverb_native: A quien tiene mala muger, ningun bien le puede venir, que bien se puede decir
proverb_en: He who has a bad wife can expect no happiness that can be so called.

proverb_native: A quien vela, todo se le revela
proverb_en: To him that watches, everything is revealed.

proverb_native: Ara bien y hondo, cogerás pan en abondo
proverb_en: Plough deep and you will have plenty of corn.

proverb_native: Ara por enjuto ó por mojado, no besarás á tu vecino en el rabo
proverb_en: Plough wet or dry, and you will not have to kiss your neighbour’s breech.

proverb_native: Arco siempre armado, ó flojo ó quebrado
proverb_en: The bow that is always bent slackens or breaks.

proverb_native: Arde verde por seco, y pagan justos por pecadores
proverb_en: The green burns for the dry, and the righteous pay for sinners.

proverb_native: Ares, no ares, renta me pagues
proverb_en: Plough or not plough, you must pay your rent.

proverb_native: A rio revuelto, ganancia de pescadores
proverb_en: It is good fishing in troubled waters.

proverb_native: Armas y dineros buenas manos quieren
proverb_en: Arms and money require good hands.

proverb_native: Arremángose mi nuera, y trastornó en el fuego la caldera
proverb_en: My daughter-in-law tucked up her sleeves, and upset the kettle into the fire.

proverb_native: Arrieros somos, y en el camino nos encontraremos
proverb_en: We are both carriers, and shall meet on the road.

proverb_native: A salvo está el que repica
proverb_en: He is in safety who rings the tocsin.

proverb_native: Así acontescen cosas récias, como ir á la plaza y venir sin orejas
proverb_en: Such awkward things will happen as going into the great square and coming back without ears.

proverb_native: Así dijó la zorra á las uvas, no pudiendolas alcazar, que no estarvan maduras
proverb_en: The grapes are sour, said the fox when he could not reach them.

proverb_native: Así está el pages entre dos advocats como el pagel entre dos gats
proverb_en: A peasant between two lawyers is like a fish between two cats.
