proverb_native: Así me decis que en el monte hay lobos, y en el valle raposos
proverb_en: So you tell me there are wolves on the mountain, and foxes in the valley.

proverb_native: Asna con pollino no va derecha al molino
proverb_en: An ass with her colt goes not straight to the mill.

proverb_native: Asno con oro alcánzalo todo
proverb_en: The golden ass passes everywhere.

proverb_native: Asno de muchos, lobos le comen
proverb_en: The ass of many owners is food for wolves.

proverb_native: Asno sea quien á asno vocea
proverb_en: An ass let him be who brays at an ass.

proverb_native: Assaz puede poco, quien no amenaza á otro
proverb_en: He can do but little who cannot threaten another.

proverb_native: A su amigo el gato siempre le deja señalado
proverb_en: The cat always leaves her mark upon her friend.

proverb_native: A tí lo digo, hijuela; entiéndelo tú, mi nuera
proverb_en: I say it to you, daughter; hear it, daughter-in-law.

proverb_native: A todo hay maña, sino á la muerte
proverb_en: There is a remedy for everything but death.

proverb_native: A tu amigo dile la mentira; si te guardare poridad, dile la verdad
proverb_en: Tell your friend a lie; and if he keeps it secret, tell him the truth.

proverb_native: A tu amigo gánale un juego, y bévele luego
proverb_en: Win a game of your friend, and drink the money on the spot.

proverb_native: A tu criado no le hartes de pan, no pedirá queso
proverb_en: Do not stuff your servant with bread, and he won’t ask for cheese.

proverb_native: A tuerto ó á derecho, ayude Dios á nuestro concejo
proverb_en: Right or wrong, God aid our purpose.

proverb_native: A tuerto ó á derecho, nuestra casa hasta al techo
proverb_en: Right or wrong, ’tis our house up to the roof.

proverb_native: A tu hijo, buen nombre y oficio
proverb_en: To your son give a good name and a trade.

proverb_native: Aun no asamos, é ya empringamos
proverb_en: We are not yet roasting, and already we make sops in the pan.

proverb_native: Aun no ensillamos, é ya cavalgamos
proverb_en: We have not yet saddled, and are already mounted.

proverb_native: Aunque el decidor sea loco, el escuchador sea cuerdo
proverb_en: Though the speaker be a fool, let the hearer be wise.

proverb_native: Aunque la mona se vista de seda, mona so queda
proverb_en: A monkey remains a monkey, though dressed in silk.

proverb_native: Aunque manso tu sabueso, no le muerdas en el bezo
proverb_en: Though your bloodhound be gentle, don’t bite him on the lip.

proverb_native: Aunque me veis con este capote, otro tengo en el monte
proverb_en: Though you see me with this coat, I have another up the mountain.

proverb_native: Aunque mi suegro sea bueno, no quiero perro con concerro
proverb_en: Though my father-in-law is a good man, I do not like a dog with a bell.

proverb_native: Aunque seas prudente viejo, no desdeñes el consejo
proverb_en: Though you are a prudent old man, do not despise counsel.

proverb_native: A un traidor dos alevosos
proverb_en: Two false men to one traitor.

proverb_native: Ausencia enemiga de amor; quan lejos de ojo, tan lejos de corazon
proverb_en: Absence is a foe to love; away from the eyes, away from the heart.

proverb_native: A vaca que no come con os bois, ó come ante, ó come despois
proverb_en: The cow that does not eat with the oxen, either eats before or after them.

proverb_native: Ave con cuchar nunca entre en tu corral
proverb_en: Let no shovel-beaked bird ever enter your yard.

proverb_native: Ave muda no hace agüero
proverb_en: A mute bird makes no omen.

proverb_native: A volpe va por o millo e non come, mas dalle con o rabo e sacode
proverb_en: The fox goes through the corn and does not eat, but brushes it down with his tail.

proverb_native: Aya cebo en el palomar, que palomas ellas se vernán
proverb_en: Let there be food in the pigeon-house, and the pigeons will come to it.

proverb_native: Ayer vaquero, hoy caballero
proverb_en: Yesterday a cowherd, to-day a cavalier.

proverb_native: Ay te duele, ay te daré
proverb_en: Where you smart there I will hit you.

proverb_native: Ay ten a gallena os ollos do ten os ovos
proverb_en: The hen’s eyes are where her eggs are.

proverb_native: Ayudándose tres, para peso de seis
proverb_en: Three who help each other are as good as six.

proverb_native: Azotan á la gata, si no hila nuestra ama
proverb_en: They whip the cat, if our mistress does not spin.

proverb_native: Azotenme en la plaza, que no lo sepan en mi casa
proverb_en: Let them whip me in the market-place, provided it be not known at home.

proverb_native: Barba bien remojada, medio rapada
proverb_en: A beard well lathered is half shaved.

proverb_native: Barriga caliente, pie dormiente
proverb_en: The paunch warm, the foot sleepy.

proverb_native: Barro y cal encubran mucho mal
proverb_en: Clay and lime conceal much evil.

proverb_native: Bel hombre no es todo pobre
proverb_en: A handsome man is not quite poor.

proverb_native: Bendita aquella casa que no tiene corona rapada
proverb_en: Happy the home in which there is no shaven crown.

proverb_native: Bien ama quien nunca olvida
proverb_en: He loves well who never forgets.

proverb_native: Bien cuenta la madre, mejor cuenta el infante
proverb_en: The mother reckons well, but the child reckons better.

proverb_native: Bien merca á quien no dicen hombre bestia
proverb_en: He buys well who is not called a donkey.

proverb_native: Bien ó mal, casado me han
proverb_en: For letter for worse they have married me.

proverb_native: Bien perdido y conocido
proverb_en: A good thing lost is a good thing valued.

proverb_native: Bien predica quien bien vive
proverb_en: He preaches well who lives well.

proverb_native: Bien sabe el asno en cuya cara rebuzna
proverb_en: The ass knows well in whose face he brays.

proverb_native: Bien sabe el huego cuya capa quema
proverb_en: The fire well knows whose cloak burns.

proverb_native: Bien sabe el sabio que no sabe, el nescio piensa que sabe
proverb_en: The wise knows that he does not know; the ignoramus thinks he knows.

proverb_native: Bien sabe la vulpeja con quien trebeja
proverb_en: The fox knows well with whom he plays tricks.

proverb_native: Bien sé lo que digo quando pan pido
proverb_en: I know well what I say when I ask for bread.

proverb_native: Bien vengas, mal, si vienes solo
proverb_en: Welcome, misfortune, if you come alone.

proverb_native: Bocado comido no gana amigo
proverb_en: A morsel eaten selfishly does not gain a friend.

proverb_native: Boca que dice de sí, dice de no
proverb_en: The mouth that says yes says no.

proverb_native: Bofeton amagado, nunca bien dado
proverb_en: A buffeting threatened is never well given.

proverb_native: Buen abogado, mal vecino
proverb_en: A good lawyer, a bad neighbour.

proverb_native: Buena es la gallina que otro cria
proverb_en: Good is the fowl which another rears.

proverb_native: Buena fama hurto encubre
proverb_en: A good name covers theft.

proverb_native: Buen amigo es el gato, sino que rascuña
proverb_en: The cat is friendly, but scratches.

proverb_native: Buenas palabras y ruines hechos engañan sabios y locos
proverb_en: Good words and bad deeds deceive both wise and simple.

proverb_native: Buena vida arrugas tira
proverb_en: A good life defers wrinkles.

proverb_native: Buen corazon quebranta mala ventura
proverb_en: A good heart breaks bad fortune.

proverb_native: Buen jubon me tengo en Francia
proverb_en: I have a good jacket in France.

proverb_native: Bueno, bueno, bueno, mas guarde Dios mi burra de su centeno
proverb_en: Good, good, good, but God keep my ass out of his rye.

proverb_native: Buen principio, la mitad es hecho
proverb_en: Well begun is half done.

proverb_native: Buen siglo haya quien dijó bolta
proverb_en: Blessings on him that said, Face about.

proverb_native: Buey viejo, sulco derecho
proverb_en: An old ox makes a straight furrow.

proverb_native: Burla burlando vase el lobo al asno
proverb_en: All in the way of joke the wolf goes to the ass.

proverb_native: Burlaos con el asno, daros ha en la cara con el rabo
proverb_en: Play with an ass and he will flirt his tail in your face.

proverb_native: Burlaos con el loco en casa, burlará con vos en la plaza
proverb_en: Play with the fool at home, and he will play with you abroad.

proverb_native: Burlas de manos, burlas de villanos
proverb_en: Manual jokes are clowns’ jokes.

proverb_native: Buscais pan de trastrigo
proverb_en: You want better bread than wheaten.

proverb_native: Buscar cinque pies al gato
proverb_en: To look for five feet in a cat.

proverb_native: Cabra coja no quiere siesta
proverb_en: A lame goat will not sleep by day.

proverb_native: Cacarrear, y no poner huevo
proverb_en: To cackle and lay no egg.

proverb_native: Cada buhonero alaba sus agujas
proverb_en: Every pedlar praises his own needles.

proverb_native: Cada cabello hace su sombra
proverb_en: Every hair casts its shadow.

proverb_native: Cada carnero de su pie cuelga
proverb_en: Let every sheep hang by its own foot.

proverb_native: Cada cosa en su tiempo, y nabos en Adviento
proverb_en: Everything in its season, and turnips in Advent.

proverb_native: Cada cuba huele al vino que tiene
proverb_en: Every cask smells of the wine it contains.

proverb_native: Cada gallo canta en su muladar
proverb_en: Every cock crows on his own dunghill.

proverb_native: Cada ollero su olla alaba, y mas si la trae quebrada
proverb_en: Every potter praises his pot, especially if cracked.

proverb_native: Cada qual siente el frio como anda vestido
proverb_en: Every one feels the cold according as he is clad.

proverb_native: Cada uno cuenta de la feria como le va en ella
proverb_en: Every one speaks of the fair as he himself finds it.

proverb_native: Cada uno en su casa, y Dios en la de todos
proverb_en: Every one in his own house, and God in all men’s.

proverb_native: Cada uno es hijo de sus obras
proverb_en: Every man is the son of his own works.

proverb_native: Cada uno estiende la pierna como tiene la cubierta
proverb_en: Every one stretches his legs according to the length of his coverlet.

proverb_native: Cada uno estornuda como Dios le ayuda
proverb_en: Every one sneezes as God pleases.

proverb_native: Cada uno por sí, y Dios por todos
proverb_en: Every one for himself, and God for us all.

proverb_native: Cada uno quiere llevar el agua á su molino, y dejar en seco el del vecino
proverb_en: Every one wishes to bring water to his own mill, and leave his neighbour’s dry.

proverb_native: Cada uno sabe donde le aprieta el zapato
proverb_en: Every one knows where his shoe pinches him.

proverb_native: Cae en la cueva el que otro á ella lleva
proverb_en: He falls into the pit who leads another into it.

proverb_native: Caldo de tripas, bien te repicas
proverb_en: Tripe broth, you make much of yourself.

proverb_native: Caldo de zorra que está frio y quema
proverb_en: Fox’s broth, cold and scalding.

proverb_native: Callar y ojos, tomaremos la madre y los pollos
proverb_en: Silence and look out, we shall catch both hen and chicks.

proverb_native: Campana cascada, nunca sana
proverb_en: A cracked bell will never be sound.

proverb_native: Can que madre tiene en villa, nunca buena ladrilla
proverb_en: The dog that has its bitch in town never barks well.

proverb_native: Cantarillo que muchas veces va á la fuente, ó deja el asa ó la frente
proverb_en: The pitcher that goes often to the well leaves either its handle or its spout.

proverb_native: Cantar mal y porfiar
proverb_en: To sing out of tune and persist in it.

proverb_native: Capon de ocho meses para mesa de rey
proverb_en: A capon eight months old is fit for a king’s table.

proverb_native: Cara de beato, y uñas de gato
proverb_en: A devotee’s face, and a cat’s claws.

proverb_native: Caro cuesta el arrepentir
proverb_en: Repentance costs dear.

proverb_native: Casa el hijo quando quisieres, y la hija quando pudieres
proverb_en: Marry your son when you will, and your daughter when you can.

proverb_native: Casa hospidada, comida y denostada
proverb_en: A house filled with guests is eaten up and ill spoken of.

proverb_native: Casa labrada y viña plantada
proverb_en: A house ready built and a vineyard ready planted.

proverb_native: Casarás y amansarás
proverb_en: Marry and grow tame.

proverb_native: Casarme quiero, comeré cabeza de olla, y sentarme he primero
proverb_en: I’ll marry, and eat the prime of the pot, and sit down first.

proverb_native: Castiga al que no es bueno, y aborrecerte ha luego
proverb_en: Chastise one that is worthless, and he will presently hate you.

proverb_native: Castillo apercebido no es decebido
proverb_en: A fortress on its guard is not surprised.

proverb_native: Cayósele el pan en la miel
proverb_en: His bread fell into the honey.

proverb_native: Cien sastres, cien molineros, y cien texederos son trecientos ladrones
proverb_en: A hundred tailors, a hundred millers, and a hundred weavers, are three hundred thieves.

proverb_native: Cierra tu puerta, y harás tu vecina buena
proverb_en: Shut your door, and you will make your neighbour a good woman.

proverb_native: Cobra buena fama, y échate á dormir
proverb_en: Get a good name, and go to sleep.

proverb_native: Cobre gana cobre, que no huesos de hombre
proverb_en: Copper begets copper, and not (the labour of) men’s bones.

proverb_native: Coces de yegua, amores para el rocin
proverb_en: The mare’s kicks are caresses to the horse.

proverb_native: Combida á tu yerno á la gallina, que él llevará la lima
proverb_en: Invite your son-in-law to a fowl, and he will take away the lemon.

proverb_native: Comer y rascar, todo es empezar
proverb_en: To eat and to scratch one has but to begin.

proverb_native: Como canta el abad, responde el sacristan
proverb_en: As the abbot sings the sacristan responds.

proverb_native: Como costal de carbonero, malo de fuera, peor de dentro
proverb_en: Like a collier’s sack, bad without and worse within.

proverb_native: Compuesta no hay muger fea
proverb_en: No woman is ugly when she is dressed.

proverb_native: Con agena mano sacar la culebra del horado
proverb_en: To draw the snake out of the hole with another’s hand.

proverb_native: Con agua pasada no muele molino
proverb_en: The mill does not grind with water that has passed.

proverb_native: Con dineros no te conocerás, sin dineros no te conocerán
proverb_en: With money you would not know yourself, without money nobody would know you.

proverb_native: Con el Rey y con la Inquisicion, chitos!
proverb_en: About the King and the Inquisition, hush!

proverb_native: Con hijo de gato no se burlan los ratones
proverb_en: Rats do not play tricks with kittens.

proverb_native: Con lo que Sancho cura, Marta cae mala
proverb_en: What cures Sancho makes Martha sick.

proverb_native: Con mal está la casa donde la rueca manda al espada
proverb_en: It fares ill with the house where the spinning-wheel commands the sword.

proverb_native: Con todo el mondo guerra, y paz con Inglaterra
proverb_en: War with all the world, and peace with England.

proverb_native: Con una cautela otra se quiebra
proverb_en: One trick is met by another.

proverb_native: Con un poco de tuerto llega el hombre á su derecho
proverb_en: With a little wrong a man comes by his right.

proverb_native: Coraçon determinado no sufre ser aconsejado
proverb_en: A determined heart will not be counselled.

proverb_native: Cornudo sois, marido: muger, y quien te lo dijo?
proverb_en: Husband, you are a cuckold: wife, who told you so?

proverb_native: Cortesía de boca mucho vale y poco cuesta
proverb_en: Lip courtesy avails much and costs little.

proverb_native: Cosa mala nunca muere
proverb_en: A bad thing never dies.

proverb_native: Cosa que no se venda, nadie la siembra
proverb_en: Nobody sows a thing that will not sell.

proverb_native: Costumbre buena, costumbre mala, el villano quiere que vala
proverb_en: Be a custom good or bad, a peasant will have it continue in force.

proverb_native: Costumbre hace ley
proverb_en: Custom becomes law.

proverb_native: Crea el cuervo, y sacarte ha los ojos
proverb_en: Foster a raven and it will peck out your eyes.

proverb_native: Criatura de un año saca la leche del calcano
proverb_en: A child of a year old sucks milk from the heel.

proverb_native: Cuidado ageno de pelo cuelga
proverb_en: Another’s care hangs by a hair.

proverb_native: Cuidados agenos matan el asno
proverb_en: Other folks’ cares kill the ass.

proverb_native: Cuñados, y perros bermejos pocos buenos
proverb_en: Of brothers-in-law and red dogs few are good.

proverb_native: Da Dios alas á la hormiga para que se pierda mas aina
proverb_en: God gives wings to the ant that she may perish the sooner.

proverb_native: Da Dios almendras á quien no tiene muelas
proverb_en: God gives almonds to one who has no teeth.

proverb_native: Dadiva de ruin á su dueño parece
proverb_en: A bad man’s gift is like his master.

proverb_native: Dadivas quebrantan peñas
proverb_en: Gifts break (or dissolve) rocks.

proverb_native: Dame donde me asiente, que yo me haré donde me acueste
proverb_en: Give me a seat, and I will make myself room to lie down.

proverb_native: Dar en el clavo
proverb_en: To hit the nail on the head.

proverb_native: De amigo reconciliado, guarte de él como del diablo
proverb_en: Beware of a reconciled friend as of the devil.

proverb_native: De aquellos polvos vienen estos lodos
proverb_en: From that dust comes this mud.

proverb_native: Debajo del buen sayo está el hombre malo
proverb_en: Under a good cloak may be a bad man.

proverb_native: Debajo de mi manto al rey mato
proverb_en: Under my cloak I kill the king.

proverb_native: Debajo de una mala capa hay un buen bebedor
proverb_en: Under a bad cloak there is a good tippler.

proverb_native: De casa del gato no vá harto el rato
proverb_en: The mouse does not leave the cat’s house with a bellyful.

proverb_native: De cosário á cosário no se llevan sino los barriles
proverb_en: Corsair against corsair nothing is got but empty casks.

proverb_native: De cuero ageno correas largas
proverb_en: Large thongs of another man’s leather.

proverb_native: De diestro á diestro el mas presto
proverb_en: Between two sharpers, the sharpest.

proverb_native: De dineros y bondad, siempre quita la mitad
proverb_en: In the report, of riches and goodness always bate one half.

proverb_native: Dedo de espada, palma de lanza, es gran ventaja
proverb_en: An inch in a sword, or a palm in a lance, is a great advantage.

proverb_native: De do sacan y no pon, presto llegan al hondon
proverb_en: Taking out and not putting in soon reaches the bottom.

proverb_native: De gran subida gran caida
proverb_en: The higher the rise the greater the fall.

proverb_native: De haré, haré, nunca me pagué; mas vale un toma que dos te daré
proverb_en: I never was satisfied with “I will, I will.” One “take this” is better than two “I will give you.”

proverb_native: De herrero á herrero no pasa dinero
proverb_en: Between Smith and Smith no money passes.

proverb_native: De hombre que no habla, y de can que no ladra, libera nos
proverb_en: From a silent man, and a dog that does not bark, deliver us.

proverb_native: Del agua mansa me libre Dios, que de la recia me guardaré yo
proverb_en: From smooth water God preserve me, from rough I will preserve myself.

proverb_native: Del alcalde al verdugo, ved como subo
proverb_en: See how he has risen from a mayor to a hangman.

proverb_native: De la mala muger te guarda, y de la buena no fies nada
proverb_en: Beware of a bad woman, and put no trust in a good one.

proverb_native: De la mano á la boca se pierde la sopa
proverb_en: Between the hand and the mouth the soup is lost.

proverb_native: De la nieve, ni cocida, ni majada, no sacarás sino agua
proverb_en: From snow, whether baked or boiled, you will get nothing but water.

proverb_native: Del dicho al hecho hay gran trecho
proverb_en: Between saying and doing there is a long road.

proverb_native: Del mal el menor
proverb_en: Of evils, the least.

proverb_native: Del mal que hombre teme, de ese muere
proverb_en: Of the malady a man fears, he dies.

proverb_native: De lo contado come el lobo
proverb_en: The wolf eats of what is counted.

proverb_native: De los leales se hinchen los hospitales
proverb_en: The poor-houses are filled with the honestest people.

proverb_native: De luengas vias luengas mentiras
proverb_en: From long journeys long lies.

proverb_native: Del pan de mi compadre buen zatico á mi ahijado
proverb_en: From my gossip’s bread a large piece for my godson.

proverb_native: De me digan, y á mi pidan
proverb_en: Let them talk of me, and beg of me.

proverb_native: De mozo rezador, y de viejo ayunador, guarde Dios mi capa
proverb_en: From a praying young man, and a fasting old man, God preserve my cloak.

proverb_native: De noche los gatos todos son pardos
proverb_en: At night all cats are grey.

proverb_native: De padre santo, hijo diablo
proverb_en: The father a saint, the son a sinner.

proverb_native: De pequeña centella, gran hoguera
proverb_en: A little spark kindles a great fire.

proverb_native: De persona callada arriedra tu morada
proverb_en: From a silent person remove your dwelling.

proverb_native: De piel agena larga la correa
proverb_en: Of other men’s leather large thongs.

proverb_native: De potro sarnoso buen caballo hermoso
proverb_en: A scabby colt may make a good horse.

proverb_native: De puerta cerrada el diablo se torna
proverb_en: The devil turns away from a closed door.

proverb_native: De quien pone los ojos en el suelo no fies tu dinero
proverb_en: Trust not your money to one whose eyes are bent on the ground.

proverb_native: De rabo de puerco nunca buen virote
proverb_en: A pig’s tail will never make a good arrow.

proverb_native: Derecho apurado, tuerto tornado
proverb_en: Right overstrained turns to wrong.

proverb_native: De ruin á ruin, quien acomete vence
proverb_en: Coward against coward, the assailant conquers.

proverb_native: De ruin paño nunca buen sayo
proverb_en: There’s no making a good cloak of bad cloth.

proverb_native: Desaprovechado como unto de mona
proverb_en: As useless as monkey’s fat.

proverb_native: Descalabrar al alguacil, y acogerse al corregidor
proverb_en: To break the constable’s head, and take refuge with the sheriff.

proverb_native: Desde que te erré, nunca bien te quisé
proverb_en: Since I wronged you, I have never liked you.

proverb_native: Deshacer cruzes en un pajar
proverb_en: To undo crosses in a straw loft

proverb_native: Despues de descalabrado untarle el casco
proverb_en: After breaking my head you bring plaister.

proverb_native: Despues del daño cada uno es sabio
proverb_en: Every one is wise when the mischief is done.

proverb_native: Despues de vendimias, cestos
proverb_en: After the vintage, baskets.

proverb_native: Despues que la casa está hecha, la deja
proverb_en: After the house is finished, he deserts it.

proverb_native: Despues que me estas castigando, ciento y veinte agujeros conté en aquel rallo. Since you have been scolding me,
proverb_en: I have counted a hundred and twenty holes in that nutmeg grater.

proverb_native: Desque nací lloré, y cada dia nace porqué
proverb_en: When I was born I wept, and every day brings a reason why.

proverb_native: Desquitóse Miguel; perdió un ducado y ganó un conejo
proverb_en: Michael is quits; he lost a ducat and gained a rabbit.

proverb_native: De traidor harás leal con bien hablar
proverb_en: Give a traitor good words and you make him loyal.

proverb_native: Detras de la cruz está el diablo
proverb_en: The devil lurks behind the cross.

proverb_native: De tu muger y de tu amigo esperto, no creas sino lo que supieres cierto
proverb_en: Of your wife and your tried friend believe nothing but what you know for certain.

proverb_native: Deudas tienes, y haces mas; si no mentiste, mentiras
proverb_en: You have debts, and make debts still; if you’ve not lied, lie you will.

proverb_native: De un hombre necio á vezes buen consejo
proverb_en: A fool sometimes gives good counsel.

proverb_native: De un solo golpe no se derrueca un roble
proverb_en: An oak is not felled at one stroke.

proverb_native: Deve algo para Pascua, y hacérsete ha corta la cuaresma
proverb_en: Have a bill to pay at Easter, and your Lent will be short.

proverb_native: Di á tu amigo tu secreto, y tenerte ha el pie en el pescuezo
proverb_en: Tell your friend your secret, and he will set his foot on your neck.

proverb_native: Dicen los niños en el solejar lo que oyen á sus padres en el hogar
proverb_en: What children hear their parents say by the fireside they repeat in the highway.

proverb_native: Dijó la sarten á la caldera, Tirte allá, cul negra
proverb_en: Said the frying pan to the kettle, Stand off, black bottom.

proverb_native: Dile que es hermosa, y tornarse ha loca
proverb_en: Tell her she is handsome, and you will turn her head.

proverb_native: Dime con quien andas, diréte quien eres
proverb_en: Tell me what company you keep, and I will tell you who you are.

proverb_native: Di mentira, y sacarás verdad
proverb_en: Tell a lie, and you will bring out the truth.

proverb_native: Dinero llama dinero
proverb_en: Money gets money.

proverb_native: Dineros de avaro dos veces van al mercado
proverb_en: Misers’ money goes twice to market.

proverb_native: Dios es el que sana, y el médico lleva la plata
proverb_en: God cures, and the doctor takes the fee.

proverb_native: Dios me dé contienda con quien me entienda
proverb_en: God grant me to argue with those who understand me.

proverb_native: Dios me libre de hombre de un libro
proverb_en: God deliver me from a man of one book.

proverb_native: Dios os libre de hidalgo de dia, y de fraile de noche
proverb_en: God deliver us from a gentleman by day and a friar by night.

proverb_native: Dios proveerá, mas buen haz de paja se querrá
proverb_en: God will provide, but a good bundle of straw will not be amiss.

proverb_native: Dios te dé fortuna, hijo, que el saber poco te vale
proverb_en: God grant you fortune, my son, for knowledge avails you little.

proverb_native: Dísela tú una vez, que el diablo se la dirá diez
proverb_en: Tell it her once, and the devil will tell it her ten times.

proverb_native: Do entra beber, sale saber
proverb_en: When drink enters, wisdom departs.

proverb_native: Do falta dicha, por demas es diligencia
proverb_en: Where luck is wanting, diligence is useless.

proverb_native: Do fueres, harás como vieres
proverb_en: Wherever you are, do as you see done.

proverb_native: Do fuerza viene, derecho se pierde
proverb_en: Where force prevails, right perishes.

proverb_native: Donde fuiste paje, no seas escudero
proverb_en: Be not an esquire where you were a page.

proverb_native: Donde hay gana, hay maña
proverb_en: Where there’s a will there’s a way.

proverb_native: Donde huego se hace, humo sale
proverb_en: Where there’s fire there’s smoke.

proverb_native: Donde irá el buey que no are?
proverb_en: Where shall the ox go and not plough?

proverb_native: Donde menos se piensa salta la liebre
proverb_en: The hare starts from where she is least expected.

proverb_native: Donde os comieron la carne, que roan los huesos
proverb_en: Where they eat your meat let them pick the bones.

proverb_native: Donde perdiste la capa, ay la cata
proverb_en: Where you lost your cloak, seek it.

proverb_native: Donde una puerta se cierra, otra se abre
proverb_en: Where one door is shut another opens.

proverb_native: Dormireis sobre ello, y tomareis acuerdo
proverb_en: Sleep over it, and you will come to a resolution.

proverb_native: Dos amigos de una bolsa, el uno canta, el otro llora
proverb_en: When there are two friends to one purse, one sings, the other weeps.

proverb_native: Dos aves de rapiña no mantienen compaña
proverb_en: Two birds of prey do not keep each other company.

proverb_native: Do tu padre fué con tinta, no vayas tu con quilma
proverb_en: Where your father has been with ink, go not you with a bag

proverb_native: Do va mas hondo el rio, hace menos ruido
proverb_en: Where the river is deepest it makes least noise.

proverb_native: Duerme á quien duele, y no duerme quien algo deve
proverb_en: A sick man sleeps, but not a debtor.

proverb_native: Echar el mango tras el destral
proverb_en: To throw the helve after the hatchet.

proverb_native: Echar la pluma al aire, y ver donde cae
proverb_en: To throw up a feather in the air, and see where it falls.

proverb_native: Echar un virote tras otro
proverb_en: To send one arrow after another.

proverb_native: Echate á enfermar, verás quien te quiere bien, y quien te quiere mal
proverb_en: Fall sick, and you will see who is your friend and who not.

proverb_native: El abad de Bamba, lo que no puede comer, dalo por su alma
proverb_en: What the abbot of Bamba cannot eat he gives away for the good of his soul.

proverb_native: El agujero llama al ladron
proverb_en: The hole invites the thief.

proverb_native: El amenazador hace perder el lugar de venganza
proverb_en: The threatener loses the opportunity of vengeance.

proverb_native: El amor verdadero no sufre cosa encubierta
proverb_en: True love suffers no concealment.

proverb_native: El amor y la fe en las obras se vee
proverb_en: Love and faith are seen in works.

proverb_native: El bien suena y el mal vuela
proverb_en: Good news is rumoured and bad news flies.

proverb_native: El bobo, si es callado, por sesudo es reputado
proverb_en: A fool, if he holds his tongue, passes for wise.

proverb_native: El buen hombre goza el hurto
proverb_en: The honest man enjoys the theft.

proverb_native: El buen pagador señor es de lo ageno
proverb_en: A good paymaster is keeper of others’ purses.

proverb_native: El buen vino la venta trae consigo
proverb_en: Good wine sells itself.

proverb_native: El buey bravo en tierra agena se hace manso
proverb_en: The fierce ox becomes tame on strange ground.

proverb_native: El buey quando se cansa, firme sienta la pata
proverb_en: The tired ox plants his foot firmly.

proverb_native: El buey que me acornó, en buen lugar me echó
proverb_en: The ox that butted me tossed me into a good place.

proverb_native: El buey sin cencerro piérdese presto
proverb_en: The ox without a bell is soon lost.

proverb_native: El campo fertil no descansando, torna se esteril
proverb_en: The fertile field becomes sterile without rest.

proverb_native: El can de buena raza, si hoy no caza, mañana caza
proverb_en: The well-bred hound, if he does not hunt to-day will hunt to-morrow.

proverb_native: El caracol, por quitar de enojos, por los cuernos trocó los ojos
proverb_en: The snail, to be rid of annoyances, bartered its eyes for horns.

proverb_native: El carnero encantado que fué por lana, y volvió trasquilado
proverb_en: The deceived sheep that went for wool and came back shorn.

proverb_native: El cebo es el que engaña, que no el pescador ni la caña
proverb_en: It is the bait that lures, not the fisherman or the rod.

proverb_native: El conejo ido, el consejo venido
proverb_en: When the rabbit has escaped, comes advice.

proverb_native: El corazon no es traidor
proverb_en: The heart is no traitor.

proverb_native: El corcobado no vee su corcoba, y vee la de su compañon
proverb_en: The hunchback does not see his own hump, but sees his companion’s.

proverb_native: El cordero manso mama á su madre, y á qualquiera; el bravo ni á la suya, ni á la agena
proverb_en: The gentle lamb sucks any ewe as well as its mother; the surly lamb sucks neither its own nor another.

proverb_native: El cuerdo no ata el saber á estaca
proverb_en: The wise man does not hang his knowledge on a hook.

proverb_native: El dar es honor, y el pedir dolor
proverb_en: To give is honour, to lose is grief.

proverb_native: El dar limosna nunca mengua la bolsa
proverb_en: Giving alms never lessens the purse.

proverb_native: El deseo hace hermoso lo feo
proverb_en: Desire beautifies what is ugly.

proverb_native: El dia de ayuno vespera es de disanto
proverb_en: A fast day is the eve of a feast day.

proverb_native: El dia de calor, ese te arropa mejor
proverb_en: On a hot day muffle yourself the more.

proverb_native: El dia que no escobé entró quien, no piense
proverb_en: The day I did not sweep the house, there came to it one I did not expect.

proverb_native: El dia que no me afeyté, vino á mi casa quien no pensé
proverb_en: The day I did not make my toilette, there came to my house one I did not expect.

proverb_native: El dia que te casas, ó te matas ó te sanas
proverb_en: The day you marry ’tis either kill or cure.

proverb_native: El dinero hace lo malo bueno
proverb_en: Money turns bad into good.

proverb_native: El estiercol no es santo, mas do cae hace milagro
proverb_en: Dung is no saint, but where it falls it works miracles.

proverb_native: El golpe de la sarten, aunque no duele, tizna
proverb_en: A blow from a frying-pan, if it does not hurt, smuts.

proverb_native: El habito no hace al monge
proverb_en: The dress does not make the friar.

proverb_native: El hijo del asno dos veces rozna al dia
proverb_en: The son of an ass brays twice a day.

proverb_native: El hijo sabe que conoce á su padre
proverb_en: It is a wise son that knows his own father.

proverb_native: El hilo por lo mas delgado quiebra
proverb_en: The thread breaks where it is thinnest.

proverb_native: El hombre es el fuego, la muger la estopa; viene el diablo y sopla
proverb_en: Man is fire, woman is tow; the devil comes with a bellows.

proverb_native: El hombre necesitado cada año apedreado
proverb_en: The poor man has his crop destroyed by hail every year.

proverb_native: El hombre propone, y Dios dispone
proverb_en: Man proposes, and God disposes.

proverb_native: El huego y el amor no dicen: Vate á tu lavor
proverb_en: Fire and love do not say “Go to your work.”

proverb_native: El huesped y el pez á tres dias huele
proverb_en: A guest and a fish stink in three days.

proverb_native: El hurtar es cosa linda, si colgasen por la pretina
proverb_en: Stealing would be a nice thing, if thieves were hanged by the girdle.

proverb_native: El infierno está lleno de buenas palabras
proverb_en: Hell is full of good intentions.

proverb_native: El invierno es ido, y el verano venido, mal haya quien bien nos hizo
proverb_en: The winter is gone, the spring is come, a fig for those who us good have done.

proverb_native: El judío échase á perder con pascuas, el moro con bodas, y el cristiano con escrituras
proverb_en: The Jew ruins himself with passovers, the Moor with wedding feasts, and the Christian with lawsuits.

proverb_native: El lobo do halla un cordero, busca otro
proverb_en: Where the wolf gets one lamb it looks for another.

proverb_native: El lobo do mane daño no hace
proverb_en: The wolf commits no mischief at home.

proverb_native: El lobo hace entre semana por donde no va el Domingo á misa
proverb_en: The wolf does that in the course of the week which hinders him from going to mass on Sunday.

proverb_native: El lobo pierde los dientes, mas no los mientes
proverb_en: The wolf loses his teeth, but not his inclinations.

proverb_native: El lobo y la vulpeja ambos son de una conseja
proverb_en: The wolf and the fox are both in one story.

proverb_native: El mal de milano, las alas quebradas y el pico sano
proverb_en: The kite’s malady, its wings broken and its beak sound.

proverb_native: El mal entra á brazadas, y sale á pulgaradas
proverb_en: Ill luck enters by fathoms and departs by inches.

proverb_native: El malo siempre piensa engaño
proverb_en: The bad man always suspects knavery.

proverb_native: El mal que de tu boca sale, en tu seno se cae
proverb_en: The evil which issues from thy mouth falls into thy bosom.

proverb_native: El mal que no tiene cura es locura
proverb_en: Folly is the most incurable of maladies.

proverb_native: El marido antes con un ojo que con un hijo
proverb_en: A husband with one eye rather than with a son.

proverb_native: El mejor lance de los dados es no jugarlos
proverb_en: The best cast at dice is not to play.

proverb_native: El mejor pienso del cavallo es el ojo de su amo
proverb_en: The best feed of a horse is his master’s eye.

proverb_native: El melon y la muger malos son de conocer
proverb_en: A melon and a woman are hard to know.

proverb_native: El mentir no tiene alcabala
proverb_en: There is no tax upon lying.

proverb_native: El mentir y el compadrar ambos audan á la par
proverb_en: Lying and gossipping go hand in hand.

proverb_native: El mozo perezoso por no dar un paso da ocho
proverb_en: The lazy servant to save one step takes eight.

proverb_native: El mur que no sabe mas de un horado, presto le toma el gato
proverb_en: The mouse that knows but one hole is soon caught by the cat.

proverb_native: El oficial que no miente, sálgase de entre la gente
proverb_en: The official who can’t lie may as well be out of the world.

proverb_native: El ojo del amo engorda el caballo
proverb_en: The eye of the master fattens the steed.

proverb_native: El ojo limpiale con el codo
proverb_en: Wipe your eye with your elbow.

proverb_native: El pan comido, la compañía deshecha
proverb_en: The bread eaten, the company departed.

proverb_native: El perro del herrero duerme á las martilladas, y despierta á las dentelladas
proverb_en: The smith’s dog sleeps at the noise of the hammer, and wakes at the grinding of teeth.

proverb_native: El perro del hortelano ni come las berzas, ni las deja comer al estraño
proverb_en: The gardener’s dog neither eats greens nor lets any one else eat them.

proverb_native: El perro del hortelano, ni hambriento ni harto
proverb_en: The gardener’s dog, neither full nor hungry.

proverb_native: El perro viejo, si ladra, da consejo
proverb_en: When the old dog barks he gives counsel.

proverb_native: El pie del dueño estierco para la heredad
proverb_en: The master’s foot is manure for the estate.

proverb_native: El porfiado albardan comerá de tu pan
proverb_en: The busy fly is in every man’s dish.

proverb_native: El puerco sarnoso revuelve la pocilga
proverb_en: A measly hog infects the whole sty.

proverb_native: El que á su enemigo popa, á sus manos muere
proverb_en: He who trifles with his enemy dies by his hand.

proverb_native: El que es enemigo de la novia, como dirá bien de la boda?
proverb_en: How shall the enemy of the bride speak well of the wedding?

proverb_native: El que está en el lodo querria meter á otro
proverb_en: He who is in the mud likes to get another into it.

proverb_native: El que está en la aceña muele, que no el que va y viene
proverb_en: He who remains in the mill grinds, not he who goes to and fro.

proverb_native: El que fué monacelo, y despues abad, sabe lo que hacen los mozos tras el altar
proverb_en: He who has been first a novice and then an abbot, knows what the boys do behind the altar.

proverb_native: El que ha ovejas, ha pellejas
proverb_en: He who has sheep has fleeces.

proverb_native: El que ley establece, guardarla debe
proverb_en: He who makes a law should keep it.

proverb_native: El que no duda, no sabe cosa alguna
proverb_en: He who doubts nothing knows nothing.

proverb_native: El que no tiene casa de suyo, vecino es de todo el mundo
proverb_en: He who has no house of his own is everywhere at home.

proverb_native: El que no tiene muger, cada dia la mata; mas quien la tiene, bien se la guarda
proverb_en: He who has no wife, is for thrashing her daily; but he that has one, takes care of her.

proverb_native: El que tarda, recauda
proverb_en: He who delays, gathers.

proverb_native: El que tiene tejados de vidrio, no tire piedras al de su vecino
proverb_en: He whose house is tiled with glass should not throw stones at his neighbour’s.

proverb_native: El rayo y el amor, la ropa sana y quemado el corazon
proverb_en: With lightning and with love, the clothes sound, the heart burned.

proverb_native: El rey va hasta do puede, y no hasta do quiere
proverb_en: The king goes as far as he can, not so far as he would.

proverb_native: El ruin barbero ni deja pelo ni cuero
proverb_en: The bad barber leaves neither hair nor skin.

proverb_native: El sol me luzga, que de la luna no he cura
proverb_en: Let the sun shine on me, for I care not for the moon.

proverb_native: El tocino de paraiso para el casado no arrepiso
proverb_en: The bacon of paradise for the married man that does not repent.

proverb_native: El tramposo presto engaña al codicioso
proverb_en: The sharper soon cheats the covetous man.

proverb_native: El usar saca oficial
proverb_en: Practice makes perfect.

proverb_native: El viejo en su tierra y el mozo en la agena, mienten de una manera
proverb_en: The old man at home, and the young abroad, lie after the same fashion.

proverb_native: El viejo por no poder, y el mozo por no saber, dejan las cosas perder
proverb_en: The old for want of ability, and the young for want of knowledge, let things be lost.

proverb_native: El vientre ayuno no oye á ninguno
proverb_en: A hungry belly listens to no one.

proverb_native: El vino anda sin calças
proverb_en: Wine wears no breeches.

proverb_native: El vino bueno no ha menester pregonero
proverb_en: Good wine needs no crier.

proverb_native: En arca abierta el justo peca
proverb_en: The righteous man sins before an open chest.

proverb_native: En boca cerrada no entra mosca
proverb_en: No fly gets into a shut mouth.

proverb_native: En buen dia buenas obras
proverb_en: The better day the better deed.

proverb_native: En cada tierra su uso
proverb_en: Every country has its custom.

proverb_native: En casa del ahorcado no se ha de mentar la soga
proverb_en: Never speak of a rope in the house of a man who was hanged.

proverb_native: En casa del gaitero todos son danzantes
proverb_en: In the bagpiper’s house they are all dancers.

proverb_native: En casa del herrero cuchillo de palo
proverb_en: In a smith’s house the knife is wooden.

proverb_native: En casa del moro no hables algarabía
proverb_en: Do not talk Arabic in the house of a Moor.

proverb_native: En casa de muger rica, ella manda siempre, y él nunca
proverb_en: In the rich woman’s house she always commands; he never.

proverb_native: En caza y en amores, entras quando quieres, y sales quando puedes
proverb_en: In hunting and in love you begin when you like and leave off when you can.

proverb_native: Encomendar las ovejas al lobo
proverb_en: To commit the sheep to the care of the wolf.

proverb_native: En el almoneda tien la boca queda
proverb_en: At an auction keep your mouth shut.

proverb_native: En el mejor paño cae la raza
proverb_en: The best cloth has uneven threads.

proverb_native: En el paño mas fino se ve mas la mancha
proverb_en: A spot shows most on the finest cloth.

proverb_native: En el rio do no hay pezes por demas es echar redes
proverb_en: It is in vain to cast nets in a river where there are no fish.

proverb_native: Engáñame en el precio, y no en lo que merco
proverb_en: Cheat me in the price and not in the goods.

proverb_native: En hora buena vengais, mal, si viens solo
proverb_en: Welcome, misfortune, if thou comest alone.

proverb_native: En la boda quien menos come es la novia
proverb_en: At the wedding-feast the least eater is the bride.

proverb_native: En quanto fué nuera, nunca tuve buena suegra, y en quanto fuí suegra, nunca tuve buena nuera
proverb_en: As long as I was a daughter-in-law I never had a good mother-in-law, and as long as I was a mother-in-law I never had a good daughter-in-law.

proverb_native: En salvo está el que repica
proverb_en: He is out of danger who rings the alarm-bell.

proverb_native: En tiempo helado el clavo vale un caballo
proverb_en: In frosty weather a nail is worth a horse.

proverb_native: Entienda primero, y habla postrero
proverb_en: Hear first, and speak afterwards.

proverb_native: En tierra de ciegos el tuerto es rey
proverb_en: The one-eyed man is a king in the country of the blind.

proverb_native: En tierra seca el agua salobre es buena
proverb_en: On dry land even brackish water is good.

proverb_native: Entre dos amigos un notario y dos testigos
proverb_en: Between two friends a notary and two witnesses.

proverb_native: Entre dos muelas molares nunca metas tus pulgares
proverb_en: Never put your thumbs between two grinders.

proverb_native: Entre tanto que cria, amamos el ama; pasado el provecho, luego olvidada
proverb_en: Whilst the nurse suckles, we love her; when she is of no further use, she is forgotten.

proverb_native: En voto del conde no mates al hombre
proverb_en: Don’t kill the man at the count’s desire.

proverb_native: Esa es buena que está al fuego y no se quema
proverb_en: She is good who is close to the fire and does not burn.

proverb_native: Esa es buena y honrada que es muerta y sepultada
proverb_en: She is good and honoured who is dead and buried.

proverb_native: Escrive antes que des, y recibe antes que escrivas
proverb_en: Let there be writing before you pay, and receipt before you write.

proverb_native: Escuchas al agujero, oirás de tú mal y del ageno
proverb_en: If you listen at a hole, you will hear ill of yourself as well as others.

proverb_native: Ese es mi amigo el que muele en mi molinillo
proverb_en: He is my friend who grinds at my mill.

proverb_native: Fiar de Dios sobre buena prenda
proverb_en: Trust in God upon good security.

proverb_native: For secreto, lo fumo lo descovre
proverb_en: Secret fire is discovered by its smoke.

proverb_native: Fraile que pide por Dios, pide por dos
proverb_en: The friar who begs for God begs for two.

proverb_native: Frailes sobrand’ojo alerte
proverb_en: Where friars abound keep your eyes open.

proverb_native: Frailes, viver con ellos, y comer con ellos, y andar con ellos, y luego vendellos, que así hacen ellos
proverb_en: As for friars, live with them, eat with them, and walk with them; then sell them as they do themselves.

proverb_native: Fuego, fuego, muchas ollas y un garbanzo en todas
proverb_en: Fire, fire, many pots on, and one pea in them all.

proverb_native: Galgo que muchas liebras levanta, ninguna mata
proverb_en: The greyhound that starts many hares kills none.

proverb_native: Gana tiene de coles quien besa al hortelano
proverb_en: She is fond of greens who kisses the gardener.

proverb_native: Gato escaldado del agua fria ha miedo
proverb_en: The scalded cat dreads cold water.

proverb_native: Gato maullador nunca buen cazador
proverb_en: A mewing cat is never a good mouser.

proverb_native: Gloria vana florece, y no grana
proverb_en: Vainglory blossoms, and bears no fruit.

proverb_native: Goza tú de tu poco, mientras busca mas el loco
proverb_en: Enjoy your little whilst the fool is seeking for more.

proverb_native: Grano no henche harnero, mas ayuda á su compañero
proverb_en: A grain does not fill a sieve, but it helps its fellow.

proverb_native: Gran sabor es comer y no escotar
proverb_en: It is very savoury to eat scot free.

proverb_native: Grano á grano hinche la gallina el papo
proverb_en: Grain by grain the hen fills her crop.

proverb_native: Guárdete Dios de hecho es
proverb_en: God keep you from “It is too late.”

proverb_native: Guayas! padre, que otra hija os nasce
proverb_en: Alas! father, another daughter is born to you.

proverb_native: Habla poco y bien, tenerte han por alquien
proverb_en: Talk little and well, and you will be looked upon as somebody.

proverb_native: Habla de la caza, y cómprala en la plaza
proverb_en: Talk of sporting, and buy game in the market.

proverb_native: Habló el buey y dijó Mu
proverb_en: The ox spoke and said “Moo.”

proverb_native: Hacer la cuenta sin la huéspeda
proverb_en: To reckon without the hostess.

proverb_native: Hágase el milagro, y hágalo el diablo
proverb_en: Let the miracle be wrought, though it be by the devil.

proverb_native: Halagar con la cola, y morder con la boca
proverb_en: To fawn with the tail, and bite with the mouth.

proverb_native: Hambre y frio entregan al hombre á su enemigo
proverb_en: Hunger and cold give a man up to his enemy

proverb_native: Harto es ciego quien no vee por tela de cedazo
proverb_en: He is blind enough who cannot see through a sieve

proverb_native: Harto es necio y loco, quien vacue su cuero por henchir el de otro
proverb_en: He is a great simpleton who starves himself to feed another

proverb_native: Hay buena cuenta, y no paresca blanca
proverb_en: The account is correct, but not a sixpence appears

proverb_native: Haz buena harina, y no toques bocina
proverb_en: Make good flour, and do not blow the trumpet

proverb_native: Haz lo que bien digo, y no lo que mal hago
proverb_en: Do what I say well, and not what I do ill

proverb_native: Haz lo que dice el fraile, y no lo que hace
proverb_en: Do what the friar says, and not what he does

proverb_native: Herradura que chacotea, clavo le falta
proverb_en: The horseshoe that clatters wants a nail

proverb_native: Hidalgo honrado antes roto que remendado
proverb_en: A true gentleman would rather have his clothes torn than mended

proverb_native: Hijo del alcalde con todo sale
proverb_en: The magistrate’s son gets out of every scrape

proverb_native: Hijo no tenemos, y nombre le ponemos
proverb_en: We have no son, and yet are giving him a name

proverb_native: Hilo y aguja, media vestidura
proverb_en: Needle and thread are half clothing

proverb_native: Hizonos Dios, y maravillámonos nos
proverb_en: God made us, and we admire ourselves

proverb_native: Hombre apercebido vale por dos
proverb_en: A man forewarned is as good as two.

proverb_native: Hombre harto no es comedor
proverb_en: A man that has had his fill is no eater

proverb_native: Hombre pobre todo es trazas
proverb_en: A poor man is all schemes

proverb_native: Hombre que no tiene cabeza no ha menester bonete
proverb_en: He who has no head wants no hat

proverb_native: Huéspeda hermosa mal para la bolsa
proverb_en: A handsome hostess is bad for the purse

proverb_native: Huir y correr no es todo uno
proverb_en: To flee and to run are not all one

proverb_native: Humo y gotera, y la muger parlera, echan al hombre de su casa fuera
proverb_en: Smoke, a dripping roof, and a scolding wife, are enough to drive a man out of his life

proverb_native: Hurtar el puerco, y dar los pies por Dios
proverb_en: To steal the pig, and give away the pettitoes for God’s sake.

proverb_native: Huyendo del toro, cayó en el arroyo
proverb_en: Flying from the bull he fell into the river.

proverb_native: Id por medio, y no careis
proverb_en: Take the middle of the way and thou wilt not fall.

proverb_native: Iglesia, ó mar, ó casa real, quien quiere medrar
proverb_en: The church, the sea, or the royal household, for whoever would thrive.

proverb_native: Ira de hermanos, ira de diablos
proverb_en: The wrath of brothers is the wrath of devils.

proverb_native: Ir á la guerra ni casar, no se ha de aconsejar
proverb_en: Never advise a man to go to the wars, or to marry.

proverb_native: Ir por lana, y volver trasquilado
proverb_en: To go for wool and come back shorn.

proverb_native: Juego de manos, juego de villanos
proverb_en: Manual play, clowns’ play.

proverb_native: Jurado ha el vano de negro no hacer blanco
proverb_en: The bath has sworn not to whiten the blackamoor.

proverb_native: Juras del que ama muger, no se han de creer
proverb_en: The oaths of one who loves a woman are not to be believed.

proverb_native: Justa razon engañar el engañador
proverb_en: It is fair and just to cheat the cheater.

proverb_native: Justicia, mas no por mi casa
proverb_en: Justice, but not in my own house.

proverb_native: La bestia que mucho anda, nunca falta quien la taña
proverb_en: The beast that goes well is never without some one to try his paces.

proverb_native: La boca y la bolsa, cerrada
proverb_en: The mouth and the purse, shut.

proverb_native: La buena vida padre y madre olvida
proverb_en: Prosperity forgets father and mother.

proverb_native: La burla dineros cuesta
proverb_en: Jesting costs money.

proverb_native: La cabra de mi vecina mas leche da que no la mia
proverb_en: My neighbour’s goat gives more milk than mine.

proverb_native: La carcel y la quaresma para los pobres es hecha
proverb_en: Prison and Lent were made for the poor.

proverb_native: La caridad bien ordenada comenza de sí propia
proverb_en: Charity well regulated begins at home.

proverb_native: La casa quemada, acudir con el agua
proverb_en: To fetch water after the house is burned.

proverb_native: La codicia rompe el saco
proverb_en: Covetousness bursts the bag.

proverb_native: La costumbre es otra naturaleza
proverb_en: Custom is second nature.

proverb_native: La coz de la yegua no hace mal al potro
proverb_en: The mare’s kick does not harm the colt.

proverb_native: La cruz en los pechos, y el diablo en los hechos
proverb_en: The cross on his breast, and the devil in his acts.

proverb_native: La cuba huele al vino que tiene
proverb_en: The cask smells of the wine it contains.

proverb_native: La cuba llena, la suegra bevida
proverb_en: The cask full, the mother-in-law drunk.

proverb_native: Ládreme el perro, y no me muerda
proverb_en: Let the dog bark so he don’t bite me.

proverb_native: La espada y la sortija, en cuya mano estan
proverb_en: The sword and the ring according to the hand that bears them.

proverb_native: La espina quando nace, la punta lleva delante
proverb_en: The thorn comes into the world point foremost.

proverb_native: La gala del nadar es saber guardar la ropa
proverb_en: The secret in swimming is to know how to take care of your clothes.

proverb_native: La gallina de mi vecina mas huevos pone que la mia
proverb_en: My neighbour’s hen lays more eggs than mine.

proverb_native: La gente pone, y Dios dispone
proverb_en: Man proposes, and God disposes.

proverb_native: La gotera dando hace señal en la piedra
proverb_en: The gutter by dropping wears the stone.

proverb_native: La hogaça no embaraça
proverb_en: One’s prog does not clog.

proverb_native: La horca lo suyo lleva
proverb_en: The gallows takes its own.

proverb_native: La justicia de Peralvillo que ahorcado el hombre le hace la pes quisa
proverb_en: Peralvillo justice: hang a man first and try him afterwards.

proverb_native: La lengua del mal amigo mas corta que cuchillo
proverb_en: The tongue of a bad friend cuts more than a knife.

proverb_native: La lengua luenga es señal de mano corta
proverb_en: A long tongue betokens a short hand.

proverb_native: La letra con sangre entra
proverb_en: The letter enters with blood.

proverb_native: La mala llaga sana, la mala fama mata
proverb_en: A bad wound may be cured, bad repute kills.

proverb_native: La mano cuerda no hace todo lo que due la lengua
proverb_en: The wise hand does not all that the tongue says.

proverb_native: La manzana podrida perde a su compaña
proverb_en: The rotten apple spoils its companion.

proverb_native: La mas cauta es tenida por mas casta
proverb_en: The most cautious passes for the most chaste.

proverb_native: La mas ruin oveja se ensucia en la colodra
proverb_en: The worst ewe dungs in the milking-pail.

proverb_native: La moça como es criada, la estopa como es hilada
proverb_en: The girl as she is taught, the flax as it is wrought.

proverb_native: La muger compuesta á su marido quita de puerta agena
proverb_en: The well-dressed woman draws her husband away from another woman’s door.

proverb_native: La muger del ciego para quien se affeyta?
proverb_en: For whom does the blind man’s wife adorn herself?

proverb_native: La muger hermosa ó loca ó presuntuosa
proverb_en: A handsome woman is either silly or vain.

proverb_native: La muger polida, la casa sucia, la puerta barrida
proverb_en: The woman in finery, the house in filth, but the doorway swept.

proverb_native: La muger quanto mas mira la cara, tanto mas destruye la casa
proverb_en: The more a woman admires her face, the more she ruins her house.

proverb_native: La muger vieja, si no serve de olla, serve de cobertera
proverb_en: The old wife, if she does not serve for a pot, serves for a cover.

proverb_native: La muger y el vidrio siempre estan en peligro
proverb_en: A woman and a glass are always in danger.

proverb_native: La muger y la salsa á la man de la lança
proverb_en: Your wife and the sauce at the lance hand (the right hand).

proverb_native: La muger y la tela, no las cates á la candela
proverb_en: Choose neither a woman nor linen by candlelight.

proverb_native: La mula y la muger por balagos hacen el mandado
proverb_en: A mule and a woman do what is expected of them.

proverb_native: La necesidad hace á la viega trotar
proverb_en: Need makes the old woman trot.

proverb_native: La occasion hace el ladron
proverb_en: Opportunity makes the thief.

proverb_native: La oveja harta, del rabo hace manta
proverb_en: The well-fed sheep makes a cloak of its tail.

proverb_native: La piedra es dura, y la gota menuda, mas cayendo de continuo, hace cavadura
proverb_en: The stone is hard and the drop is small, but a hole is made by the constant fall.

proverb_native: La pobreza no quita virtud, ni la riqueza la pone
proverb_en: Poverty does not destroy virtue, nor does wealth bestow it.

proverb_native: La primera muger escoba, y la segunda señora
proverb_en: The first wife is a broom, and the second a lady.

proverb_native: La quinta rueda al carro no hace sino embaraçar
proverb_en: A fifth wheel to a cart is but an encumbrance.

proverb_native: La sangre sin fuego hierve
proverb_en: Blood boils without fire.

proverb_native: Las llaves en la cinta, y el perro en la cocina
proverb_en: The keys at the girdle, the dog in the larder.

proverb_native: Las malas nuevas siempre son ciertas
proverb_en: Bad news is always true.

proverb_native: Las manos blancas no ofenden
proverb_en: White hands are no offence.

proverb_native: Las sopas y los amores, los primieros son mejores
proverb_en: Of soups and loves the first are the best.

proverb_native: Las tripas esten llenas, que ellas llevan á las piernas
proverb_en: Let the guts be full, for it is they that carry the legs.

proverb_native: La suegra ha de ser rogada, y olla reposada
proverb_en: The mother-in-law must be entreated, and the pot must be let stand.

proverb_native: La telaraña suelta al rato, y la mosca apana
proverb_en: The spider’s web lets the rat escape and catches the fly.

proverb_native: La tierra que el hombre sabe, esa es su madre
proverb_en: The land a man knows is his mother.

proverb_native: La traicion aplace, mas no el que la hace
proverb_en: The act of treachery is liked, but not he that does it.

proverb_native: La una mano á la otra lava, y las dos á la cara
proverb_en: One hand washes the other, and both the face.

proverb_native: La vaca harta de la cola haz brigata
proverb_en: The full-fed cow makes company of her tail.

proverb_native: Lavar la cabeça del asno perdimiento de javon
proverb_en: It is a loss of soap to wash the ass’s head.

proverb_native: La verdad, como el olio, siempre anda en somo
proverb_en: Truth, like oil, always comes to the surface.

proverb_native: La vida y el alma, mas no el alvarda
proverb_en: My life and soul (are at your service), but not my pack-saddle.

proverb_native: Lleva tu la arteza, marido, que yo llevaré el cedaço, que pesa como el diablo
proverb_en: Do you carry the trough, husband, and I will carry the sieve, which is as heavy as the devil.

proverb_native: Lo ageno siempre pia por su dueño
proverb_en: What is another’s always pines for its master.

proverb_native: Lo barato es caro
proverb_en: Bargains are dear.

proverb_native: Lo mio mio, lo de Juan mi hermano, suyo y mio
proverb_en: What is mine is my own; my brother Juan’s is his and mine.

proverb_native: Lo peor del pleito es, que de uno nacen ciento
proverb_en: The worst of a lawsuit is that out of one there grow a hundred.

proverb_native: Lo perdido vaya por amor de Dios
proverb_en: Let what is lost go for God’s sake.

proverb_native: Lo que come mi vicino no aproveche a mi tripa
proverb_en: What my neighbour eats does my stomach no good.

proverb_native: Lo que con los ojos veo, con el dedo lo adevino
proverb_en: What I see with my eyes I can guess with my fingers.

proverb_native: Lo que hace el loco á la derreria, hace el sabio á la primeria
proverb_en: What the fool does at last the wise man does at first.

proverb_native: Lo que hecho es, hecho ha de ser por esta vez
proverb_en: What is done, is done for this time.

proverb_native: Lo que la fuerça no puede, ingenio lo vince
proverb_en: What force cannot do ingenuity may.

proverb_native: Lo que la loba hace, al lobo aplace
proverb_en: What the she-wolf does pleases the he-wolf.

proverb_native: Lo que la muger quiere, Dios lo quiere
proverb_en: What a woman wills, God wills.

proverb_native: Lo que mucho se desea, no se cree aunque se vea
proverb_en: What is much desired is not believed when it comes.

proverb_native: Lo que mucho vale, de so tierra sale
proverb_en: What much is worth comes from the earth.

proverb_native: Lo que no acerta en un ano, acerta en un rato
proverb_en: What does not happen in a year may happen in a moment.

proverb_native: Lo que no lleva Christo, lleva el fisco
proverb_en: What Christ does not take the exchequer takes.

proverb_native: Lo que no quieres para ti, no lo quieras para mi
proverb_en: What you dislike for yourself do not like for me.

proverb_native: Lo que saben tres, sabe toda res
proverb_en: What three know, everybody knows.

proverb_native: Lo que se usa, no se escusa
proverb_en: What is in use, wants no excuse.

proverb_native: Lo que te dijeren al oido, no lo digas a tu marido
proverb_en: What is whispered in your ear tell not to your husband.

proverb_native: Los amenazados comen pan
proverb_en: Threatened men eat bread.

proverb_native: Los dichos en nos, los hechos en Dios
proverb_en: Man proposes, God disposes.

proverb_native: Los niños y los locos dicen la verdad
proverb_en: Children and fools speak the truth.

proverb_native: Los pies del hortolano no echan á perder la huerta
proverb_en: The gardener’s feet do no harm to the garden.

proverb_native: Los que cabras no tienen, y cabritos venden, de donde les vienen?
proverb_en: They who don’t keep goats and yet sell kids, where do they get them?

proverb_native: Los yerros del medico, la tierra los cubre
proverb_en: The earth hides as it takes, the physician’s mistakes.

proverb_native: Madre, casar, casar, que carrafico me quiere llevar
proverb_en: Mother, marry me, marry me, or the gull will fly away with me.

proverb_native: Madre, que cosa es casar? Hija, hilar, parir, y llorar
proverb_en: Mother, what is marrying? Spinning, bearing children, and crying, daughter.

proverb_native: Madruga y verás, trabaja y habrás
proverb_en: Rise early and watch, labour and catch.

proverb_native: Maja los ajos, Pedro, mientra yo rallo el queso
proverb_en: Pound the garlic, Pedro, whilst I grate the cheese.

proverb_native: Mal me quieren mis comadres, porque les digo las verdades
proverb_en: My gossips don’t like me because I tell them truths.

proverb_native: Mal se cubre la cabra con el rabo
proverb_en: The goat can’t well cover herself with her tail.

proverb_native: Mal sobre mal, y piedra por cabezal
proverb_en: Ill-luck upon ill-luck, and a stone for a pillow.

proverb_native: Mañana sera otro dia
proverb_en: To-morrow will be another day.

proverb_native: Manda y descuida, no se hará cosa ninguna
proverb_en: Give orders and do no more, and nothing will come of it.

proverb_native: Manda y hazlo, y quitarte has de cuidado
proverb_en: Order and do it, and you will be rid of anxiety.

proverb_native: Manos duchas comen truchas
proverb_en: Skilled hands eat trouts.

proverb_native: Marihuela, fuiste a la boda? No, madre, mas galana estava la novia
proverb_en: Were you at the wedding, Molly? No, mother, but the bride was very fine.

proverb_native: Marido, no veas: muger, ciega seas
proverb_en: Husband, don’t see; wife, be blind.

proverb_native: Mas ablanda el dinero que palabras de caballero
proverb_en: Money soothes more than a gentleman’s words.

proverb_native: Mas apaga buena palabra que caldera de agua
proverb_en: A good word extinguishes more than a pailful of water.

proverb_native: Mas cerca está la camisa que el sayo
proverb_en: The shirt is nearer than the frock.

proverb_native: Mas cerca están mis dientes que mis parientes
proverb_en: My teeth are nearer than my kindred.

proverb_native: Mas descubre un hambriento que cien letrados
proverb_en: A hungry man discovers more than a hundred lawyers.

proverb_native: Mas hace quien quiere que quien puede
proverb_en: He who strives to do, does more than he who has the power.

proverb_native: Mas son los amenazados que los heridos
proverb_en: There are more threatened than hurt.

proverb_native: Mas tira moça que soga
proverb_en: A girl draws more than a rope.

proverb_native: Mas vale cabeza de raton que cola de leon
proverb_en: Better be the head of a rat than the tail of a lion.

proverb_native: Mas vale con mal asno contender que la leña acuestas traer
proverb_en: It is better to strive with a stubborn ass than to carry the wood on one’s back.

proverb_native: Mas vale el mal conocido que el bien por venir
proverb_en: Better suffer a known evil than change for uncertain good.

proverb_native: Mas vale humo de mi casa que fuego de la agena
proverb_en: Better is the smoke of my own house than the fire of another’s.

proverb_native: Mas vale mala avenencia que buena sentencia
proverb_en: A bad compromise is better than a successful suit.

proverb_native: Mas vale pajaro en mano que buitre volando
proverb_en: A sparrow in the hand is better than a bustard on the wing.

proverb_native: Mas vale puñado de natural que almozada de ciencia
proverb_en: A handful of motherwit is worth a bushel of learning.

proverb_native: Mas vale que digan, Aqui huyó, que Aqui murió
proverb_en: Better they should say, “There he ran away,” than “There he died.”

proverb_native: Mas vale que sobre, que no que falte
proverb_en: Better there should be too much than too little.

proverb_native: Mas vale regla que renta
proverb_en: Better is rule than rent.

proverb_native: Mas vale rodear que no ahogar
proverb_en: Better go about than be drowned.

proverb_native: Mas vale ruin asno que ser asno
proverb_en: Better have a bad ass than to be your own ass.

proverb_native: Mas vale salto de mata que ruego de hombres buenos
proverb_en: It is better to leap over the ditch than trust to the pleadings of good men.

proverb_native: Mas vale solo que mal acompañado
proverb_en: Better be alone than in bad company.

proverb_native: Mas vale tarde que nunca
proverb_en: Better late than never.

proverb_native: Mas vale tuerto que ciego
proverb_en: Better one-eyed than stone blind.

proverb_native: Mas vale una abeja que mil moscas
proverb_en: One bee is better than a thousand flies.

proverb_native: Mas vale un toma que dos te dare
proverb_en: Better one “Take this,” than two “I will give you.”

proverb_native: Mas vale vuelta de llave que conciencia de frayle
proverb_en: A turn of the key is better than the conscience of a friar.

proverb_native: Mas val perder que mas perder
proverb_en: It is better to lose than lose more.

proverb_native: Mas ven quatro ojos que dos
proverb_en: Four eyes see more than two.

proverb_native: Mataras y matarte han, y mataran á quien te matare
proverb_en: Kill and thou wilt be killed, and he will be killed who kills thee.

proverb_native: Mejor es dobrar que quebrar
proverb_en: Better to bend than break.

proverb_native: Mejor me parece tu jarro mellado que el mio sano
proverb_en: Your cracked jug seems better to me than my sound one.

proverb_native: Menea la cola el can, no por ti, sino por el pan
proverb_en: The dog wags his tail, not for you but for your bread.

proverb_native: Mete mendigo en tu pajar, y hacer se te ha heredero
proverb_en: Put a beggar into your barn and he will make himself your heir.

proverb_native: Meter aguja, y sacar reja
proverb_en: To put in a needle and take out a bar.

proverb_native: Miedo guarda viña
proverb_en: Fear guards the vineyard.

proverb_native: Mientra en mi casa me estoy, rey me soy
proverb_en: In my own house I am a king.

proverb_native: Mientra la grande se abaja, la chica barre la casa
proverb_en: Whilst the tall wench is stooping, the little one has swept the house.

proverb_native: Miguel, Miguel, no tienes abejas y vendes miel
proverb_en: Miguel, Miguel, you have no bees, and yet sell honey.

proverb_native: Mirais lo que bebo, y no la sed que tengo
proverb_en: You notice what I drink, and not the thirst I feel.

proverb_native: Mira que ates que desates
proverb_en: See that you tie so that you can untie.

proverb_native: Mostrar primero la horca que el lugar
proverb_en: To parade the gallows before the town.

proverb_native: Mucho sabe la zorra, pero mas el que la toma
proverb_en: The fox is knowing, but more knowing he who catches him.

proverb_native: Muchos besan manos que quierian ver cortadas
proverb_en: Many kiss hands they would fain see chopped off.

proverb_native: Mucho tiene que hacer quien ha de gustar a todos
proverb_en: He has much to do who would please everybody.

proverb_native: Muda el lobo los dientes y no los mientes
proverb_en: The wolf changes his teeth but not his disposition.

proverb_native: Muger, no te las cuento, mas doze morcillas hace un puerco
proverb_en: I don’t count them to you, wife, but a hog makes twelve puddings.

proverb_native: Muger, viento, y ventura, presto se muda
proverb_en: Women, wind, and fortune, soon change.

proverb_native: Nace en la huerta lo que no siembra el hortelano
proverb_en: In the garden more grows, than the gardener sows.

proverb_native: Nacenle alas a la hormiga para que se pierde mas aina
proverb_en: The ant gets wings that she may perish the sooner.

proverb_native: Nadar y nadar, y á la orilla ahogar
proverb_en: To swim and swim more, and be drowned on shore.

proverb_native: Nadie seria mesonero sino fuese por el dinero
proverb_en: No one would be an innkeeper but for money.

proverb_native: Necio es, quien piensa que otro no piensa
proverb_en: He is a fool who thinks that another does not think.

proverb_native: Necios y porfiados hacen ricos los letrados
proverb_en: Fools and the perverse fill the lawyers’ purse.

proverb_native: Ni absente sin culpa, ni presente sin disculpa
proverb_en: Absent, none without blame; present, none without excuse.

proverb_native: Ni buen frayle por amigo, ni malo por enemigo
proverb_en: Neither a good friar for friend, nor a bad one for enemy.

proverb_native: Ni con cada mal al fisico, ni con cada pleito al letrado, ni con cada sed al jarro
proverb_en: Go not with every ailment to the doctor, with every plea to the lawyer, or with every thirst to the can.

proverb_native: Ni do ni tomo, como judio en sábado
proverb_en: I neither give nor take, like a Jew on the Sabbath.

proverb_native: Ni el anzuelo, ni la caña, mas el cebo las engaña
proverb_en: It is not the hook or the rod, but the bait that lures.

proverb_native: Ni estoy al vado, ni á la puente
proverb_en: I am neither at the ford nor the bridge.

proverb_native: Ni firmes carta que no leas, ni bebas agua que no veas
proverb_en: Neither sign a paper without reading it, nor drink water without seeing it.

proverb_native: Ni hermosa que mate, ni fea que espante
proverb_en: Neither handsome enough to kill, nor ugly enough to frighten.

proverb_native: Ni judio necio, ni liebre perezosa
proverb_en: No Jew a fool; no hare lazy.

proverb_native: Ni perder derechos, ni llevar cohechos
proverb_en: Lose no rights, and commit no extortions.

proverb_native: Ni rey traidor, ni papa descomulgado
proverb_en: No king was ever a traitor, or pope excommunicated.

proverb_native: Ni sirvas á quien sirvió, ni pidas á quien pidió
proverb_en: Neither serve one who has been a servant, nor beg of one who has been a beggar.

proverb_native: No asamos, y ya empringamos
proverb_en: We are not roasting, and already we are basting.

proverb_native: No asoleges tu mijo á la puerta de tu enemigo
proverb_en: Do not spread your corn to dry at an enemy’s door.

proverb_native: No ay ladron sin encubridor
proverb_en: There is no thief without a receiver.

proverb_native: No compres asno de recuero, ni te cases con hija de mesonero
proverb_en: Do not buy a carrier’s ass, or marry an innkeeper’s daughter.

proverb_native: No creais, marido, lo que veeredes, sino lo que yo os dixeré
proverb_en: Don’t believe what you see, husband, but only what I tell you.

proverb_native: No da quien quiere, sino quien tiene
proverb_en: Not he gives who likes, but who has.

proverb_native: No dé Dios tanto buen á nuestros amigos que nos desconoscan
proverb_en: May God not so prosper our friends that they forget us.

proverb_native: No dice el umbral sino lo que oye al quincial
proverb_en: The threshold says nothing but what it hears of the hinge.

proverb_native: No digais mal del año hasta que sea pasado
proverb_en: Speak not ill of the year until it is past.

proverb_native: No diga la lengua por do pague la cabeza
proverb_en: Let not the tongue utter what the head must pay for.

proverb_native: No diga nadie, de esta agua no beberé
proverb_en: Let no one say, “Of this water I will not drink.”

proverb_native: No digo quien eres, que tu te lo dirás
proverb_en: I do not tell thee what thou art, thou wilt tell it thyself.

proverb_native: No entra en misa la campana, y á todos llama
proverb_en: The bell does not go to mass, yet calls every one to it.

proverb_native: No es aquella gallina buena, que come en tu casa y pone en la agena
proverb_en: It is a bad hen that eats at your house and lays at another’s.

proverb_native: No es de vero lágrimas en la muger, ni coxuear en el perro
proverb_en: A woman’s tears and a dog’s limping are not real.

proverb_native: No es en mano del piloto que dexe el viento su soplo
proverb_en: It is not in the pilot’s power to prevent the wind from blowing.

proverb_native: No es nada, sino que matan a mi marido
proverb_en: It is nothing, they are only thrashing my husband.

proverb_native: No es tan bravo el leon como le pintan
proverb_en: The lion is not so fierce as he is painted.

proverb_native: No falte cibo al palomar, que las palomas ellas se vernán
proverb_en: Let there be no lack of food in the pigeon-house, and the pigeons will come to it.

proverb_native: No falte voluntad, que no faltará lugar
proverb_en: Where there is no want of will, there will be no want of opportunity.

proverb_native: No habria palabra mala, si no fuese mal tomada
proverb_en: There would be no ill word if it were not ill taken.

proverb_native: No hace poco quien su casa quema: espanta los ratones, y escalientase á la leña
proverb_en: He does not a little who burns his house: he frightens the rats, and warms himself.

proverb_native: No hace tanto la zorra en un año como paga en un hora
proverb_en: The fox does not do as much mischief in a year as it pays for in an hour.

proverb_native: No halla agua en la mar
proverb_en: He cannot find water in the sea.

proverb_native: No hay bestia fiera que no se huelgue con su compañera
proverb_en: There is no beast so savage but sports with its mate

proverb_native: No hay casa do no haya su calla! calla!
proverb_en: There is no house without its hush! hush!

proverb_native: No hay cerradura, si es de oro la ganzua
proverb_en: There is no lock, if the pick is of gold

proverb_native: No hay ladron sin encubridor
proverb_en: If there were no receiver there would be no thief

proverb_native: No hay mejor bocado que el hurtado
proverb_en: There is no choicer morsel than that which is stolen

proverb_native: No hay mejor remiendo que el del mismo paño
proverb_en: There is no better patch than one off the same cloth

proverb_native: No hay olla tan fea que no halle su cobertera
proverb_en: There is no pot so ugly but finds its cover

proverb_native: No hay pariente pobre
proverb_en: No relation is poor

proverb_native: No hay peor burla que la verdadera
proverb_en: There is no worse joke than a true one

proverb_native: No hay peor sordo que el que no quiere oir
proverb_en: None so deaf as he that won’t hear

proverb_native: No hay placer que no enfade, y mas si cuesta de valde
proverb_en: There is no pleasure but palls, and the more so if it costs nothing

proverb_native: No hay tal madre como la que pare
proverb_en: There is no mother like the mother that bore us

proverb_native: No hay tal razon como la del baston
proverb_en: There’s no argument like that of the stick

proverb_native: No hay tal testigo como buen moduelo de vino
proverb_en: There is no such witness as a good measure of wine

proverb_native: No hiere Dios con dos manos
proverb_en: God does not smite with both hands

proverb_native: No inventó la polvera
proverb_en: He did not invent gunpowder

proverb_native: No juega Mosé, porque no tiene que
proverb_en: Moses

proverb_native: No lo quiero, no lo quiero, mas échadme lo en la capilla
proverb_en: I don’t want it, I don’t want it, but put it into my hood

proverb_native: No mata la carga sino la sobrecarga
proverb_en: It is not the load but the overload that kills

proverb_native: No me digas oliva hasta que me veas cogida
proverb_en: Call me not olive till you see me gathered

proverb_native: No me llames bien hadada hasta que me veas enterrada
proverb_en: Call me not fortunate till you see me buried

proverb_native: No piden todos para un santo
proverb_en: All do not beg for one saint.

proverb_native: No quiebra delgado, sino gordo y mal hilado
proverb_en: It is not the fine, but the coarse and ill-spun that breaks.

proverb_native: No se acuerda la suegra que fué nuera
proverb_en: The mother-in-law does not remember that she was a daughter-in-law.

proverb_native: No seais hornéra si teneis la cabeza de manteca
proverb_en: Be not a baker if your head is butter.

proverb_native: No se hacen las bodas de hongos á solas
proverb_en: The wedding feast is not made with mushrooms only.

proverb_native: No se hacen tortillas sin romper huevos
proverb_en: You can’t make pancakes without breaking eggs.

proverb_native: No se hizo la miel por la boca del asno
proverb_en: Honey was not made for the mouth of the ass.

proverb_native: No se pierde todo lo que está en peligro
proverb_en: All is not lost that is in danger.

proverb_native: No se queje del engaño quien por la muestra compra el paño
proverb_en: Let him not complain of being cheated who buys cloth by the pattern.

proverb_native: No serás amado si de tí solo tienes cuidado
proverb_en: You will not be loved if you care for none but yourself.

proverb_native: No se toman truchas á bragas enjutas
proverb_en: Trouts are not caught with dry breeches.

proverb_native: Nos ollos de miña sogra vejo en quando o demo a toma
proverb_en: I see by my mother-in-law’s eyes when the devil takes hold of her.

proverb_native: No son palabras para mi tia, que aun de las obras no se fia
proverb_en: Words will not do for my aunt, for she does not put faith even in deeds.

proverb_native: No son soldados todos los que van á la guerra
proverb_en: All are not soldiers who go to the wars.

proverb_native: No te alegres de mi duelo, que quando el mio fuere viejo el tuyo será nuevo
proverb_en: Do not rejoice at my grief, for when mine is old yours will be new.

proverb_native: No te tomes con el ollero, que del burro hace dinero
proverb_en: Don’t scuffle with the potter, for he makes money by the damage.

proverb_native: No vive mas el leal que quanto quiere el traidor
proverb_en: The loyal man lives no longer than the traitor pleases.

proverb_native: Nunca los ausentes se hallaron justos
proverb_en: The absent were never in the right.

proverb_native: Nunca mucho costó poco
proverb_en: Much never cost little.

proverb_native: Nunca pidas á quien tiene, sino á quien sabes que te quiere
