proverb_en: Never ask of him who has, but of him you know wishes you well.

proverb_native: Obra de comun, obra de ningun
proverb_en: What’s everybody’s business is nobody’s business.

proverb_native: Obres son amores, que no buenas razones
proverb_en: Deeds are love, and not fine phrases.

proverb_native: O demo á os suyos quiere
proverb_en: The devil is fond of his own.

proverb_native: Ojos hay que de lagañas se enamoran
proverb_en: There are eyes that fall in love with bleared ones.

proverb_native: Ojos que no ven, corazon que no quiebra
proverb_en: If the eyes don’t see, the heart won’t break.

proverb_native: Olla de muchos mal mejida y peor cocida
proverb_en: The stew mixed by many is ill-seasoned and worse cooked.

proverb_native: Olla que mucho hierve, sabor pierde
proverb_en: The stew that boils much loses favour.

proverb_native: O morirá el asno, ó quien le aguija
proverb_en: Either the ass will die, or he that goads it.

proverb_native: Onza de estado, libra de oro
proverb_en: An ounce of state to a pound of gold.

proverb_native: O rico, o pinjádo
proverb_en: Either rich or hanged.

proverb_native: Oro es lo que oro vale
proverb_en: That is gold which is worth gold.

proverb_native: Oveja harta de su rabo se espanta
proverb_en: The full-fed sheep is frightened at its own tail.

proverb_native: Oveja que bala, bocado pierde
proverb_en: The sheep that bleats loses a mouthful.

proverb_native: Ovejas bobas, por do va una, van todas
proverb_en: Silly sheep, where one goes, all go.

proverb_native: Paga lo que debes, sabrás lo que tienes
proverb_en: If you pay what you owe, what you’re worth you’ll know.

proverb_native: Paga lo que debes, sanarás del mal que tienes
proverb_en: Pay what you owe, and be cured of your complaint.

proverb_native: Pagase el rey de la traicion, mas no de quien la hace
proverb_en: The king likes the treachery, but not the traitor.

proverb_native: Palabra de boca, piedra de honda
proverb_en: A word from the mouth, a stone from a sling.

proverb_native: Palabras azucaradas por mas son amargas
proverb_en: Sugared words generally prove bitter.

proverb_native: Palabra y piedra suelta no tiene vuelta
proverb_en: A word and a stone once launched cannot be recalled.

proverb_native: Palo de ciego, que sáca polvo de debajo de agua
proverb_en: A blind man’s stroke, which raises a dust from beneath water.

proverb_native: Panadera erades antes, aunque ahora traeis guantes
proverb_en: You used to be a baker, though now you wear gloves.

proverb_native: Pan ageno caro cuesta
proverb_en: Another’s bread costs dear.

proverb_native: Papel y tinta, y poca justicia
proverb_en: Paper and ink and little justice.

proverb_native: Para azotar el perro, que se come el hierro
proverb_en: If you want to beat a dog, say he eat your iron.

proverb_native: Para cada jueves no hay un par de orejas
proverb_en: There is not a pair of ears for every Jew.

proverb_native: Para el mal que hoy se acaba no es remedio el de mañana
proverb_en: To-morrow’s remedy will not ward off the evil of today.

proverb_native: Para los desdichados se hizo la horca
proverb_en: The gallows was made for the unlucky.

proverb_native: Pariente a la clara el hijo de mi hermana
proverb_en: My sister’s son is a kinsman beyond dispute.

proverb_native: Parto malo, y hija en cabo
proverb_en: A bad labour, and a daughter after all.

proverb_native: Pasa la fiesta, y el loco resta
proverb_en: The feast passes and the fool remains.

proverb_native: Paz y paciencia, y muerte con penitencia
proverb_en: Peace and patience, and death with penitence.

proverb_native: Pedir sobrado por salir con lo mediano
proverb_en: Ask too much to get enough.

proverb_native: Pedra de ygreja oro goteja
proverb_en: A church stone drops gold.

proverb_native: Pelean los ladrones y descubrense los hurtos
proverb_en: When thieves fall out the thefts come to light.

proverb_native: Penséme santiguar, y quebréme el ojo
proverb_en: I thought to cross myself, and I put out my eye.

proverb_native: Pensé que no tenia marido, y comime la olla
proverb_en: I thought I had no husband, and I eat up the stew.

proverb_native: Pereza, llave de pobreza
proverb_en: Sloth is the key of poverty.

proverb_native: Perro alcucero nunca buen conejero
proverb_en: A kitchen-dog is never a good rabbit-hunter.

proverb_native: Perro ladrador nunca buen mordedor
proverb_en: A barking dog was never a good biter.

proverb_native: Perro lanudo, muerto de hambre, y no creido de ninguno
proverb_en: A shock dog is starved and nobody believes it.

proverb_native: Perro que lobos mata, lobos le matan
proverb_en: The dog that kills wolves, is killed by wolves.

proverb_native: Peso y medida quitan al hombre fatiga
proverb_en: Weight and measure save a man toil.

proverb_native: Pícame Pedro, y yo me lo quiero
proverb_en: Peter pinches me, and I like it.

proverb_native: Piedra movediza nunca moho la cubija
proverb_en: A rolling stone gathers no moss.

proverb_native: Piensa el ladron que todos son de sa condicion
proverb_en: The thief thinks that all men are like himself.

proverb_native: Piensan los enamorados que tienen los otros los ojos quebrados
proverb_en: Lovers think that others have no eyes.

proverb_native: Pierde el mes lo suyo, pero no el año
proverb_en: The month loses its own, but not the year.

proverb_native: Piés que son duchos de andar, no pueden quedos estar
proverb_en: Feet that are used to move cannot remain quiet.

proverb_native: Planta muchas veces traspuesta ni crece ni tredra
proverb_en: A tree often transplanted neither grows nor thrives.

proverb_native: Pobreza no es vileza, ma es ramo de picardia
proverb_en: Poverty is no sin, but it is a branch of roguery.

proverb_native: Poca barba, poca verguenza
proverb_en: Little beard, little modesty.

proverb_native: Poca hiel hace amarga mucha miel
proverb_en: A little gall embitters much honey.

proverb_native: Poco á poco se va léjos
proverb_en: Little by little one goes far.

proverb_native: Poco daño espanta, y mucho amansa
proverb_en: A little loss frightens, a great one tames.

proverb_native: Pollino que me lleve, y no caballo que me arrastre
proverb_en: Give me the ass that carries me in preference to the horse that throws me.

proverb_native: Por amor del bou, llepa lo llop el jou
proverb_en: For love of the ox the wolf licks the yoke.

proverb_native: Por donde fueres, haz como vieres
proverb_en: Wherever you may be, do as you see done.

proverb_native: Por donde menos se piensa salta la liebre
proverb_en: The hare starts from where it is least expected.

proverb_native: Por donde va la mar, vayan las arenas
proverb_en: Where the sea goes let the sands go.

proverb_native: Por do quiera hay su legua de mal camino
proverb_en: Whatever way you take there is a league of bad road.

proverb_native: Por do salta la cabra, salta la que la mama
proverb_en: Where the goat leaps, leaps that which sucks her.

proverb_native: Por el alabado dejé el conocido, y vi me arrepentido
proverb_en: I left what I knew for what I heard praised, and repented.

proverb_native: Por el hilo se saca el ovillo
proverb_en: By the thread we unwind the skein.

proverb_native: Por falta de hombres buenos, á mi padre hicieron alcalde
proverb_en: For the want of worthy men they made my father alcade.

proverb_native: Porfia mata la caza
proverb_en: Perseverance kills the game.

proverb_native: Por la calle de despues se va á la casa de nunca
proverb_en: By the street of “By-and-by” one arrives at the house of “Never.”

proverb_native: Por las haldas del vicario sube el diablo al campanario
proverb_en: The devil gets into the belfry by the vicar’s skirts.

proverb_native: Por mucho madrugar, no amanece mas aina
proverb_en: For all one’s early rising, it dawns none the sooner.

proverb_native: Por mucho que corra la liebre, mas corre el galgo, pues la prende
proverb_en: Fast as the hare runs, the greyhound outruns her, since he catches her.

proverb_native: Por no gastar lo que basta, lo que era excusado se gasta
proverb_en: Through not spending enough, we spend too much.

proverb_native: Por nuevas no peneas, hacerse han viejas, y saber las has
proverb_en: Do not fret for news, it will grow old and you will know it.

proverb_native: Por oir misa, y dar cebada, nunca se perdió jornada
proverb_en: Nothing is lost on a journey by stopping to pray or to feed your horse.

proverb_native: Por ser rey, se quiebra toda ley
proverb_en: Every law is broken to become a king.

proverb_native: Por si o por no, señor marido, ponéos la capilla
proverb_en: Whether it be so or not, husband, put on your hood.

proverb_native: Por sol que haga no dexes tu capa en casa
proverb_en: However bright the sun may shine, leave not your cloak at home.

proverb_native: Por soto no vayas tras otro
proverb_en: In a wood don’t walk behind another.

proverb_native: Por temor, no pierdas honor
proverb_en: Do not lose honour through fear.

proverb_native: Por turbia que esté, no digas, de esta agua no beberé
proverb_en: However foul it be, never say, Of this water I will not drink.

proverb_native: Por un punto se pierde un zapato
proverb_en: For want of a nail the shoe is lost.

proverb_native: Por viejo que sea el barco, pasa una vez el vado
proverb_en: Old as is the boat it may cross the ferry once.

proverb_native: Por vuestra alma vayan esos pater nosters
proverb_en: Let those pater nosters be for your own soul.

proverb_native: Pregonar vino, y vender vinagre
proverb_en: To cry up wine, and sell vinegar.

proverb_native: Prenda que come, ninguno la tome
proverb_en: Let no one take a pawn that eats.

proverb_native: Primero son mis dientes que mis parientes
proverb_en: My teeth before my relations.

proverb_native: Posesion, y buena razon, y lanza en puño
proverb_en: Possession and good right, with lance in hand.

proverb_native: Potros cayendo, y mozos perdiendo, van asesando
proverb_en: Colts by falling, and lads by losing, grow prudent.

proverb_native: Puerco fiado, gruñe todo el año
proverb_en: A pig bought on credit grunts all the year.

proverb_native: Puerco fresco, y vino nuevo, Christianillo al cimenterio
proverb_en: Fresh pork and new wine, send a Christian to the churchyard.

proverb_native: Puerta abierta al santo tienta
proverb_en: An open door tempts a saint.

proverb_native: Puesque la casa se quema, calentémonos todos
proverb_en: Since the house is on fire, let us warm ourselves.

proverb_native: Pues tenemos hogaças, no busquemos tortas
proverb_en: Since we have loaves let us not look for cakes.

proverb_native: Puridad de dos, puridad de Dios; puridad de tres, de todos es
proverb_en: A secret between two is God’s secret, a secret between three is everybody’s.

proverb_native: Puteria ni hurto nunca se encubren mucho
proverb_en: Whoredom and thieving are never long concealed.

proverb_native: Qual el dueño tal el perro
proverb_en: As is the master, so is his dog.

proverb_native: Qual el tiempo, tal el tiento
proverb_en: As are the times, so are the manners.

proverb_native: Qual es el rey, tal es la grey
proverb_en: As is the king, so are his people.

proverb_native: Quando á tu hija le viniere su hado, no aguardes que venga su padre del mercado
proverb_en: When a good offer comes for your daughter, don’t wait till her father returns from market.

proverb_native: Quando ayunque, sufre, quando mazo, tunde
proverb_en: When you are an anvil, bear; when you are a hammer, strike.

proverb_native: Quando comieres pan reciente, no bebas de la fuente
proverb_en: When you eat new bread, don’t drink water.

proverb_native: Quando Dios amanece, para todos amanece
proverb_en: When God gives light he gives it for all.

proverb_native: Quando Dios no quiere, el santo no puede
proverb_en: When God will not the saint cannot.

proverb_native: Quando Dios quiere, con todos vientos llueve
proverb_en: When God pleases it rains with every wind.

proverb_native: Quando Dios quiere en sereno llueve
proverb_en: When God pleases, it rains in fair weather.

proverb_native: Quando el bazo crece el cuerpo enmagrece
proverb_en: When the spleen increases, the body diminishes.

proverb_native: Quando el cosario promete misas y cera, con mal anda la galera
proverb_en: When the corsair promises masses and candles, it goes ill with the galley.

proverb_native: Quando el diablo reza, engañarte quiere
proverb_en: When the devil says his prayers he wants to cheat you.

proverb_native: Quando el Español canta, ó rabia, ó no tiene blanca
proverb_en: When the Spaniard sings, he is either mad or has no money.

proverb_native: Quando el guardian juega á los naypes, qué haran los frayles?
proverb_en: When the prior plays cards, what will the monks do?

proverb_native: Quando el hierro está encendido, entonces ha de ser batido
proverb_en: When the iron is hot, then is the time to strike.

proverb_native: Quando el necio es acordado, el mercado es ya pasado
proverb_en: When the fool has made up his mind the market is over.

proverb_native: Quando el rio no hace ruido, ó no lleva agua, ó va muy crecido
proverb_en: When the river makes no noise, it is either dried up or much swollen.

proverb_native: Quando el viejo no puede beber la huesa le pueden hacer
proverb_en: When an old man cannot drink, prepare his grave.

proverb_native: Quando el villano está en el mulo, ni conoce á Dios, ni al mundo
proverb_en: Set a peasant on horseback, and he forgets both God and man.

proverb_native: Quando el villano está rico, no tiene pariente, ni amigo
proverb_en: When a peasant gets rich, he knows neither relations nor friends.

proverb_native: Quando en verano es invierno, y en invierno verano, nunca buen año
proverb_en: When the summer is winter, and the winter summer, it is a sorry year.

proverb_native: Quando fueres á casa, agena llama defuera
proverb_en: When you go to a strange house knock at the door.

proverb_native: Quando fueres por camino, no digas mal de tu enemigo
proverb_en: When you are on the road speak not ill of your enemy.

proverb_native: Quando la criatura dienta la muerte la tienta
proverb_en: When the child cuts its teeth, death is on the watch.

proverb_native: Quando la mala ventura se duerme, nadie la despierte
proverb_en: When ill-luck sleeps, let no one wake her.

proverb_native: Quando llueve en Agosto, llueve miel y mosto
proverb_en: When it rains in August, it rains honey and wine.

proverb_native: Quando llueve en Febrero, todo el año es tempero
proverb_en: When it rains in February, it will be temperate all the year.

proverb_native: Quando no dan los campos, no han los santos
proverb_en: When the fields yield not, the saints have not.

proverb_native: Quando os pedimos, Dueña os decimos; quando os tenemos, como queremos
proverb_en: When we ask a favour, we say, Madam; when we obtain it, what we please.

proverb_native: Quando pobre, franco; quando rico, avaro
proverb_en: When poor, liberal; when rich, stingy.

proverb_native: Quando te dieren el anillo, pon el dedillo
proverb_en: When they offer you a ring, hold out your finger.

proverb_native: Quando te dieren la vaquilla, acude con la soguilla
proverb_en: When they give you the calf, be ready with the halter.

proverb_native: Quando todos te dijeren que eres asno, rebuzna
proverb_en: When every one says you are an ass, bray.

proverb_native: Quando una puerta se cierra, ciento se abren
proverb_en: When one door shuts, a hundred open.

proverb_native: Quando un lobo come á otro, no hay que comer en el soto
proverb_en: When one wolf eats another, there is nothing to eat in the wood.

proverb_native: Quando uno ne quiere, dos no barajan
proverb_en: Two cannot fall out if one does not choose.

proverb_native: Quando vieras tu casa quemar, llegate á escalentar
proverb_en: When thou seest thy house in flames, go warm thyself by it.

proverb_native: Quando zuga el abeja miel torna, y quando el araña ponzoña
proverb_en: When the bee sucks, it makes honey, when the spider, poison.

proverb_native: Quan léjos de ojo, tan léjos de corazon
proverb_en: Out of sight, out of mind.

proverb_native: Quanto sabes no dirás, quanto vés no juzgarás, si quieres vivir en paz
proverb_en: Tell not all you know, nor judge of all you see, if you would live in peace.

proverb_native: Quatro cosas sacan al hombre de tino, la muger, el tabaco, naypes y vino
proverb_en: Four things put a man beside himself—women, tobacco, cards, and wine.

proverb_native: Quebrarse un ojo para sacar à otro los dos
proverb_en: To lose one eye that you may deprive another of two.

proverb_native: Quebrasteme la cabeza, y ahora me untas el casco
proverb_en: You have broken my head and now you bring plaister.

proverb_native: Quebreme el pie, quizá por bien
proverb_en: I broke my leg, perhaps for my good.

proverb_native: Quem jugata co ferro, jugata co demo
proverb_en: He who plays with a sword plays with the devil.

proverb_native: Quem mal quer os seus, no querrá ben os alleus
proverb_en: He that is unkind to his own will not be kind to others.

proverb_native: Queso de ovejas, leche de cabras, manteca de vacas
proverb_en: Cheese from the ewe, milk from the goat, butter from the cow.

proverb_native: Qui barat, el cap se grat
proverb_en: He who hunts after bargains will scratch his head.

proverb_native: Qui de tot es moll, de tot es foll
proverb_en: Who is tender in everything is a fool in everything.

proverb_native: Quien abrojos siembra espinas coje
proverb_en: He who sows brambles reaps thorns.

proverb_native: Quien acecha por agujero, ve su duelo
proverb_en: He who peeps through a hole will discover his dole.

proverb_native: Quien adelante no mira, atras se queda
proverb_en: He who does not look before lags behind.

proverb_native: Quien á dos señores ha de servir, al uno ha de mentir
proverb_en: He who has two masters to serve must lie to one of them.

proverb_native: Quien al cielo escupe, en la cara le cae
proverb_en: He who spits above himself will have it fall on his face.

proverb_native: Quien á los veinte no entiende, á treinta no sabe y á quarenta no tiene, ruin vejez le espera
proverb_en: He who at twenty understands nothing, at thirty knows nothing, and at forty has nothing, will lead a wretched old age.

proverb_native: Quien amaga y no da, miedo ha
proverb_en: He who threatens to strike, and does not, is afraid.

proverb_native: Quien a mano agena espera, mal yanta y peor cena
proverb_en: He who lives in hopes, breakfasts ill and sups worse.

proverb_native: Quien á muchos amos sirve á alguno ha de hacer falta
proverb_en: He who serves many masters must neglect some of them.

proverb_native: Quien anda al reves, anda al camino dos veces
proverb_en: He who takes the wrong road must make his journey twice over.

proverb_native: Quien á su enemigo popa, á sus manos muere
proverb_en: He who makes light of his enemy dies by his hand.

proverb_native: Quien á su muger no honra, á si mismo deshonra
proverb_en: He who does not honour his wife, dishonours himself.

proverb_native: Quien á su perro quiere matar, rabia le ha de levantar
proverb_en: He who wants to kill his dog has only to say he is mad.

proverb_native: Quien á treinta no asesa, no comprará dehesa
proverb_en: He who at thirty has no brains, will never purchase an estate.

proverb_native: Quien a veinte no es galan, ni á treinta tiene fuerza, ni á quarenta riqueza, ni á cincuenta esperiencia, ni será galan, ni fuerte, ni rico, ni prudente
proverb_en: He that is not gallant at twenty, strong at thirty, rich at forty, or experienced at fifty, will never be gallant, strong, rich, or prudent.

proverb_native: Quien bien ama, tarde olvida
proverb_en: He who loves well is slow to forget.

proverb_native: Quien bien ata, bien desata
proverb_en: He that ties well, unties well. (Safe bind, safe find.)

proverb_native: Quien bien bayla, de boda en boda se anda
proverb_en: He who dances well goes from wedding to wedding.

proverb_native: Quien bien quiere á Beltran, bien quiere á su can
proverb_en: He who loves Bertrand loves his dog. (Love me, love my dog).

proverb_native: Quien bien quiere á Pedro, no hace mal á su perro
proverb_en: He who loves Peter won’t harm his dog.

proverb_native: Quien bien quiere, bien obedece
proverb_en: He who loves well, obeys well.

proverb_native: Quien bien quiere, de léjos ve
proverb_en: A well-wisher sees from afar.

proverb_native: Quien bien siembra, bien coge
proverb_en: He who sows well, reaps well.

proverb_native: Quien bien te hará, ó se te muere, ó se te va
proverb_en: He who does good to you either dies or goes away.

proverb_native: Quien bueyes ha perdido, cencerros se le antojan
proverb_en: He who has lost his oxen is always hearing bells.

proverb_native: Quien busca halla
proverb_en: He who seeks, finds.

proverb_native: Quien calla, otorga
proverb_en: Silence gives consent.

proverb_native: Quien calla piedras apaña
proverb_en: He who is silent gains store.

proverb_native: Quien canta, sus males espanta
proverb_en: Who sings, drives away care.

proverb_native: Quien come la carne que roa el hueso
proverb_en: He who eats the meat let him pick the bone.

proverb_native: Quien come y condensa, dos veces pone la mesa
proverb_en: He who eats and puts by, has sufficient for two meals.

proverb_native: Quien come y dexa, dos veces pone la mesa
proverb_en: A penny spared is a penny saved.

proverb_native: Quien compra cavallo, compra cuidado
proverb_en: He who buys a horse buys care.

proverb_native: Quien compra y vende lo que gasta no siente
proverb_en: He who buys and sells does not feel what he spends.

proverb_native: Quien con el viejo burló, primero rió y después lloro
proverb_en: He who made fun of the old man, laughed at first and cried afterwards.

proverb_native: Quien con lobos anda, á aullar se enseña
proverb_en: He who goes with wolves learns to howl.

proverb_native: Quien con perros se echa, con pulgas se levanta
proverb_en: He who lies down with dogs gets up with fleas.

proverb_native: Quien con ropa agena se viste, en la calle se queda en cuerpo
proverb_en: Who arrays himself in other men’s garments is stripped on the highway.

proverb_native: Quien con tosco ha de entender, mucho seso ha menester
proverb_en: He who has to deal with a blockhead has need of much brains.

proverb_native: Quien da lo suyo ántes de su muerte, que le den con un mazo en la frente
proverb_en: Who gives what he has before he is dead, take a mallet and knock that fool on the head.

proverb_native: Quien da presto, da dos veces
proverb_en: He gives twice who gives in a trice.

proverb_native: Quien de ageno se viste, en la calle le desnudan
proverb_en: He who dresses in others’ clothes will be undressed on the highway.

proverb_native: Quien del alacran está picado, la sombra le espanta
proverb_en: He who has been stung by the scorpion is frightened at its shadow.

proverb_native: Quien de locura enfermó, tarde sanó
proverb_en: Whoever falls sick of folly, is long in getting cured.

proverb_native: Quien desalaba la cosa ese la compra
proverb_en: He who finds fault wants to buy.

proverb_native: Quien desparte lleva la peor parte
proverb_en: He who divides gets the worst share.

proverb_native: Quien de todos es amigo, ó es muy pobre, ó es muy rico
proverb_en: He who is everybody’s friend is either very poor or very rich.

proverb_native: Quien dice lo que quiere, oye lo que no quiere
proverb_en: He who says what he likes, hears what he don’t like.

proverb_native: Quien dice lo suyo, mal callará lo ageno
proverb_en: He who tells his own secret will hardly keep another’s.

proverb_native: Quien dineros y pan tiene, consuegra con quien quiere
proverb_en: He who has both money and bread, may choose with whom his daughter to wed.

proverb_native: Quien echa agua en la garrafa de golpe, mas derrama que ella coje
proverb_en: He who pours water hastily into a bottle spills more than goes in.

proverb_native: Quien el aceyte mesura, las manos se unta
proverb_en: He who measures oil greases his hands.

proverb_native: Quien en la plaza á labrar se mete, muchos adestradores tiene
proverb_en: He who works on the highway will have many advisers.

proverb_native: Quien en un año quiere ser rico, al medio le ahorcan
proverb_en: He who wants to be rich in a year comes to the gallows in half a year.

proverb_native: Quien en una piedra dos veces tropieza, no es maravilla se quiebre la cabeza
proverb_en: No wonder if he breaks his head who stumbles twice over one stone.

proverb_native: Quien escucha, su mal oye
proverb_en: Listeners hear no good of themselves.

proverb_native: Quien esta en su tienda, no le achacan que se halló en la contienda
proverb_en: He that minds his business at home, will not be accused of taking part in the fray.

proverb_native: Quien estropieza y no cae, en su paso añade
proverb_en: He who stumbles and does not fall mends his pace.

proverb_native: Quién es tu enemigo? Hombre de tu oficio
proverb_en: Who is your enemy? A man of your own trade.

proverb_native: Quién es tu enemigo? El de tu oficio
proverb_en: Two of a trade can never agree.

proverb_native: Quien feo ama, hermoso le parece
proverb_en: She who loves an ugly man thinks him handsome.

proverb_native: Quien fia ó promete, en deuda se mete
proverb_en: He who pledges or promises runs in debt.

proverb_native: Quien guarda halla
proverb_en: He who saves, finds.

proverb_native: Quien guarda su poridad escusa mucho mal
proverb_en: He who keeps his own secret avoids much mischief.

proverb_native: Quien hace lo que quiere, no hace lo que debe
proverb_en: He who does what he likes, does not what he ought.

proverb_native: Quien hace por comun, hace por ningun
proverb_en: He who gives to the public, gives to no one.

proverb_native: Quien hace un cesto, hará ciento
proverb_en: He that makes one basket can make a hundred.

proverb_native: Quien ha criados, ha enemigos no escusados
proverb_en: He who has servants has unavoidable enemies.

proverb_native: Quien ha de echar el cascabel al gato?
proverb_en: Who is to bell the cat?

proverb_native: Quien ha de llevar el gato al agua?
proverb_en: Who is to carry the cat to the water?

proverb_native: Quien la fama ha perdido, muerto anda en la vida
proverb_en: He who has lost his reputation is a dead man among the living.

proverb_native: Quien la miel menea, siempre se le pega de ella
proverb_en: He that stirs honey will have some of it stick to him.

proverb_native: Quien la raposa ha de engañar, cumplele madrugar
proverb_en: He who would cheat the fox must rise early.

proverb_native: Quien las cosas mucho apura, no tiene vida segura
proverb_en: Who is always prying into other men’s affairs, leads a dangerous life.

proverb_native: Quien las sabe, las tañe
proverb_en: Let him play the instrument who knows how.

proverb_native: Quien la vaca del rey come flaca, gorda la paga
proverb_en: He who eats the king’s cow lean, pays for it fat.

proverb_native: Quien lazo me armó, en él cayó
proverb_en: He who laid a snare for me has fallen into it.

proverb_native: Quien lejos va a casar, ó va engañado ó va á engañar
proverb_en: He who goes far from home to marry, goes either to deceive or be deceived.

proverb_native: Quien lengua ha, á Roma va
proverb_en: He who has a tongue goes to Rome.

proverb_native: Quien lleva las obladas que taña las campanas
proverb_en: He who receives the offerings let him ring the bells.

proverb_native: Quien madre tiene en villa, siete veces se amortaza cada dia
proverb_en: The servant wench that has a mother in town swoons seven times a day.

proverb_native: Quien mala cama hace, en ella se yace
proverb_en: As you make your bed so you must lie in it.

proverb_native: Quien mal anda en mal acaba
proverb_en: He who begins badly, ends badly.

proverb_native: Quien malas hadas no halla, de las buenas se enhada
proverb_en: He that has no ill luck grows weary of good luck.

proverb_native: Quien mal casa, tarde enviuda
proverb_en: He who marries ill, is long in becoming widowed.

proverb_native: Quien mas corre, menos vuela
proverb_en: The more haste the less speed.

proverb_native: Quien mas sabe mas calla
proverb_en: Who knows most says least.

proverb_native: Quien mas tiene, mas quiere
proverb_en: The more one has the more one wants.

proverb_native: Quien ménos procura, alcanza mas bien
proverb_en: He who asks the fewest favours is the best received.

proverb_native: Quien mucho abarca, poco aprieta
proverb_en: He who grasps at much holds fast little.

proverb_native: Quien mucho duerme, poco aprende
proverb_en: He who sleeps much, learns little.

proverb_native: Quien mucho habla, en algo acierta
proverb_en: He who talks much is sometimes right.

proverb_native: Quien mucho habla, mucho yerra
proverb_en: Who talks much, errs much.

proverb_native: Quien no adoba gotera, adoba casa entera
proverb_en: He who does not repair his gutter has a whole house to repair.

proverb_native: Quien no alza un alfiler, no tiene en nada á su muger
proverb_en: He who does not pick up a pin cares nothing for his wife.

proverb_native: Quien no aprieta en vallejo, no aprieta en consejo
proverb_en: He who has no voice in the valley, will have none in the council.

proverb_native: Quien no castiga culito, no castiga culazo
proverb_en: He who does not whip the child does not mend the youth.

proverb_native: Quien no está enseñado á bragas, las costuras le hacen llagas
proverb_en: When a man is not used to breeches the seams gall him.

proverb_native: Quien no hace mas que otro, no vale mas que otro
proverb_en: He who does no more than another is no better than another.

proverb_native: Quien no miente, no viene de buena gente
proverb_en: He that does not lie, does not come of good blood.

proverb_native: Quien no parece, perece
proverb_en: He who does not show himself, is overlooked.

proverb_native: Quien no se aventura, no ha ventura
proverb_en: Who ventures nothing has no luck.

proverb_native: Quien no te conoce te compre
proverb_en: Let him who does not know you buy you.

proverb_native: Quien no tiene mas de un sayo no puede prestarlo
proverb_en: He who has but one coat cannot lend it.

proverb_native: Quien no va á carava, no sabe nada
proverb_en: He who does not mix with the crowd knows nothing.

proverb_native: Quien ó A quien Dios no le dió hijos, el diablo le dió sobrinos
proverb_en: He to whom God gives no sons, the devil gives nephews.

proverb_native: Quien ó A quien no habla, no le oye Dios
proverb_en: He who does not speak, God does not hear.

proverb_native: Quien ó A quien pone los ojos en el suelo, no fies tu dinero
proverb_en: He who looks demurely trust not with your money.

proverb_native: Quien padre tiene alcalde, seguro va á juicio
proverb_en: He goes safely to trial whose father is a judge.

proverb_native: Quien peces quiere, mojarse tiene
proverb_en: He who wants to catch fish must not mind a wetting.

proverb_native: Quien pesca un pez, pescador es
proverb_en: He who catches one fish is a fisherman.

proverb_native: Quien poco sabe, presto lo reza
proverb_en: He who knows little soon tells it.

proverb_native: Quien presta, no cobra; y si cobra, no todo; y si todo, no tal; y si tal, enemigo mortal
proverb_en: Who lends recovers not; or if he recovers, recovers not all; or if all, not such; or if such, a mortal enemy.

proverb_native: Quien primero viene, primero muele
proverb_en: He who comes first grinds first.

proverb_native: Quien promete, en deuda se mete
proverb_en: He who promises incurs a debt.

proverb_native: Quien quando puede no quiere, quando quiere no puede
proverb_en: He that will not when he can, cannot when he will.

proverb_native: Quien quiere medrar, iglesia, ó mar, ó casa real
proverb_en: He who would thrive must follow the church, the sea, or the king’s service.

proverb_native: Quien quiere tomar, conviénele dar
proverb_en: He who would take must give.

proverb_native: Quien quiere vivir sano, la ropa de invierno traiga en verano
proverb_en: He that would be healthy must wear his winter clothes in summer.

proverb_native: Quien quisiere muger hermosa, el sabado la escoja
proverb_en: He that would have a beautiful wife should choose her on a Saturday.

proverb_native: Quien quisiere mula sin tacha, ándese á pie
proverb_en: He who wants a mule without fault must walk on foot.

proverb_native: Quien quisiere vivir sano, coma poco y cene temprano
proverb_en: He that would be healthy, must eat temperately, and sup early.

proverb_native: Quien quita la ocasion, quita el pecado
proverb_en: He who avoids the temptation avoids the sin.

proverb_native: Quien ramo pone, su vino quiere vender
proverb_en: He who hangs out a branch wants to sell his wine.

proverb_native: Quien se fia de amigo no fiel, buen testigo tiene contra el
proverb_en: He that trusts a faithless friend, has a good witness against him.

proverb_native: Quien se guarda, Dios le guarda
proverb_en: God helps him who helps himself.

proverb_native: Quien se muda, Dios le ayuda
proverb_en: He who reforms, God assists.

proverb_native: Quien siembra abrojos, no ande descalzo
proverb_en: He who sows brambles must not go barefoot.

proverb_native: Quien siempre me miente, nunca me engaña
proverb_en: He who always tells me a lie never cheats me.

proverb_native: Quien sirve al commun, sirve á ningun
proverb_en: He who helps everybody, helps nobody.

proverb_native: Quien sirve no es libre
proverb_en: He who serves is not free.

proverb_native: Quien solo come su gallo, solo ensille su caballo
proverb_en: He that eats his fowl alone may saddle his horse alone.

proverb_native: Quien su carro unta, sus bueyes ayuda
proverb_en: He who greases his cart-wheels helps his oxen.

proverb_native: Quien te cubre te descubre
proverb_en: That which covers thee discovers thee.

proverb_native: Quien te da el capon, dale la pierna y el alon
proverb_en: To him who gives the capon you may spare a leg and wing.

proverb_native: Quien te hace fiesta que no te suele hacer, ó te quiere engañar, ó te ha menester
proverb_en: He that is more civil than usual, either wants to cozen you or has need of you.

proverb_native: Quien tiempo tiene y tiempo atiende, tiempo viene que se arrepiente
proverb_en: Who has time yet waits for time, comes to a time of repentance.

proverb_native: Quien tiene arte, va por toda parte
proverb_en: He who has a trade may travel through the world.

proverb_native: Quien tiene boca, no diga á otro, Sopla
proverb_en: Let him that has a mouth not say to another, Blow.

proverb_native: Quien tiene enemigos no duerma
proverb_en: He who has enemies, let him not sleep.

proverb_native: Quien tiene hijas para casar, tome vedijas para hilar
proverb_en: He who has daughters to marry, let him give them silk to spin.

proverb_native: Quien tiene quatro, y gasta cinco no ha menester bolsico
proverb_en: He who has got four and spends five, has no occasion for a purse.

proverb_native: Quien tiene tejado de vidrio, no tire piedras al de su vecino
proverb_en: He who has a glass roof should not throw stones at his neighbour’s.

proverb_native: Quien tiene una hora de espacio, no muere ahorcado
proverb_en: He that has an hour’s start will not be hanged.

proverb_native: Quien todo lo niega, todo lo confiesa
proverb_en: He who denies everything confesses everything.

proverb_native: Quien todo lo quiere, todo lo pierde
proverb_en: He who grasps all loses all.

proverb_native: Quien tras otro cabalga, no ensilla quando quiere
proverb_en: He who rides behind another does not saddle when he will.

proverb_native: Quien tuviere hijo varon, no llame á otro ladron
proverb_en: He who has a son grown up should not call another a thief.

proverb_native: Quien una vez hurta, fiel nunca
proverb_en: He who steals once is never trusty.

proverb_native: Quiéralo Dios, Matea, que este hijo nuestro sea
proverb_en: God grant, dear wife, that this son be ours.

proverb_native: Quieres buen mercado? Con el necio necesitado
proverb_en: Do you want to buy cheap? Buy of a needy fool.

proverb_native: Quieres hacer del ladron fiel? Fiate de el
proverb_en: If you would make a thief honest, trust him.

proverb_native: Quieres que te siga el can? Dale pan
proverb_en: If you would have the dog follow you, give him bread.

proverb_native: Quieres ver loba parida? Casa la hija
proverb_en: Do you want to see a wolf with young

proverb_native: Qui escudella daltri espera, freda la menja
proverb_en: He who waits for another’s platter has a cold meal.

proverb_native: Quitáron me el espejo por fea, y dieronlo á la ciega
proverb_en: They took away the mirror from me because I was ugly, and gave it to the blind woman.

proverb_native: Raposa que mucho tarda, caça aguarda
proverb_en: The fox that tarries long is on the watch for prey.

proverb_native: Raton que no sabe mas de un horado, presto le toma el gato
proverb_en: The rat that knows but one hole is soon caught by the cat.

proverb_native: Recebido ya el daño, atapar el horado
proverb_en: To stop the hole after the mischief is done.

proverb_native: Reniego de cuentas, con deudos y deudas
proverb_en: Curses on accounts with relations.

proverb_native: Reniego del amigo, que cubre con las alas y muerde con el pico
proverb_en: Avoid a friend who covers you with his wings and destroys you with his beak.

proverb_native: Resfriadas duelen mas las llagas
proverb_en: Wounds pain most when grown cool.

proverb_native: Rifaban los rocines del vidriero, y él mirando qual daba mejor coz al compañero
proverb_en: The glass-dealer’s horses fell out, and he looked on to see which kicked hardest.

proverb_native: Riñen las comadres y dicense las verdades
proverb_en: The gossips fall out and tell each other truths.

proverb_native: Rogar al santo hasta pasar del trance
proverb_en: To pray to the saint until the danger is past.

proverb_native: Ruego de grande fuerza es que te hace
proverb_en: A great man’s entreaty is a command.

proverb_native: Ruegos porque cante, y ruegos porque calle
proverb_en: Entreaties to get him to sing, and entreaties to leave off.

proverb_native: Ruego y derecho hacen el hecho
proverb_en: Entreaty and right do the deed.

proverb_native: Sabedlo, coles, que espinacas hay en la olla
proverb_en: Know, cabbages, that there is spinach in the stew.

proverb_native: Saberlo como su Paternoster
proverb_en: He knows it as well as his Lord’s Prayer.

proverb_native: Saca lo tuyo al mercado, y uno te dirá prieto y otro blanco
proverb_en: Tell your affairs in the market-place, and one will call them black and another white.

proverb_native: Sacar el ascua con mano agena
proverb_en: To take out a burning coal with another’s hand.

proverb_native: Sacar el pie del lodo
proverb_en: To draw the foot out of the mire.

proverb_native: Sacarlo de entre los cardos, sacároslo hemos de entre las manos
proverb_en: Pluck it from among the thistles, and we will take it off your hands.

proverb_native: Sacar un fuego con otro fuego
proverb_en: To quench fire with fire.

proverb_native: Sacar un pie del lodo, y meter otro
proverb_en: To take one foot out of the mire and put the other into it.

proverb_native: Sacar verdad por decir mentira
proverb_en: To discover truth by telling a falsehood.

proverb_native: Sacristan de amen
proverb_en: An amen clerk.

proverb_native: Salamon pasó por su puerta quando nació, mas no entró dentro
proverb_en: When he was born, Solomon passed by his door, and would not go in.

proverb_native: Salga pez, ó salga rana, á la capacha. C
proverb_en: ome fish, come frog, all goes into the basket.

proverb_native: Salir de un lodo y entrar en otro
proverb_en: To get out of one muck into another.

proverb_native: Salir del lodo, y caer en el arroyo
proverb_en: Out of the mire and into the brook.

proverb_native: Saltar de la sarten, y dar en las brasas
proverb_en: To jump out of the frying-pan and fall into the fire.

proverb_native: Salud y alegria belleza cria; atavio y afeito cuesta dinero y miente
proverb_en: Health and cheerfulness make beauty; finery and cosmetics cost money and lie.

proverb_native: Sal vertida, nunca bien cogida
proverb_en: Salt spilt is never all gathered.

proverb_native: Sanan cuchilladas, mas no malas palabras
proverb_en: Wounds from the knife are healed, but not those from the tongue

proverb_native: Sanan llagas, y no malas palabras
proverb_en: Wounds heal, but not ill words.

proverb_native: Sangrarle y purgarle; si se muriere, enterrarle
proverb_en: Bleed him and purge him; if he dies, bury him.

proverb_native: Sea mi enemigo, y vaya á mi molino
proverb_en: Be my enemy and go to my mill.

proverb_native: Sease velado, y sease un palo
proverb_en: Let it be a husband, though it be but a log.

proverb_native: Seco y no de hambre mas recio es que alambre
proverb_en: A man that is lean, not from hunger, is harder than brass.

proverb_native: Señal mortal no quierer sanar
proverb_en: Not to wish to recover is a mortal symptom.

proverb_native: Ser alguno un caxon de sastre
proverb_en: To be like a tailor’s pattern-book.

proverb_native: Ser como el escudero de Guadalaxara, que de lo que dice de noche, no hay nada á la mañana
proverb_en: To be like the esquire of Guadalaxara, who knew nothing in the morning of what he said at night.

proverb_native: Ser como el puerro, tener la cabeza blanca, y lo demas verde
proverb_en: To be like a leek, have a grey head and the rest green.

proverb_native: Ser como piojo en costura
proverb_en: To be like a louse in a seam.

proverb_native: Ser como unas ortigas
proverb_en: To be like a bunch of nettles.

proverb_native: Ser el sastre del Campillo, que cosia de valde, y ponia el hilo
proverb_en: To be like the tailor of Campillo, who worked for nothing, and found thread.

proverb_native: Ser mercader mas va en el cobrar, que en el vender
proverb_en: To be a merchant, the art consists more in getting paid than in making sales.

proverb_native: Ser toda hoja sin fruto
proverb_en: All leaf and no fruit.

proverb_native: Si bien me quieres, Juan, tus obras me lo diran
proverb_en: If you love me, John, your acts will tell me so.

proverb_native: Si da el cántaro en la piedra, ó la piedra en el cántaro, mal para el cántaro
proverb_en: Whether the pitcher strike the stone, or the stone the pitcher, woe be to the pitcher.

proverb_native: Si de alguno te quieres vengar, has de callar
proverb_en: If you want to be revenged, hold your tongue.

proverb_native: Si el cielo se cae, pararle las manos
proverb_en: If the sky falls, hold up your hands.

proverb_native: Si el cielo se cae, quebrarse han las ollas
proverb_en: If the sky falls there will be pots broken.

proverb_native: Si el niño lloráre, acallelo su madre, y si no quisiere callar, déxelo llorar
proverb_en: If the child cries let the mother hush it, and if it will not be hushed let it cry.

proverb_native: Siembra trigo en barrial, y pón viña en cascajal
proverb_en: Sow corn in clay, and plant vines in sand.

proverb_native: Siéntate en tu lugar, no te harán levantar
proverb_en: Seat yourself in your place and you will not be made to quit it.

proverb_native: Si esta pella á la pared no pega, á lo menos dexará señal
proverb_en: If this ball does not stick to the wall it will at least leave a mark.

proverb_native: Siete es convite, y nueve es convicio
proverb_en: Seven is company, and nine confusion.

proverb_native: Siete hermanos en un consejo, de lo tuerto hacen derecho
proverb_en: Seven brothers in a council make wrong right.

proverb_native: Si la locura fuese dolores, en cada casa darian voces
proverb_en: If folly were a pain, there would be groaning in every house.

proverb_native: Si la piedra da en el cántaro, mal para el cántaro; y si el cántaro da en la piedra, mal para el cántaro
proverb_en: If the pitcher knocks against a stone, woe to the pitcher; and if the stone knocks against the pitcher, woe to the pitcher.

proverb_native: Si lo cena, no lo almuerza
proverb_en: If you eat it up at supper, you cannot have it at breakfast.

proverb_native: Si no va el otero á Mahoma, vaya Mahoma al otero
proverb_en: If the mountain will not go to Mahomet, Mahomet must go to the mountain.

proverb_native: Si quieres buena fama, no te dé el sol en la cama
proverb_en: If you would acquire fame, let not the sun shine on you in bed.

proverb_native: Si quieres dar de palos á tu muger, pídele al sol á beber
proverb_en: If you want to thrash your wife, ask her for a drink of water in the sun.

proverb_native: Si quieres enfermar, lavate la cabeza y vete á echar
proverb_en: If you want to be dead, wash your head and go to bed.

proverb_native: Si quieres ser bien servido, sírvete a tú mismo
proverb_en: If you wish to be well served, serve yourself.

proverb_native: Si quieres ver quanto vale un ducado, búscalo prestado
proverb_en: If you want to know what a ducat is worth, try to borrow one.

proverb_native: Sirve á señor, y sabrás que es dolor
proverb_en: Serve a lord and you’ll know what is grief.

proverb_native: Si secretos quieres saber, buscalos en el pesar ó en el placer
proverb_en: If you want to know secrets, seek for them in trouble or in pleasure.

proverb_native: Si se perdieron los anillos, aquí quedáron los dedillos
proverb_en: If the rings are lost, here are the fingers still.

proverb_native: Si soy bobo, meteme el dedo en la boca
proverb_en: If I am a fool, put your finger in my mouth.

proverb_native: Si tienes médico amigo, quitale la gorra y envialo á casa de tu enemigo
proverb_en: If you have a friend who is a doctor, make your bow and send him to the house of your enemy.

proverb_native: Si uno dos y tres te dicen que eres asno, ponte un rabo
proverb_en: If one, two, three say you are an ass, put on a tail.

proverb_native: Sobre brevas vino bebas
proverb_en: Drink wine upon figs.

proverb_native: Sobre gusto no ha disputa
proverb_en: There is no disputing about taste.

proverb_native: Sobre peras vino bebas, y sea tanto que naden ellas
proverb_en: After stuffing pears within, drink old wine until they swim.

proverb_native: Sobre un huevo pone la gallina
proverb_en: The hen lays upon an egg.

proverb_native: So el sayal, hay al
proverb_en: Under the sackcloth there is something hid.

proverb_native: Soltero, pavon; desposado, leon; casado, asno
proverb_en: Bachelor, a peacock; betrothed, a lion; married, an ass.

proverb_native: So mi manto al rey mando
proverb_en: Under my cloak I command the king.

proverb_native: Sopla, herrero, ganarás dinero
proverb_en: Blow, smith, and you’ll get money.

proverb_native: Soplar y sorber no puede junto ser
proverb_en: One cannot blow and swallow at the same time.

proverb_native: So vayna de oro cuchillo de plomo
proverb_en: Under a gold sheath a leaden knife.

proverb_native: Tan grande es el yerro como el que yerra
proverb_en: The fault is as great as he that commits it.

proverb_native: Tantas veces va el cántaro á la fuente, que dexa el asa ó la frente
proverb_en: The pitcher goes so often to the well, that it leaves its handle or its mouth.

proverb_native: Tanto es Pedro de Dios, que no le medra Dios
proverb_en: Peter is so godly that God does not improve his condition.

proverb_native: Tanto quiere el diablo á su hijo que le quiebra el ojo
proverb_en: The devil is so fond of his son that he put out his eye.

proverb_native: Tener á alguno en ascuas
proverb_en: To keep one upon hot coals.

proverb_native: Tener el pie en dos zapatos
proverb_en: To have the foot in two shoes.

proverb_native: Tener el seso en los calcañares
proverb_en: To have one’s brains in one’s heels.

proverb_native: Tener la barriga á la boca
proverb_en: To have the belly up to one’s mouth.

proverb_native: Tener pelos en el corazon
proverb_en: To have hairs on his heart.

proverb_native: Tirar coces contra el aguijon
proverb_en: To kick against the pricks.

proverb_native: Tirar la piedra y esconder la mano
proverb_en: To throw the stone and conceal the hand.

proverb_native: Todo camino vá á Roma
proverb_en: Every road leads to Rome.

proverb_native: Todo es nada lo de este mundo, si no se endereza al segundo
proverb_en: All things of this world are nothing, unless they have reference to the next.

proverb_native: Todo saldrá en la colada
proverb_en: It will all come out in the soapsuds.

proverb_native: Todos son buenos, y mi capa no parece
proverb_en: They are all honest men, but my cloak is not to be found.

proverb_native: Tomar la ocasion por los cabellos
proverb_en: To take opportunity by the forelock.

proverb_native: Tomar las calzas de Villadiego
proverb_en: To take Villadiego’s boots.

proverb_native: Tonto, sin saber latin, nunca es gran tonto
proverb_en: A fool, unless he know Latin, is never a great fool.

proverb_native: Trabajar para el obispo
proverb_en: To work for the bishop.

proverb_native: Traerlo escrito en la frente
proverb_en: To have it written on his forehead.

proverb_native: Tragarse un camello, y no poder pasar un mosquito
proverb_en: To swallow a camel, and strain at a gnat.

proverb_native: Tramontana no tiene trigo, y el hombre pobre no tiene amigo
proverb_en: A north wind has no corn, and a poor man no friend.

proverb_native: Traspasa el rico las leyes, y es castigado el pobre
proverb_en: The rich man transgresses the law, and the poor man is punished.

proverb_native: Tras el vicio viene el fornicio
proverb_en: After one vice a greater follows.

proverb_native: Tras los dias viene el seso
proverb_en: Sense comes with age.

proverb_native: Tras pared ni tras seto, no digas en secreto
proverb_en: Do not tell your secrets behind a wall or a hedge.

proverb_native: Trasquilenme en la plaza, y no lo sepan en mi casa
proverb_en: They may whip me in the market-place, so it be not known at home.

proverb_native: Tres cosas matan al hombre, soles, cenas, y penas
proverb_en: Three things kill a man: a scorching sun, suppers, and cares.

proverb_native: Tres hijas y una madre, quatro diablos para el padre
proverb_en: Three daughters and their mother, four devils for the father.

proverb_native: Tripa llena, ni bien huye ni bien pelea
proverb_en: A full belly is neither good for flight, nor for fighting.

proverb_native: Tripas llevan corazon, que no corazon tripas
proverb_en: The bowels support the heart, and not the heart the bowels.

proverb_native: Triste es la casa, donde la gallina canta, y el gallo calla
proverb_en: It goes ill in the house where the hen sings and the cock is silent.

proverb_native: Tu dinero mudo, no lo descubras á ninguno
proverb_en: Discover not your silent money

proverb_native: Una cautela con otra se quiebra
proverb_en: One knavery is met by another.

proverb_native: Una cosa piensa el vayo, y otra el que lo ensilla
proverb_en: The horse thinks one thing, and his rider another.

proverb_native: Una en el clavo y ciento en la herradura
proverb_en: One stroke on the nail and a hundred on the horseshoe.

proverb_native: Un agravio consentido, otro venido
proverb_en: One grievance borne, another follows.

proverb_native: Una golondrina no hace verano
proverb_en: One swallow does not make a summer.

proverb_native: Una mano lava la otra, y ambas la cara
proverb_en: One hand washes the other, and both the face.

proverb_native: Un amor saca otro
proverb_en: One love drives out another.

proverb_native: Un asno entre muchas monas, cocanle todas
proverb_en: One ass among many monkeys is grinned at by all.

proverb_native: Un cabello hace sombra en el suelo
proverb_en: A hair casts its shadow on the ground.

proverb_native: Un lobo no muerde á otro
proverb_en: One wolf does not bite another.

proverb_native: Un loco hace ciento
proverb_en: One fool makes a hundred.

proverb_native: Uno levanta la caza, y otro la mata
proverb_en: One starts the game and another bags it.

proverb_native: Uno ojo á la sarten y otro á la gata
proverb_en: One eye on the frying-pan and the other on the cat.

proverb_native: Unos tienen la fama, y otros cardan la lana
proverb_en: Some have the fame, and others card the wool.

proverb_native: Un puerco encenagado procura encenagar á otro
proverb_en: A bespattered hog tries to bespatter another.

proverb_native: Un ruin mientras mas lo ruegan mas se estiende
proverb_en: The more you court a clown the statelier he grows.

proverb_native: Un solo golpe no derriba á un roble
proverb_en: An oak is not felled at one blow.

proverb_native: Uso hace maestro
proverb_en: Practice makes perfect.

proverb_native: Vallestero que mal tira, presto tiene la mentira
proverb_en: The archer that shoots badly has a lie ready.

proverb_native: Van a misa los zapateros, ruegan a Dios que mueran carneros
proverb_en: Shoemakers go to mass and pray that sheep may die.

proverb_native: Vanse los gatos, y estiendense los ratos
proverb_en: When the cat’s away the rats will play.

proverb_native: Vaso malo nunca cae de mano
proverb_en: A crazy vessel never falls from the hand.

proverb_native: Vaya con Dios, que un pan me lleva
proverb_en: Go in God’s name, for he takes a loaf of mine.

proverb_native: Vende público y compra secreto
proverb_en: Sell publicly and buy privately.

proverb_native: Vender gato por liebre
proverb_en: To sell a cat for a hare.

proverb_native: Vender miel al colmenero
proverb_en: To sell honey to the beekeeper.

proverb_native: Ventura te dé Dios, hijo, que saber poco te basta
proverb_en: God give you luck, my son, for little wit must serve your turn.

proverb_native: Ver el cielo por un embudo
proverb_en: To see the sky through a funnel.

proverb_native: Ver, oir y callar
proverb_en: See, hear, and hold your tongue.

proverb_native: Verse alguno en calzas prietas
proverb_en: To find oneself in tight breeches.

proverb_native: Viejo como la sarna
proverb_en: As old as the itch.

proverb_native: Viene ventura á quien la procura
proverb_en: Luck comes to those who look after it.

proverb_native: Viento y ventura poco dura
proverb_en: Wind and good luck are seldom lasting.

proverb_native: Vino acedo, y tocino añejo, y pan de centeno, sostienen la casa en peso
proverb_en: Sour wine, old bacon, and rye bread keep a house rich.

proverb_native: Vino que es bueno no ha menester pregonero
proverb_en: Good wine needs no crier.

proverb_native: Viuda lozana, ó casada, ó sepultada, ó emparedada
proverb_en: A buxom widow must be married, buried, or cloistered.

proverb_native: Viva quien vence
proverb_en: Long life to the conqueror.

proverb_native: Vos doña, yo doña, quien botara á porca fora
proverb_en: You a lady, I a lady, who is to put the sow out of doors?

proverb_native: Voz del pueblo es voz de Dios
proverb_en: The voice of the people is the voice of God.

proverb_native: Xabonar cabeza de asno, perdimiento de xabon
proverb_en: To lather an ass’s head is only wasting soap.

proverb_native: Yerba mala no le empece la helada
proverb_en: Ill weeds are not hurt by the frost.

proverb_native: Yo como tú y tú como yo, el diablo te me dió
proverb_en: I am like you and you like me, the devil united us.

proverb_native: Yo dueña y vos doncella, quien barrerá la casa?
proverb_en: I mistress and you miss, who is to sweep the house?

proverb_native: Yo duro y vos duro, quien llevará lo maduro
proverb_en: I stubborn and you stubborn, who is to carry the load?

proverb_native: Yo molondron, tu molondrona, cásate conmigo, Antonia
proverb_en: I a lazy lout, you a lazy lout, marry me, Antonia.

proverb_native: Yo sé que me sé, mas de esto callar me he
proverb_en: I know what I know, but will say nothing about it.

proverb_native: Zapatero, á tu zapato
proverb_en: Shoemaker, stick to your last.

proverb_native: Zorro en zorrera el humo lo echa fuera
proverb_en: When a fox is in his hole, the smoke fetches him out.

proverb_native: Zurrar á uno la badana
proverb_en: To thrash one’s jacket.

proverb_native: A adem, a mulher, e a cabra, he má cousa sendo magra
proverb_en: A goose, a woman, and a goat, are bad things lean.

proverb_native: A agoa o dá, a agoa o leva
proverb_en: What water gives, water takes away.

proverb_native: A agoa tudo lava
proverb_en: Water washes everything.

proverb_native: A amigo naõ encubras teu segredo, que darás causa a perdelo
proverb_en: Conceal not your secret from your friend, or you deserve to lose him.

proverb_native: A apressada pergunta, vagarosa resposta
proverb_en: To a hasty question a leisurely answer.

proverb_native: Abaixaõ-se as cadeiras, levantaõ-se as tripeças
proverb_en: Chairs sink and stools rise.

proverb_native: Abaixaõ-se os muros, levantaõ-se os monturos
proverb_en: Walls sink and dunghills rise.

proverb_native: A besta comedeira pedras, na cevadeira
proverb_en: For a voracious beast pebbles in his feed.

proverb_native: A besta que muito anda, nunca falta quem tanja
proverb_en: The beast that goes well never wants a rider to try its paces.

proverb_native: A boca naõ admitte fiador
proverb_en: The belly does not accept bail.

proverb_native: A boi velho naõ cates abrigo
proverb_en: You need not find a shelter for an old ox.

proverb_native: Abraçou-se o asno com a ameixieira, e acharaõ-se parentes
proverb_en: The ass embraced the thistle, and they found themselves relations.

proverb_native: Abre tua bolsa, abrirei a minha boca
proverb_en: Open your purse, and I will open my mouth.

proverb_native: A cabra de minha visinha, mais leite dá que a minha
proverb_en: My neighbour’s goat gives more milk than mine.

proverb_native: A caõ mordido todos o mordem
proverb_en: All bite the bitten dog.

proverb_native: A carne de lobo dente de caõ
proverb_en: To wolf’s flesh dog’s teeth.

proverb_native: A casa do amigo rico irás sendo requerido, e a casa do necessitado sem ser chamado
proverb_en: Go to your rich friend’s house when invited; to your poor friend’s without invitation.

proverb_native: Accommodar o pé ao sapato, e naõ o sapato ao pé
proverb_en: Fit the foot to the shoe, not the shoe to the foot.

proverb_native: Acenai ao discreto, dai-o por feito
proverb_en: Give a hint to the man of sense, and consider the thing done.

proverb_native: Achaques ao odre, que sabe ao pez
proverb_en: The wine-skin has its reasons for smelling of pitch.

proverb_native: A chave na cinta faz a mim boa, e á minha visinha
proverb_en: The key at the girdle keeps me good and my neighbour too.

proverb_native: Achou o cego hum dinheiro
proverb_en: The blind man has picked up a coin.

proverb_native: Acompanha com os bons, e séras hum delles

proverb_native: A dor de cabeça minha, e as vaccas nossas

proverb_native: A espada e o annel, segundo a maõ em que estiver

proverb_native: A gente pobre moeda miuda

proverb_native: Agoa molle em pedra dura, tanto dá, até que fura

proverb_native: Agoa salobra na terra secca he doce
proverb_en: Brackish water is sweet in a drought.

proverb_en: The lucky man has a daughter for his firstborn.

proverb_native: Ainda que a garça voe alta, o falcaõ a mata

proverb_native: Alcança quem naõ cança
proverb_en: Who faints not, achieves.

proverb_native: Alfaiate mal vestido, sapateiro mal calçado

proverb_native: Alchimia he provada, ter renda, e naõ gastar nada
proverb_en: It is approved alchemy to have an income and spend nothing.

proverb_native: A lingua longa he sinal de maõ curta
proverb_en: A long tongue betokens a short hand.

proverb_native: A má lingua, tesoura
proverb_en: For a bad tongue scissors.

proverb_native: A máo bácoro, boa lande
proverb_en: To the lean pig a fat acorn.

proverb_native: Amar, e saber naõ póde ser
proverb_en: To love and be wise is incompatible.

proverb_native: Ama-se a traiçaõ, aborrece-se o traidor
proverb_en: The treason approved, the traitor abhorred.

proverb_native: A má visinha dá a agulha sem linha
proverb_en: The bad neighbour gives a needle without thread.

proverb_native: Ameaça muitos, quem affronta hum
proverb_en: He threatens many who affronts one.

proverb_native: Amigo de bom tempo, muda-se com o vento
proverb_en: A fair-weather friend changes with the wind.

proverb_native: Amigo de todos, e de nenhum, tudo he hum
proverb_en: Everybody’s friend or nobody’s friend, is all one.

proverb_native: Amigo quebrado soldará, mas naõ sarará
proverb_en: Broken friendship may be soldered but can never be made sound.

proverb_native: Amigos e mulas fallecem a duras
proverb_en: Friends and mules fail us at hard passes.

proverb_native: A mingoa de paõ, boas saõ tortas

proverb_native: A molher, e a gallinha por andar se perde asinha

proverb_native: A molher, e a ovelha com cedo a cortelha
proverb_en: Your wife and sheep early at home.

proverb_native: A molher, e o vidro, sempre estaõ em perigo
proverb_en: Women and glass are always in danger.

proverb_native: Amor, e senhoria, naõ quer companhia
proverb_en: Love and lordship like not fellowship.

proverb_native: Amor louco, eu por ti, e tu por outro
proverb_en: Mad love, I for you and you for another.

proverb_native: Amor naõ tem lei

proverb_native: A mórtos, e a idos, naõ ha amigos

proverb_native: A mouro morto, graõ lançada

proverb_en: Much caution does no harm.

proverb_native: A muita cera queima a igreja

proverb_en: Too much familiarity breeds contempt.

proverb_en: By going gains the mill, and not by standing still.

proverb_en: Let me go warm and folk may laugh.

proverb_native: Antes a lãa se perca, que a ovelha

proverb_native: Antes com bons a furtar, que com máos a orar

proverb_en: Foot firm till death.

proverb_native: Azeite, vinho e amigo, o mais antigo
proverb_en: Of oil, wine, and friends, the oldest.

proverb_native: Barba remolhada, meia rapada

proverb_native: Barriga quente, pé dormente

proverb_en: Under a shabby cloak may be a smart drinker.

proverb_native: Debaixo do sahal, ha al

proverb_native: De bons propositos está o inferno cheio

proverb_native: De casa do gato, naõ vai o rato farto
proverb_en: The rat does not leave the cat’s house with a bellyful.

proverb_native: De casta lhe vem ao galgo ter o rabo longo
proverb_en: It is the nature of the greyhound to carry a long tail.

proverb_native: Dedo de espada, e palmo de lança, he grã vantagem
proverb_en: A finger’s length in a sword, and a palm in a lance, are a great advantage.

proverb_native: De ferreiro a ferreiro naõ passa dinheiro
proverb_en: Between smith and smith no money passes.

proverb_native: De grande rio, grande peixe
proverb_en: From great rivers come great fish.

proverb_native: Deita-te sem cea, amanhecerás sem divida

proverb_native: Deita-te tarde, levanta-te cedo, veras teu mal, e o alheio

proverb_native: Deixar fazer a Deos, que he santo velho
proverb_en: He is an old saint, and may leave it in the hands of God.

proverb_native: De máo ninho naõ cries passarinho
proverb_en: Do not rear a bird of a bad breed.

proverb_native: De noite todos os gatos saõ pardos
proverb_en: At night all cats are grey.

proverb_native: Deos ajuda aos que trabalhaõ
proverb_en: God helps those that help themselves.

proverb_native: Deos consente, mas naõ sempre
proverb_en: God permits, but not for ever.

proverb_native: Deos he o que sara, e o mestre leva a prata
proverb_en: God heals, and the doctor gets the money.

proverb_native: Deos me dé contenda con quem me entenda
proverb_en: God grant me to dispute with those who understand me.

proverb_native: De pai santo, filho diabo
proverb_en: The father a saint, the son a devil.

proverb_native: Depois de rapar, naõ he que tosquiar
proverb_en: After shaving there’s nothing to shear.

proverb_native: Depois de vindimas, cavanejos
proverb_en: Baskets after the vintage.

proverb_native: De porta cerrada, o diabo se torna
proverb_en: From a closed door the devil turns away.

proverb_native: De rabo de porco, nunca bom virote
proverb_en: You can’t make a good shaft of a pig’s tail.

proverb_native: De ruim a ruim, quem acommette vence
proverb_en: Of two cowards, the one who attacks conquers the other.

proverb_native: Despreza teu inimigo, serás logo vencido
proverb_en: Despise your enemy and you will soon be beaten.

proverb_native: Dinheiro emprestaste, inimigo ganhaste
proverb_en: Money lent, an enemy made.

proverb_native: Dinheiro faz batalha, e naõ braço largo
proverb_en: Money wins the battle, not the long arm.

proverb_native: Dinheiro he a medida de todas as cousas
proverb_en: Money is the measure of all things.

proverb_native: Dize ao amigo teu segredo, e por-te-ha o pé no pescoço

proverb_native: Dizei-lhe que he formosa, e tornar-se-ha douda

proverb_native: Dize-me com quem andas, dirte-hei que manhas has

proverb_native: Dizem os filhos ao soalheiro, o que ouvem dizer ao fumeiro
proverb_en: Children tell in the highway what they hear by the fireside.

proverb_native: Do bom, bom penhor, e do máo, nenhum penhor nem fiador
proverb_en: Of the good man a good pledge, and of the bad neither pledge nor surety.

proverb_native: Doce he a guerra, para quem naõ andou nella
proverb_en: War is sweet to him who does not go to it.

proverb_native: Do contado come o lobo
proverb_en: The wolf eats of what is counted.

proverb_native: Do fogo te guardarás, e do máo homem naõ poderás
proverb_en: You may keep yourself safe from fire, but not from a bad man.

proverb_native: Do mal o menos
proverb_en: Of evils, choose the least.

proverb_native: Donde fogo naõ ha, fumo naõ se levanta
proverb_en: Where there’s no fire there’s no smoke.

proverb_native: Donde foste pagem, naõ serás escudeiro
proverb_en: Where you were a page, be not an esquire.

proverb_native: Donde tiraõ, e naõ põem, cedo chegaõ ao fundo
proverb_en: Taking out without putting in, soon comes to the bottom.

proverb_native: Donde vas, mal? Onde ha mais mal
proverb_en: Whither goest thou, Misfortune? To where there is more.

proverb_native: Dôr de mulher morta, dura até a porta
proverb_en: Grief for a dead wife lasts to the door.

proverb_native: Do soldado que naõ tem capa, guarda a tua na arca
proverb_en: From the soldier who has no cloak, keep your own in your chest.

proverb_native: Duas aves de rapina naõ se guardaõ companhia
proverb_en: Birds of prey do not flock together.

proverb_native: Duro com duro naõ faz bom muro
proverb_en: Hard upon hard does not make a good wall.

proverb_native: Egoa cançada prado acha
proverb_en: The tired mare goes willingly to grass.

proverb_native: Elle tem cabeça, pois tambem hum alfinete a tem
proverb_en: He has a head, and so has a pin.

proverb_native: Em boca cerrada, naõ entra mosca
proverb_en: No flies get into a shut mouth.

proverb_native: Em bons dias, boas obras
proverb_en: The better day the better deed.

proverb_native: Em casa do ladraõ, naõ lembrar baraço
proverb_en: Never mention a rope in the house of a thief.

proverb_native: Em quanto ha vida, ha esperança
proverb_en: While there is life there is hope.

proverb_native: Em quanto a grande se abaixa, a pequena varre a casa
proverb_en: Whilst the tall maid is stooping, the little one sweeps the house.

proverb_native: Engana-me no preço, e naõ no que mereço
proverb_en: Cheat me in price, and not in the goods.

proverb_native: Ensaboar a cabeça do asno, perda do sabão
proverb_en: To lather an ass’s head is a waste of soap.

proverb_native: Entrar por hum ouvido, e sahir pello outro
proverb_en: In at one ear and out at the other.

proverb_native: Erva má, naõ lhe empece a geada
proverb_en: Ill weeds are not hurt by frost.

proverb_native: Estar como o peixe n’agoa
proverb_en: To be like a fish in the water.

proverb_native: Este hé meu amigo, que móe no meu moinho
proverb_en: He is my friend who grinds at my mill.

proverb_native: Fallai no lobo ver-lhe-heis a pelle
proverb_en: Talk of the wolf and behold his skin.

proverb_native: Falla pouco, e bem, ter-te-haõ por alguem
proverb_en: Speak little and well, they will think you somebody.

proverb_native: Fallar, fallar, naõ enche barriga
proverb_en: Fine words don’t fill the belly.

proverb_native: Fardel de pedinte nunca he cheio
proverb_en: A beggar’s wallet is never full.

proverb_en: Make your son your heir and not your steward.

proverb_native: Faze boa farinha, e naõ toques bosina
proverb_en: Make good flour and you need no trumpet.

proverb_native: Faze bem, naõ cates a quem
proverb_en: Do good and care not to whom.

proverb_native: Faze da noite, noite, e do dia, dia; viveras com alegria

proverb_native: Fazei-vos mel, comer-vos-haõ as moscas

proverb_native: Faze mal, e espera outro tal
proverb_en: Do ill, and expect the like.

proverb_native: Fazenda herdada he menos estimada
proverb_en: An estate inherited is the less valued.

proverb_native: Fazer conta sem a hospeda
proverb_en: To reckon without one’s hostess.

proverb_native: Fazer de huma pulga hum cavalleiro armado
proverb_en: To make of a flea a knight cap-a-pie.

proverb_native: Faze-te morto, deixar-te-ha o touro
proverb_en: Feign death and the bull will leave you.

proverb_native: Feita a lei, cuidada a malicia
proverb_en: The law devised, its evasion contrived.

proverb_native: Ferro, que naõ se usa, enche-se de ferrugem
proverb_en: Iron that is not used soon rusts.

proverb_native: Fiandeira, fiai manso, que me estorvais, que estou rezando
proverb_en: Spinner, spin softly, you disturb me; I am praying.

proverb_native: Filhos casados, cuidados dobrados
proverb_en: Children married, cares increased.

proverb_native: Fome, e frio mette a pessoa com seu inimigo
proverb_en: Hunger and cold surrender a man to his enemy.

proverb_native: Fugir do fumo, e cahir no fogo

proverb_native: Fugir do lodo, e cahir no arroio
proverb_en: To get out of the mire and fall into the river.

proverb_native: Fui para me benzer, e quebrei hum olho
proverb_en: I meant to cross myself and put out one of my eyes.

proverb_native: Furtar o carneiro, e dar os pés pelo amor de Deos
proverb_en: To steal a sheep and give away the trotters for God’s sake.

proverb_native: Galgo, compra-lo, e naõ cria-lo

proverb_native: Galgo que muitas lebres levanta, nenhuma mata
proverb_en: The greyhound that starts many hares kills none.

proverb_native: Gallinha, que em casa fica, sempre pica
proverb_en: The hen that stays at home picks up the crumbs.

proverb_native: Gallo bom nunca foi gordo
proverb_en: A good cock was never fat.

proverb_native: Gato escaldado da agoa fria há medo
proverb_en: A scalded cat dreads cold water.

proverb_native: Graõ a graõ enche a gallinha o papo
proverb_en: Grain by grain the hen fills her crop.

proverb_en: He avoided the fly and swallowed the spider.

proverb_en: In war, hunting, and love, for one pleasure a hundred pains.

proverb_en: There are ills that happen for good.

proverb_native: Hir-se-haõ os hospedes, comeremos o pato
proverb_en: The guests will go away, and we will eat the pasty.

proverb_native: Homem apaixonado, naõ admitte conselho
proverb_en: An angry man heeds no counsel.

proverb_native: Homem apercebido, meio combatido
proverb_en: He who is well prepared has half won the battle.

proverb_native: Homem atrevido, odre de bom vinho, e vaso de vidro pouco duraõ
proverb_en: A rash man, a skin of good wine, and a glass vessel, do not last long.

proverb_native: Homem de bem, tem palavra, como Rei
proverb_en: An honest man’s word is as good as the king’s.

proverb_native: Homem de palha val mais, que mulher de ouro
proverb_en: A man of straw is better than a woman of gold.

proverb_native: Homem de teu officio, teu inimigo
proverb_en: The man of your own trade is your enemy.

proverb_native: Homem farto, naõ he comedor
proverb_en: A full man is no eater.

proverb_native: Homem morto, naõ falla
proverb_en: A dead man does not speak.

proverb_native: Homem nescio, dá ás vezes bom conselho
proverb_en: Fools sometimes give wise men counsel.

proverb_native: Homem pobre, depois de comer ha fome
proverb_en: A poor man is hungry after eating.

proverb_native: Homem põe, e Deos dispõe
proverb_en: Man proposes, and God disposes.

proverb_native: Honra ao bom, para que te honre, e ao máo, para que te naõ deshonre
proverb_en: Honour a good man that he may honour you, and a bad man that he may not dishonour you.

proverb_native: Honra e proveito naõ cabem em hum sacco
proverb_en: Honour and profit will not keep in one sack.

proverb_native: Hospeda formosa damno faz á bolsa
proverb_en: A handsome hostess is bad for the purse.

proverb_native: Huma desgraça alcança outra
proverb_en: One misfortune brings on another.

proverb_native: Hum aggravo consentido, outro vindo
proverb_en: One wrong submitted to, another follows.

proverb_native: Huma maõ lava a outra, e ambas o rosto
proverb_en: One hand washes the other, and both the face.

proverb_native: Hum doudo fará cento
proverb_en: One fool makes a hundred.

proverb_native: Hum graõ naõ enche o celleiro, mas ajuda a seu companheiro

proverb_native: Ira de irmaõs, ira de diabos
proverb_en: The wrath of brothers is the wrath of devils.

proverb_native: Ladre-me o caõ, naõ me morda
proverb_en: Let the dog bark at me, so he don’t bite me.

proverb_en: The tongue goes to the aching tooth.

proverb_native: Lá vaõ leis, onde querem cruzados
proverb_en: Laws go where dollars please.

proverb_native: Levantar a lebre, para que outrem medre
proverb_en: To start the hare for another’s profit.

proverb_native: Levar agoa ao mar
proverb_en: To carry water to the sea.

proverb_native: Lobo faminto naõ tem assento
proverb_en: A hungry wolf is not at rest.

proverb_native: Longe da vista, longe do coraçaõ
proverb_en: Out of sight, out of mind.

proverb_native: Madruga, e verás; trabalha, e terás
proverb_en: Rise early, and you will observe; labour, and you will have.

proverb_native: Mãi aguçosa, filha preguiçosa
proverb_en: A bustling mother makes a slothful daughter.

proverb_native: Mãi, casai-me logo, que se me arruga o rosto

proverb_native: Mãi, que cousa he casar? Filha, fiar, parir, e chorar

proverb_native: Mais abranda o dinheiro, que palavra de cavalleiro
proverb_en: Money soothes more than the words of a cavalier.

proverb_native: Mais apaga boa palavra, que caldeira de agua
proverb_en: A good word quenches more than a cauldron of water.

proverb_native: Mais asinha se toma hum mentiroso, que hum coxo
proverb_en: A liar is sooner caught than a cripple.

proverb_native: Mais barato he o comprado, que o pedido
proverb_en: What is bought is cheaper than a gift.

proverb_native: Mais descobre huma hora de jogo, que hum anno de conversaçaõ
proverb_en: An hour of play discovers more than a year of conversation.

proverb_native: Mais faz quem quer, que quem pode
proverb_en: He that will, does more than he that can.

proverb_native: Mais quero asno que me leve, que cavallo que me derrube
proverb_en: Better an ass that carries me than a horse that throws me.

proverb_native: Mais quero para meus dentes, que para meus parentes
proverb_en: I want more for my teeth than for my relations.

proverb_native: Mais val arrodear, que afogar
proverb_en: Better go round than be drowned.

proverb_native: Mais val ás vezes favor, que justiça, nem razaõ
proverb_en: Favour oft avails more than justice or reason.

proverb_native: Mais val bem de longe, que mal de perto
proverb_en: Better a distant good than a near evil.

proverb_native: Mais val boa regra, que boa renda
proverb_en: Good management is better than good income.

proverb_native: Mais val callar, que mal fallar

proverb_native: Mais valem amigos na praça, que dinheiro na arca
proverb_en: Better have friends in the market-place than money in your coffer.

proverb_native: Mais val ganhar no lodo, que perder no ouro
proverb_en: Better gain in mud than lose in gold.

proverb_native: Mais val guardar, que pedir

proverb_native: Mais val hum passaro na maõ, que dous que vaõ voando
proverb_en: One bird in the hand is worth two flying.

proverb_native: Mais val hum toma, que dous te darei

proverb_native: Mais val merecer honra, e naõ a ter, que tendo-a, naõ a merecer
proverb_en: Better deserve honour and not have it, than have it and not deserve it.

proverb_native: Mais val perder, que mais perder
proverb_en: Better lose than lose more.

proverb_native: Mais val ruim asno, que ser asno

proverb_native: Mais val salto de mata, que rogos de homens bons
proverb_en: Better is a leap over the ditch than the entreaties of good men.

proverb_native: Mais val só, que mal accompanhado
proverb_en: Better alone than in bad company.

proverb_native: Mais val tarde, que nunca
proverb_en: Better late than never.

proverb_native: Mais vém dous olhos, que hum
proverb_en: Two eyes see more than one.

proverb_native: Mais vém quatro olhos, que dous
proverb_en: Four eyes see more than two.

proverb_en: Another’s misfortune does not cure my pain.

proverb_native: Mal haja o ventre, que do paõ comido se esquece
proverb_en: Ill befal the belly that forgets eaten bread.

proverb_native: Malhar no ferro em quanto está quente
proverb_en: Strike while the iron is hot.

proverb_en: My gossips don’t like me because I tell them truths.
